text.DRB: Mosabab also and Jemlech, and Josa the son of Amasias,

text.NRSV: Joel, Jehu son of Joshibiah son of Seraiah son of Asiel,
text.Rotherham: and Joel,—and Jehu, the son of Joshibiah, the son of Seraiah, the son of Asiel:
text.KJV: And Joel, and Jehu the son of Josibiah, the son of Seraiah, the son of Asiel,
text.NABRE: Joel, Jehu, son of Joshibiah, son of Seraiah, son of Asiel,
text.DRB: and Joel, & Jehu the son of Josabia, the son of Saraia, the son of Asiel.

text.NRSV: Elioenai, Jaakobah, Jeshohaiah, Asaiah, Adiel, Jesimiel, Benaiah,
text.Rotherham: and Elioenai, and Jaakobah, and Jeshohaiah, and Asaiah, and Adiel, and Jesimiel, and Benaiah:
text.KJV: And Elioenai, and Jaakobah, and Jeshohaiah, and Asaiah, and Adiel, and Jesimiel, and Benaiah,
text.NABRE: Elioenai, Jaakobah, Jeshohaiah, Asaiah, Adiel, Jesimiel, Benaiah,
text.DRB: and Elioenai, & Jacoba, & Isuhaia, & Asaia, & Adiel, and Ismiel, & Banaia,

text.NRSV: Ziza son of Shiphi son of Allon son of Jedaiah son of Shimri son of Shemaiah—
text.Rotherham: and Ziza, son of Shiphi, son of Allon, son of Jedaiah, son of Shimri, son of Shemaiah.
text.KJV: And Ziza the son of Shiphi, the son of Allon, the son of Jedaiah, the son of Shimri, the son of Shemaiah;
text.NABRE: Ziza, son of Shiphi, son of Allon, son of Jedaiah, son of Shimri, son of Shemaiah—
text.DRB: Ziza also the son of Zephei the son of Allon the son of Adaia, the son of Semri the son of Samaia.

text.NRSV: these mentioned by name were princes in their families, and their ancestral houses increased greatly.
text.Rotherham: These, introduced by their names, were leading men in their families. And, their ancestral house, brake forth exceedingly;
text.KJV: These mentioned by their names were princes in their families: and the house of their fathers increased greatly.
text.NABRE: these just named were princes in their clans, and their ancestral houses spread out to such an extent
text.DRB: These renowned Princes in their kindreds, and in the house of their affinities they were multiplied exceedingly.

text.NRSV: They journeyed to the entrance of Gedor, to the east side of the valley, to seek pasture for their flocks,
text.Rotherham: so they went to the entering in of Gerar, unto the east of the valley,—to seek pasture for their flocks;
text.KJV: And they went to the entrance of Gedor, even unto the east side of the valley, to seek pasture for their flocks.
text.NABRE: that they went to the approaches of Gedor, east of the valley, seeking pasture for their flocks.
text.DRB: And they went forth to enter into Gador as far as the East side of the valley, and to seek pastures for their flocks.

text.NRSV: where they found rich, good pasture, and the land was very broad, quiet, and peaceful; for the former inhabitants there belonged to Ham.
text.Rotherham: and they found pasture, fat and good, and, the land, was broad on both hands, and quiet, and secure,—for, of Ham, were the dwellers there aforetime.
text.KJV: And they found fat pasture and good, and the land was wide, and quiet, and peaceable; for they of Ham had dwelt there of old.
text.NABRE: They found abundant and good pastures, and the land was spacious, quiet, and peaceful—for the Hamites dwelt there formerly.
text.DRB: And they found fat pastures, and very good, and a country very large and quiet and fruitful, in the which before had dwelt the stock of Cham.

text.NRSV: These, registered by name, came in the days of King Hezekiah of Judah, and attacked their tents and the Meunites who were found there, and exterminated them to this day, and settled in their place, because there was pasture there for their flocks.
text.Rotherham: But these written by name came in the days of Hezekiah king of Judah, and smote their tents, and the Meunim who were found there, and devoted them, until this day, and dwelt in their stead,—for there was pasture for their flocks, there.
text.KJV: And these written by name came in the days of Hezekiah king of Judah, and smote their tents, and the habitations that were found there, and destroyed them utterly unto this day, and dwelt in their rooms: because there was pasture there for their flocks.
text.NABRE: They who have just been listed by name set out during the reign of Hezekiah, king of Judah, and attacked their tents and also the Meunites who were there. They put them under the ban that is still in force to this day and dwelt in their place because they found pasture there for their flocks.
text.DRB: These therefore, whom before we described by name, came in the days of Ezechias the king of Juda: and they struck their tabernacles, and the inhabitants that were found there, & clean destroyed them until this present day: and they dwelt for them, because they found there most fat pastures.

text.NRSV: And some of them, five hundred men of the Simeonites, went to Mount Seir, having as their leaders Pelatiah, Neariah, Rephaiah, and Uzziel, sons of Ishi;
text.Rotherham: And, some of them, of the sons of Simeon, went to Mount Seir, five hundred men,—with Pelatiah, and Neariah, and Rephaiah, and Uzziel, sons of Ishi, at their head;
text.KJV: And some of them, even of the sons of Simeon, five hundred men, went to mount Seir, having for their captains Pelatiah, and Neariah, and Rephaiah, and Uzziel, the sons of Ishi.
text.NABRE: Five hundred of them (the Simeonites) went to Mount Seir, with Pelatiah, Neariah, Rephaiah, and Uzziel, sons of Ishi, at their head.
text.DRB: Also of the children of Simeon there went into mount Seir five hundred men, having their Princes Phaltias and Naaria and Raphaia and Oziel the sons of Jesi:

text.NRSV: they destroyed the remnant of the Amalekites that had escaped, and they have lived there to this day.
text.Rotherham: and they smote the remainder that had escaped, of the Amalekites,—and dwelt there—[as they have] unto this day.
text.KJV: And they smote the rest of the Amalekites that were escaped, and dwelt there unto this day.
text.NABRE: They attacked the surviving Amalekites who had escaped, and have lived there to the present day.
text.DRB: and they struck the remnant of the Amalecites, which were able to escape, and they dwelt there for them until this day.

text.NRSV: The sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel. (He was the firstborn, but because he defiled his father’s bed his birthright was given to the sons of Joseph son of Israel, so that he is not enrolled in the genealogy according to the birthright;
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel, for, he, was the firstborn, but because he defiled the couch of his father, his birthright was given to the sons of Joseph son of Israel,—and he is not to be enrolled in the place of firstborn,
text.KJV: Now the sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel, (for he was the firstborn; but, forasmuch as he defiled his father’s bed, his birthright was given unto the sons of Joseph the son of Israel: and the genealogy is not to be reckoned after the birthright.
text.NABRE: Chapter 5 Reuben. The sons of Reuben, the firstborn of Israel. (He was indeed the firstborn, but because he defiled the couch of his father his birthright was given to the sons of Joseph, son of Israel, so that he is not listed in the family records according to his birthright.
text.DRB: Also the sons of Ruben the first-begotten of Israel (for he was his first-begotten: but when he had violated his father's bed, his first-birth-right was given to the sons of Joseph the son of Israel, and he was not reputed for the first-begotten.

text.NRSV: though Judah became prominent among his brothers and a ruler came from him, yet the birthright belonged to Joseph.)
text.Rotherham: for, Judah, prevailed over his brethren, so that, even the prince, is from him,—although, the birthright, pertaineth to Joseph;
text.KJV: For Judah prevailed above his brethren, and of him came the chief ruler; but the birthright was Joseph’s:)
text.NABRE: Judah, in fact, became powerful among his brothers, so that the ruler came from him, though the birthright had been Joseph’s.)
text.DRB: Moreover Judas, which was the strongest among his brethren, of his stock sprang the Princes: but the first-birth-right was reputed to Joseph.)

text.NRSV: The sons of Reuben, the firstborn of Israel: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel, Hanoch and Pallu, Hezron and Carmi.
text.KJV: The sons, I say, of Reuben the firstborn of Israel were, Hanoch, and Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
text.NABRE: The sons of Reuben, the firstborn of Israel, were Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
text.DRB: The sons then of Ruben the first-begotten of Israel: Enoch, and Phallu, Esron, and Charmi.

text.NRSV: The sons of Joel: Shemaiah his son, Gog his son, Shimei his son,
text.Rotherham: The sons of Joel, Shemaiah his son, Gog his son, Shimei his son;
text.KJV: The sons of Joel; Shemaiah his son, Gog his son, Shimei his son,
text.NABRE: His son was Joel, whose son was Shemaiah, whose son was Gog, whose son was Shimei,
text.DRB: The sons of Joel: Samia his son, Gog his son, Semei his son.

text.NRSV: Micah his son, Reaiah his son, Baal his son,
text.Rotherham: Micah his son, Reaiah his son, Baal his son;
text.KJV: Micah his son, Reaia his son, Baal his son,
text.NABRE: whose son was Micah, whose son was Reaiah, whose son was Baal,
text.DRB: Micha his son, Reia his son, Baal his son,

text.NRSV: Beerah his son, whom King Tilgath-pilneser of Assyria carried away into exile; he was a pioneer of the Reubenites.
text.Rotherham: Beerah his son, whom Tilgath-pilneser king of Assyria carried away captive,—he, was a leader to the Reubenites;
text.KJV: Beerah his son, whom Tilgath–pilneser king of Assyria carried away captive: he was prince of the Reubenites.
text.NABRE: whose son was Beerah, whom Tilgath-pileser, the king of Assyria, took into exile; he was a prince of the Reubenites.
text.DRB: Beera his son, whom Theglathphalnasar the king of the Assyrians led away captive, and he was Prince in the tribe of Ruben.

text.NRSV: And his kindred by their families, when the genealogy of their generations was reckoned: the chief, Jeiel, and Zechariah,
text.Rotherham: and his brethren, by his families, in the genealogical registering, by their generations,—the chief Jeiel, and Zechariah;
text.KJV: And his brethren by their families, when the genealogy of their generations was reckoned, were the chief, Jeiel, and Zechariah,
text.NABRE: His brothers who belonged to his clans, when they were listed in the family records according to their descendants, were: Jeiel, the chief, and Zechariah,
text.DRB: And his brethren, and all his kindred, when they were numbered by their families, had these Princes Jehiel, and Zacharias.

text.NRSV: and Bela son of Azaz, son of Shema, son of Joel, who lived in Aroer, as far as Nebo and Baal-meon.
text.Rotherham: and Bela, the son of Azaz, the son of Shema, the son of Joel,—he, dwelt in Aroer, even as far as Nebo, and Baal-meon;
text.KJV: And Bela the son of Azaz, the son of Shema, the son of Joel, who dwelt in Aroer, even unto Nebo and Baal–meon:
text.NABRE: and Bela, son of Azaz, son of Shema, son of Joel. The Reubenites lived in Aroer and as far as Nebo and Baal-meon;
text.DRB: Moreover Bala the son of Azaz, the son of Samma, the son of Joel, he dwelt in Aroer as far as Nebo, and Beelmeon.

text.NRSV: He also lived to the east as far as the beginning of the desert this side of the Euphrates, because their cattle had multiplied in the land of Gilead.
text.Rotherham: and, eastward, dwelt he as far as the entering in of the desert, from the river Euphrates,—because, their cattle, were multiplied in the land of Gilead;
text.KJV: And eastward he inhabited unto the entering in of the wilderness from the river Euphrates: because their cattle were multiplied in the land of Gilead.
text.NABRE: toward the east they dwelt as far as the wilderness which extends from the Euphrates River, for they had much livestock in the land of Gilead.
text.DRB: Against the east quarter also he dwelt unto the entrance of the desert, and the river Euphrates. For they possessed a great number of cattle in the land of Galaad.

text.NRSV: And in the days of Saul they made war on the Hagrites, who fell by their hand; and they lived in their tents throughout all the region east of Gilead.
text.Rotherham: and, in the days of Saul, they made war with the Hagrites, who fell by their hand,—so they dwelt in their tents, over all the face of the land east of Gilead.
text.KJV: And in the days of Saul they made war with the Hagarites, who fell by their hand: and they dwelt in their tents throughout all the east land of Gilead.
text.NABRE: In Saul’s time they waged war with the Hagrites, and when they had defeated them they dwelt in their tents throughout the region east of Gilead.
text.DRB: And in the days of Saul they fought against the Agarenes, and slew them, and dwelt for them in their tabernacles, in all the quarter, that looketh to the East of Galaad.

text.NRSV: The sons of Gad lived beside them in the land of Bashan as far as Salecah:
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Gad, over against them, dwelt, in the land of Bashan, as far as Salecah:
text.KJV: And the children of Gad dwelt over against them, in the land of Bashan unto Salchah:
text.NABRE: Gad. The Gadites lived alongside them in the land of Bashan as far as Salecah.
text.DRB: But the children of Gad dwelt over-against them in the land of Basan, as far as Selcha:

text.NRSV: Joel the chief, Shapham the second, Janai, and Shaphat in Bashan.
text.Rotherham: Joel the chief, and Shapham, the second,—and Janai and Shaphat, in Bashan;
text.KJV: Joel the chief, and Shapham the next, and Jaanai, and Shaphat in Bashan.
text.NABRE: Joel was chief, Shapham was second in command, and Janai was judge in Bashan.
text.DRB: Johel the head, and Sapham the second: and Janai, & Saphat in Basan.

text.NRSV: And their kindred according to their ancestral houses: Michael, Meshullam, Sheba, Jorai, Jacan, Zia, and Eber, seven.
text.Rotherham: and their brethren of their ancestral house, Michael and Meshullam and Sheba and Jorai and Jacan and Zia and Eber, seven.
text.KJV: And their brethren of the house of their fathers were, Michael, and Meshullam, and Sheba, and Jorai, and Jachan, and Zia, and Heber, seven.
text.NABRE: Their brothers, according to their ancestral houses, were: Michael, Meshullam, Sheba, Jorai, Jacan, Zia, and Eber—seven.
text.DRB: And their brethren according to the houses of their kindreds, Michael, and Mosollam, and Sebe, and Jorai, and Jacan, and Zie, & Heber, seven.

text.NRSV: These were the sons of Abihail son of Huri, son of Jaroah, son of Gilead, son of Michael, son of Jeshishai, son of Jahdo, son of Buz;
text.Rotherham: These, were the sons of Abihail, son of Huri, son of Jaroah, son of Gilead, son of Michael, son of Jehishai, son of Jahdo, son of Buz,
text.KJV: These are the children of Abihail the son of Huri, the son of Jaroah, the son of Gilead, the son of Michael, the son of Jeshishai, the son of Jahdo, the son of Buz;
text.NABRE: These were the sons of Abihail, son of Huri, son of Jaroah, son of Gilead, son of Michael, son of Jeshishai, son of Jahdo, son of Buz.
text.DRB: These are the sons of Abihail, the son of Huri, the son of Jara, the son of Galaad, the son of Michael, the son of Jesesi, the son of Jeddo, the son of Buz.

text.NRSV: Ahi son of Abdiel, son of Guni, was chief in their ancestral houses;
text.Rotherham: Ahi son of Abdiel, son of Guni, chief of their ancestral house;
text.KJV: Ahi the son of Abdiel, the son of Guni, chief of the house of their fathers.
text.NABRE: Ahi, son of Abdiel, son of Guni, was the head of their ancestral houses.
text.DRB: Also the brethren of the son of Abdiel, the son of Guni, prince of the house in their families.

text.NRSV: and they lived in Gilead, in Bashan and its towns, and in all the pasture lands of Sharon to their limits.
text.Rotherham: and they dwelt in Gilead—in Bashan, and in her villages,—and in all the pasture-lands of Sharon, up to their outgoings.
text.KJV: And they dwelt in Gilead in Bashan, and in her towns, and in all the suburbs of Sharon, upon their borders.
text.NABRE: They dwelt in Gilead, in Bashan and its towns, and in all the pasture lands of Sirion to the borders.
text.DRB: And they dwelt in Galaad, and in Basan, and in the towns thereof, and in all the suburbs of Saron, unto the borders.

text.NRSV: All of these were enrolled by genealogies in the days of King Jotham of Judah, and in the days of King Jeroboam of Israel.
text.Rotherham: All of them, were genealogically registered in the days of Jotham king of Judah,—and in the days of Jeroboam king of Israel.
text.KJV: All these were reckoned by genealogies in the days of Jotham king of Judah, and in the days of Jeroboam king of Israel.
text.NABRE: All were listed in the family records in the time of Jotham, king of Judah, and of Jeroboam, king of Israel.
text.DRB: All these were numbered in the days of Joathan the king of Juda, and in the days of Jeroboam the king of Israel.

text.NRSV: The Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh had valiant warriors, who carried shield and sword, and drew the bow, expert in war, forty-four thousand seven hundred sixty, ready for service.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Reuben and Gad, and half tribe of Manasseh, of the sons of valour, men bearing shield and sword and bending the bow, and instructed in war, were forty-four thousand and seven hundred and sixty, ready to go forth in the host.
text.KJV: The sons of Reuben, and the Gadites, and half the tribe of Manasseh, of valiant men, men able to bear buckler and sword, and to shoot with bow, and skilful in war, were four and forty thousand seven hundred and threescore, that went out to the war.
text.NABRE: The Reubenites, Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh were warriors, men who bore shield and sword and who drew the bow, trained in warfare—forty-four thousand seven hundred and sixty men fit for military service.
text.DRB: The children of Ruben, and of Gad, and the half tribe of Manasses, men of war, carrying shields, and swords, and bending the bow, and taught to battles, four and forty thousand and seven hundred threescore going forth to fight.

text.NRSV: They made war on the Hagrites, Jetur, Naphish, and Nodab;
text.Rotherham: So they made war with the Hagrites,—and Jetur and Naphish, and Nodab;
text.KJV: And they made war with the Hagarites, with Jetur, and Nephish, and Nodab.
text.NABRE: When they waged war against the Hagrites and against Jetur, Naphish, and Nodab,
text.DRB: They fought against the Agarenes, but the Itureians, and Naphis, and Nodab

text.NRSV: and when they received help against them, the Hagrites and all who were with them were delivered into their hands, for they cried to God in the battle, and he granted their entreaty because they trusted in him.
text.Rotherham: and were helped against them, and the Hagrites and all that were with them were delivered into their hand,—for, unto God, made they outcry, in the war, and he suffered himself to be entreated by them, because they put their trust in him;
text.KJV: And they were helped against them, and the Hagarites were delivered into their hand, and all that were with them: for they cried to God in the battle, and he was intreated of them; because they put their trust in him.
text.NABRE: they received help so that the Hagrites and all who were with them were delivered into their power. For during the battle they cried out to God, and he heard them because they had put their trust in him.
text.DRB: gave them aid. And the Agarenes were delivered into their hands, and all that were with them, because they called upon God when they fought: and he heard them, because they believed in him.

text.NRSV: They captured their livestock: fifty thousand of their camels, two hundred fifty thousand sheep, two thousand donkeys, and one hundred thousand captives.
text.Rotherham: and they captured their cattle—their camels, fifty thousand, and, flocks, two hundred and fifty thousand, and, asses, two thousand,—and, persons of men, a hundred thousand;
text.KJV: And they took away their cattle; of their camels fifty thousand, and of sheep two hundred and fifty thousand, and of asses two thousand, and of men an hundred thousand.
text.NABRE: Along with one hundred thousand persons they also captured their livestock: fifty thousand camels, two hundred fifty thousand sheep, and two thousand donkeys.
text.DRB: And they took all that they possessed, Camels fifty thousand, and sheep two hundred fifty thousand, and asses two thousand, and of men an hundred thousand souls.

text.NRSV: Many were slain, because the war was of God. And they lived in their territory until the exile.
text.Rotherham: for, many slain, fell, for, of God, was the war,—and they dwelt in their stead, until the captivity.
text.KJV: For there fell down many slain, because the war was of God. And they dwelt in their steads until the captivity.
text.NABRE: Many were slain and fell; for “From God the victory.” They dwelt in their place until the time of the exile.
text.DRB: And many fell down wounded: for it was the battle of our Lord. And they dwelt for them until the transmigration.

text.NRSV: The members of the half-tribe of Manasseh lived in the land; they were very numerous from Bashan to Baal-hermon, Senir, and Mount Hermon.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of the half tribe of Manasseh, dwelt in the land,—from Bashan, as far as to Baal-hermon and Senir and Mount Hermon, they, were multiplied.
text.KJV: And the children of the half tribe of Manasseh dwelt in the land: they increased from Bashan unto Baal–hermon and Senir, and unto mount Hermon.
text.NABRE: The Half-tribe of Manasseh. The half-tribe of Manasseh lived in the land of Bashan as far as Baal-hermon, Senir, and Mount Hermon; they were numerous.
text.DRB: Also the children of the half tribe of Manasses possessed the land, from the coasts of Basan unto Baal, Hermon, and Sanir, & mount Hermon, for the number was great.

text.NRSV: These were the heads of their ancestral houses: Epher, Ishi, Eliel, Azriel, Jeremiah, Hodaviah, and Jahdiel, mighty warriors, famous men, heads of their ancestral houses.
text.Rotherham: And, these, were the heads of their ancestral house,—Epher and Ishi and Eliel and Azriel and Jeremiah and Hodaviah and Jahdiel, men who were heroes of valour, men of renown, chiefs to their ancestral house.
text.KJV: And these were the heads of the house of their fathers, even Epher, and Ishi, and Eliel, and Azriel, and Jeremiah, and Hodaviah, and Jahdiel, mighty men of valour, famous men, and heads of the house of their fathers.
text.NABRE: The following were the heads of their ancestral houses: Epher, Ishi, Eliel, Azriel, Jeremiah, Hodaviah, and Jahdiel—men who were warriors, famous men, and heads over their ancestral houses.
text.DRB: And these were the Princes of the house of their kindred, Epher, & Jesi, and Eliel, & Esriel, and Jeremia, and Odoia, and Jediel, most valiant men and mighty, & renowned Princes in their families.

text.NRSV: But they were unfaithful to the God of their ancestors, and prostituted themselves to the gods of the peoples of the land, whom God had destroyed before them.
text.Rotherham: But they dealt unfaithfully with the God of their fathers,—and went unchastely after the gods of the peoples of the land, whom God destroyed from before them;
text.KJV: And they transgressed against the God of their fathers, and went a whoring after the gods of the people of the land, whom God destroyed before them.
text.NABRE: However, they acted treacherously toward the God of their ancestors by prostituting themselves to follow the gods of the peoples of the land, whom God had destroyed before them.
text.DRB: But they forsook the God of their fathers, and fornicated after the gods of the peoples of the land, whom God took away before them.

text.NRSV: So the God of Israel stirred up the spirit of King Pul of Assyria, the spirit of King Tilgath-pilneser of Assyria, and he carried them away, namely, the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh, and brought them to Halah, Habor, Hara, and the river Gozan, to this day.
text.Rotherham: so the God of Israel stirred up the spirit of Pul king of Assyria, and the spirit of Tilgath-pilneser king of Assyria, and he took them away captive, even the Reubenites and the Gadites and the half tribe of Manasseh,—and brought them in unto Halah, and Habor, and Hara, and to the river of Gozan, unto this day.
text.KJV: And the God of Israel stirred up the spirit of Pul king of Assyria, and the spirit of Tilgath–pilneser king of Assyria, and he carried them away, even the Reubenites, and the Gadites, and the half tribe of Manasseh, and brought them unto Halah, and Habor, and Hara, and to the river Gozan, unto this day.
text.NABRE: Therefore the God of Israel stirred up against them the anger of Pul, king of Assyria, and the anger of Tilgath-pilneser [ sic ], king of Assyria, who deported the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh and brought them to Halah, Habor, and Hara, and to the river Gozan, where they have remained to this day. Levi.
text.DRB: And the God of Israel raised up the spirit of Phul king of the Assyrians, and the spirit of Thelgathphalnasar king of Assur: and he transported Ruben, and Gad, and the half tribe of Manasses, and brought them into Lahela, and into Habor, and to Ara, and to the river of Gozan, until this day.

text.NRSV: The sons of Levi: Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Levi, Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
text.KJV: The sons of Levi; Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
text.NABRE: Chapter 6 The sons of Levi were Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
text.DRB: The sons of Levi: Gerson, Caath, and Merari.

text.NRSV: The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Kohath, Amram, Izhar, and Hebron, and Uzziel.
text.KJV: And the sons of Kohath; Amram, Izhar, and Hebron, and Uzziel.
text.NABRE: The sons of Gershon were named Libni and Shimei.
text.DRB: The sons of Caath: Amram, Isaar, Hebron, and Oziel.

text.NRSV: The children of Amram: Aaron, Moses, and Miriam. The sons of Aaron: Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
text.Rotherham: And, the children of Amram, Aaron, and Moses, and Miriam,—and, the sons of Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
text.KJV: And the children of Amram; Aaron, and Moses, and Miriam. The sons also of Aaron; Nadab, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
text.NABRE: The sons of Kohath were Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
text.DRB: The children of Amram: Aaron, Moyses, and Maria. The sons of Aaron: Nadab and Abiu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

text.NRSV: Eleazar became the father of Phinehas, Phinehas became the father of Abishua,
text.Rotherham: Eleazar, begat Phinehas, Phinehas, begat Abishua;
text.KJV: Eleazar begat Phinehas, Phinehas begat Abishua,
text.NABRE: The sons of Merari were Mahli and Mushi. These were the clans of Levi, according to their ancestors.
text.DRB: Eleazar begat Phinees, and Phinees begat Abisue,

text.NRSV: Abishua became the father of Bukki, Bukki became the father of Uzzi,
text.Rotherham: and, Abishua, begat Bukki, and, Bukki, begat Uzzi;
text.KJV: And Abishua begat Bukki, and Bukki begat Uzzi,
text.NABRE: Of Gershon: his son Libni, whose son was Jahath, whose son was Zimmah,
text.DRB: and Abisue begat Bocci, and Bocci begat Ozi.

text.NRSV: Uzzi became the father of Zerahiah, Zerahiah became the father of Meraioth,
text.Rotherham: and, Uzzi, begat Zerahiah, and, Zerahiah, begat Meraioth;
text.KJV: And Uzzi begat Zerahiah, and Zerahiah begat Meraioth,
text.NABRE: whose son was Joah, whose son was Iddo, whose son was Zerah, whose son was Jetherai.
text.DRB: Ozi begat Zaraias, and Zaraias begat Maraioth.

text.NRSV: Meraioth became the father of Amariah, Amariah became the father of Ahitub,
text.Rotherham: Meraioth, begat Amariah, and, Amariah, begat Ahitub;
text.KJV: Meraioth begat Amariah, and Amariah begat Ahitub,
text.NABRE: The sons of Kohath: his son Amminadab, whose son was Korah, whose son was Assir,
text.DRB: Moreover Meraioth begat Amarias, and Amarias begat Achitob.

text.NRSV: Ahitub became the father of Zadok, Zadok became the father of Ahimaaz,
text.Rotherham: and, Ahitub, begat Zadok, and, Zadok, begat Ahimaaz;
text.KJV: And Ahitub begat Zadok, and Zadok begat Ahimaaz,
text.NABRE: whose son was Elkanah, whose son was Ebiasaph, whose son was Assir,
text.DRB: Achitob begat Sadoc, and Sadoc begat Achimaas,

text.NRSV: Ahimaaz became the father of Azariah, Azariah became the father of Johanan,
text.Rotherham: and, Ahimaaz, begat Azariah, and, Azariah, begat Johanan,
text.KJV: And Ahimaaz begat Azariah, and Azariah begat Johanan,
text.NABRE: whose son was Tahath, whose son was Uriel, whose son was Uzziah, whose son was Shaul.
text.DRB: Achimaas begat Azarias, Azarias begat Johanan,

text.NRSV: and Johanan became the father of Azariah (it was he who served as priest in the house that Solomon built in Jerusalem).
text.Rotherham: and, Johanan, begat Azariah,—he, it was who ministered as priest, in the house which Solomon built in Jerusalem;
text.KJV: And Johanan begat Azariah, (he it is that executed the priest’s office in the temple that Solomon built in Jerusalem:)
text.NABRE: The sons of Elkanah were Amasai and Ahimoth,
text.DRB: Johanan begat Azarias. The same is he that executed the priestly office in the house, which Salomon built in Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Azariah became the father of Amariah, Amariah became the father of Ahitub,
text.Rotherham: and Azariah begat Amariah,—and, Amariah, begat Ahitub;
text.KJV: And Azariah begat Amariah, and Amariah begat Ahitub,
text.NABRE: whose son was Elkanah, whose son was Zophai, whose son was Nahath,
text.DRB: And Azarias begat Amarias, and Amarias begat Achitob,

text.NRSV: Ahitub became the father of Zadok, Zadok became the father of Shallum,
text.Rotherham: and, Ahitub, begat Zadok, and, Zadok, begat Shallum;
text.KJV: And Ahitub begat Zadok, and Zadok begat Shallum,
text.NABRE: whose son was Eliab, whose son was Jeroham, whose son was Elkanah, whose son was Samuel.
text.DRB: and Achitob begat Sadoc, and Sadoc begat Sellum,

text.NRSV: Shallum became the father of Hilkiah, Hilkiah became the father of Azariah,
text.Rotherham: and, Shallum, begat Hilkiah, and Hilkiah, begat Azariah;
text.KJV: And Shallum begat Hilkiah, and Hilkiah begat Azariah,
text.NABRE: The sons of Samuel were Joel, the firstborn, and Abijah, the second.
text.DRB: Sellum begat Helcias, and Helcias begat Azarias,

text.NRSV: Azariah became the father of Seraiah, Seraiah became the father of Jehozadak;
text.Rotherham: and, Azariah, begat Seraiah, and, Seraiah, begat Jehozadak;
text.KJV: And Azariah begat Seraiah, and Seraiah begat Jehozadak,
text.NABRE: The sons of Merari: Mahli, whose son was Libni, whose son was Shimei, whose son was Uzzah,
text.DRB: Azarias begat Saraias, & Saraias begat Josedec.

text.NRSV: and Jehozadak went into exile when the Lord sent Judah and Jerusalem into exile by the hand of Nebuchadnezzar.
text.Rotherham: and, Jehozadak, departed, when Yahweh carried away Judah and Jerusalem into captivity,—by the hand of Nebuchadnezzar.
text.KJV: And Jehozadak went into captivity, when the Lord carried away Judah and Jerusalem by the hand of Nebuchadnezzar.
text.NABRE: whose son was Shimea, whose son was Haggiah, whose son was Asaiah.
text.DRB: Moreover Josedec went forth, when our Lord transported Juda and Jerusalem by the hands of Nabuchodonosor.

text.NRSV: The sons of Levi: Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Levi, Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.
text.KJV: The sons of Levi; Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.
text.NABRE: The following were established by David for the service of song in the Lord ’s house at the time when the ark had a resting place.
text.DRB: The sons then of Levi: Gerson, Caath, and Merari.

text.NRSV: These are the names of the sons of Gershom: Libni and Shimei.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the names of the sons of Gershom, Libni and Shimei.
text.KJV: And these be the names of the sons of Gershom; Libni, and Shimei.
text.NABRE: They served as singers before the tabernacle of the tent of meeting until Solomon built the house of the Lord in Jerusalem, and they performed their services according to the order prescribed for them.
text.DRB: And these be the names of the sons of Gerson: Lobni and Semei.

text.NRSV: The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Kohath, were Amram, and Izhar, and Hebron, and Uzziel.
text.KJV: And the sons of Kohath were, Amram, and Izhar, and Hebron, and Uzziel.
text.NABRE: Those who so performed are the following, together with their sons. Among the Kohathites: Heman, the chanter, son of Joel, son of Samuel,
text.DRB: The sons of Caath: Amram, and Isaar, and Hebron, and Oziel.

text.NRSV: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites according to their ancestral houses.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Merari, Mahli and Mushi. And, these, are the families of Levi by their fathers:
text.KJV: The sons of Merari; Mahli, and Mushi. And these are the families of the Levites according to their fathers.
text.NABRE: son of Elkanah, son of Jeroham, son of Eliel, son of Toah,
text.DRB: The sons of Merari: Moholi and Musi. And these are the kindreds of Levi according to their families.

text.NRSV: Of Gershom: Libni his son, Jahath his son, Zimmah his son,
text.Rotherham: To Gershom, pertained Libni his son, Jahath his son, Zimmah his son,
text.KJV: Of Gershom; Libni his son, Jahath his son, Zimmah his son,
text.NABRE: son of Zuph, son of Elkanah, son of Mahath, son of Amasi,
text.DRB: Gerson, Lobni his son, Jahath his son, Zamma his son,

text.NRSV: Joah his son, Iddo his son, Zerah his son, Jeatherai his son.
text.Rotherham: Joah his son, Iddo his son, Zerah his son, Jeatherai his son.
text.KJV: Joah his son, Iddo his son, Zerah his son, Jeaterai his son.
text.NABRE: son of Elkanah, son of Joel, son of Azariah, son of Zephaniah,
text.DRB: Joah his son, Addo his son, Zara his son, Jethrai his son.

text.NRSV: The sons of Kohath: Amminadab his son, Korah his son, Assir his son,
text.Rotherham: The sons of Kohath, Amminadab his son, Korah his son, Assir his son;
text.KJV: The sons of Kohath; Amminadab his son, Korah his son, Assir his son,
text.NABRE: son of Tahath, son of Assir, son of Ebiasaph, son of Korah,
text.DRB: The sons of Caath, Aminadab his son, Core his son, Asir his son,

text.NRSV: Elkanah his son, Ebiasaph his son, Assir his son,
text.Rotherham: Elkanah his son, and Ebiasaph his son, and Assir his son;
text.KJV: Elkanah his son, and Ebiasaph his son, and Assir his son,
text.NABRE: son of Izhar, son of Kohath, son of Levi, son of Israel.
text.DRB: Elcana his son, Abiasaph his son, Asir his son.

text.NRSV: Tahath his son, Uriel his son, Uzziah his son, and Shaul his son.
text.Rotherham: Tahath his son, Uriel his son, Uzziah his son, and Shaul his son.
text.KJV: Tahath his son, Uriel his son, Uzziah his son, and Shaul his son.
text.NABRE: His brother Asaph stood at his right hand. Asaph was the son of Berechiah, son of Shimea,
text.DRB: Thahath his son, Uriel his son, Ozias his son, Saul his son.

text.NRSV: The sons of Elkanah: Amasai and Ahimoth,
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Elkanah, Amasai, and Ahimoth.
text.KJV: And the sons of Elkanah; Amasai, and Ahimoth.
text.NABRE: son of Michael, son of Baaseiah, son of Malchijah,
text.DRB: The sons of Elcana: Amasai, and Achimoth,

text.NRSV: Elkanah his son, Zophai his son, Nahath his son,
text.Rotherham: As for Elkanah, the sons of Elkanah, Zophai his son, and Nahath his son;
text.KJV: As for Elkanah: the sons of Elkanah; Zophai his son, and Nahath his son,
text.NABRE: son of Ethni, son of Zerah, son of Adaiah,
text.DRB: & Elcana: The sons of Elcana: Sophai his son, Nahath his son,

text.NRSV: Eliab his son, Jeroham his son, Elkanah his son.
text.Rotherham: Eliab his son, Jeroham his son, Elkanah his son.
text.KJV: Eliab his son, Jeroham his son, Elkanah his son.
text.NABRE: son of Ethan, son of Zimmah, son of Shimei,
text.DRB: Eliab his son, Jeroham his son, Elcana his son.

text.NRSV: The sons of Samuel: Joel his firstborn, the second Abijah.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Samuel, the firstborn, [Joel], and, the second, Abiah.
text.KJV: And the sons of Samuel; the firstborn Vashni, and Abiah.
text.NABRE: son of Jahath, son of Gershon, son of Levi.
text.DRB: The sons of Samuel: the first-begotten Vasseni, and Abia.

text.NRSV: The sons of Merari: Mahli, Libni his son, Shimei his son, Uzzah his son,
text.Rotherham: the sons of Merari, Mahli,—Libni his son, Shimei his son, Uzzah his son;
text.KJV: The sons of Merari; Mahli, Libni his son, Shimei his son, Uzza his son,
text.NABRE: Their brothers, the Merarites, stood at the left: Ethan, son of Kishi, son of Abdi, son of Malluch,
text.DRB: And the sons of Merari, Moholi: Lobni his son, Semei his son, Oza his son,

text.NRSV: Shimea his son, Haggiah his son, and Asaiah his son.
text.Rotherham: Shimea his son, Haggiah his son, Asaiah his son.
text.KJV: Shimea his son, Haggiah his son, Asaiah his son.
text.NABRE: son of Hashabiah, son of Amaziah, son of Hilkiah,
text.DRB: Sammaa his son, Haggia his son, Asaia his son.

text.NRSV: These are the men whom David put in charge of the service of song in the house of the Lord, after the ark rested there.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are they whom David caused to stand, as the servants of song, in the house of Yahweh, when he had given rest unto the ark;
text.KJV: And these are they whom David set over the service of song in the house of the Lord, after that the ark had rest.
text.NABRE: son of Amzi, son of Bani, son of Shemer,
text.DRB: These are they, whom David appointed over the singing men of the house of our Lord, since the Ark was placed:

text.NRSV: They ministered with song before the tabernacle of the tent of meeting, until Solomon had built the house of the Lord in Jerusalem; and they performed their service in due order.
text.Rotherham: so they became attendants before the habitation of the tent of meeting, with song, until Solomon built the house of Yahweh, in Jerusalem,—and they took their stand according to their prescribed manner over their work.
text.KJV: And they ministered before the dwelling place of the tabernacle of the congregation with singing, until Solomon had built the house of the Lord in Jerusalem: and then they waited on their office according to their order.
text.NABRE: son of Mahli, son of Mushi, son of Merari, son of Levi.
text.DRB: & they ministered before the tabernacle of testimony, singing, until Salomon built the house of our Lord in Jerusalem: & they stood according to their order in the ministry.

text.NRSV: These are the men who served; and their sons: Of the Kohathites: Heman, the singer, son of Joel, son of Samuel,
text.Rotherham: Yea, these, are they who stood, with their sons. Of the sons of the Kohathites, Heman the singer, son of Joel, son of Samuel,
text.KJV: And these are they that waited with their children. Of the sons of the Kohathites: Heman a singer, the son of Joel, the son of Shemuel,
text.NABRE: Their brother Levites were appointed to all the other services of the tabernacle of the house of God.
text.DRB: And these are they, which assisted with their sons, of the sons of Caath, Hemam singing man, the son of Joel, the son of Samuel,

text.NRSV: son of Elkanah, son of Jeroham, son of Eliel, son of Toah,
text.Rotherham: son of Elkanah, son of Jeroham, son of Eliel, son of Toah,—
text.KJV: The son of Elkanah, the son of Jeroham, the son of Eliel, the son of Toah,
text.NABRE: However, it was Aaron and his sons who made the sacrifice on the altar for burnt offerings and on the altar of incense; they alone had charge of the holy of holies and of making atonement for Israel, as Moses, the servant of God, had commanded.
text.DRB: the son of Elcana, the son of Jeroham, the son of Eliel, the son of Thohu,

text.NRSV: son of Zuph, son of Elkanah, son of Mahath, son of Amasai,
text.Rotherham: son of Zuph, son of Elkanah, son of Mahath, son of Amasai:—
text.KJV: The son of Zuph, the son of Elkanah, the son of Mahath, the son of Amasai,
text.NABRE: These were the sons of Aaron: his son Eleazar, whose son was Phinehas, whose son was Abishua,
text.DRB: the son of Suph, the son of Elcana, the son of Mahath, the son of Amasai,

text.NRSV: son of Elkanah, son of Joel, son of Azariah, son of Zephaniah,
text.Rotherham: son of Elkanah, son of Joel, son of Azariah, son of Zephaniah,—
text.KJV: The son of Elkanah, the son of Joel, the son of Azariah, the son of Zephaniah,
text.NABRE: whose son was Bukki, whose son was Uzzi, whose son was Zerahiah,
text.DRB: the son of Elcana, the son of Johel, the son of Azaria, the son of Sophonias,

text.NRSV: son of Tahath, son of Assir, son of Ebiasaph, son of Korah,
text.Rotherham: son of Tahath, son of Assir, son of Ebiasaph, son of Korah;—
text.KJV: The son of Tahath, the son of Assir, the son of Ebiasaph, the son of Korah,
text.NABRE: whose son was Meraioth, whose son was Amariah, whose son was Ahitub,
text.DRB: the son of Thahath, the son of Asir, the son of Abiasaph, the son of Core,

text.NRSV: son of Izhar, son of Kohath, son of Levi, son of Israel;
text.Rotherham: son of Izhar, son of Kohath, son of Levi, son of Israel.
text.KJV: The son of Izhar, the son of Kohath, the son of Levi, the son of Israel.
text.NABRE: whose son was Zadok, whose son was Ahimaaz.
text.DRB: the son of Isaar, the son of Caath, the son of Levi, the son of Israel.

text.NRSV: and his brother Asaph, who stood on his right, namely, Asaph son of Berechiah, son of Shimea,
text.Rotherham: And, his brother Asaph who stood on his right hand, Asaph son of Berechiah, son of Shimea,—
text.KJV: And his brother Asaph, who stood on his right hand, even Asaph the son of Berachiah, the son of Shimea,
text.NABRE: The following were their dwelling places, by encampments in their territories. To the sons of Aaron who belonged to the clan of the Kohathites, since the lot fell to them,
text.DRB: And his brother Asaph, who stood on his right hand, Asaph the son of Barachias, the son of Samaa,

text.NRSV: son of Michael, son of Baaseiah, son of Malchijah,
text.Rotherham: son of Michael, son of Baaseiah, son of Malchijah,—
text.KJV: The son of Michael, the son of Baaseiah, the son of Malchiah,
text.NABRE: was assigned Hebron in the land of Judah, with its adjacent pasture lands.
text.DRB: the son of Michael, the son of Basaia, the son of Melchia.

text.NRSV: son of Ethni, son of Zerah, son of Adaiah,
text.Rotherham: son of Ethni, son of Zerah, son of Adaiah;—
text.KJV: The son of Ethni, the son of Zerah, the son of Adaiah,
text.NABRE: However, the open country and the villages belonging to the city had been given to Caleb, the son of Jephunneh.
text.DRB: the son of Athanai, the son of Zara, the son of Adaia,

text.NRSV: son of Ethan, son of Zimmah, son of Shimei,
text.Rotherham: son of Ethan, son of Zimmah, son of Shimei,—
text.KJV: The son of Ethan, the son of Zimmah, the son of Shimei,
text.NABRE: There were assigned to the sons of Aaron: Hebron a city of refuge, Libnah with its pasture lands, Jattir with its pasture lands, Eshtemoa with its pasture lands,
text.DRB: the son of Ethan, the son of Zamma, the son of Semei.

text.NRSV: son of Jahath, son of Gershom, son of Levi.
text.Rotherham: son of Jahath, son of Gershom, son of Levi.
text.KJV: The son of Jahath, the son of Gershom, the son of Levi.
text.NABRE: Holon with its pasture lands, Debir with its pasture lands,
text.DRB: the son of Jeth, the son of Gerson, the son of Levi.

text.NRSV: On their left were their kindred the sons of Merari: Ethan son of Kishi, son of Abdi, son of Malluch,
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Merari, their brethren, on the left hand, Ethan son of Kishi, son of Abdi, son of Malluch;—
text.KJV: And their brethren the sons of Merari stood on the left hand: Ethan the son of Kishi, the son of Abdi, the son of Malluch,
text.NABRE: Ashan with its pasture lands, Jetta with its pasture lands, and Beth-shemesh with its pasture lands.
text.DRB: And the children of Merari their brethren, on the left hand, Ethan the son of Cusi, the son of Abdi, the son of Maloch,

text.NRSV: son of Hashabiah, son of Amaziah, son of Hilkiah,
text.Rotherham: son of Hashabiah, son of Amaziah, son of Hilkiah,—
text.KJV: The son of Hashabiah, the son of Amaziah, the son of Hilkiah,
text.NABRE: Also from the tribe of Benjamin: Gibeon with its pasture lands, Geba with its pasture lands, Almon with its pasture lands, Anathoth with its pasture lands. In all, they had thirteen cities with their pasture lands.
text.DRB: the son of Hasabia, the son of Amasia, the son of Helcias,

text.NRSV: son of Amzi, son of Bani, son of Shemer,
text.Rotherham: son of Amzi, son of Bani, son of Shemer;—
text.KJV: The son of Amzi, the son of Bani, the son of Shamer,
text.NABRE: The other Kohathites obtained ten cities by lot for their clans from the tribe of Ephraim, from the tribe of Dan, and from the half-tribe of Manasseh.
text.DRB: the son of Amasai, the son of Boni, the son of Somer,

text.NRSV: son of Mahli, son of Mushi, son of Merari, son of Levi;
text.Rotherham: son of Mahli, son of Mushi, son of Merari, son of Levi.
text.KJV: The son of Mahli, the son of Mushi, the son of Merari, the son of Levi.
text.NABRE: The clans of the Gershonites obtained thirteen cities from the tribes of Issachar, Asher, and Naphtali, and from the half-tribe of Manasseh in Bashan.
text.DRB: the son of Moholi, the son of Musi, the son of Merari, the son of Levi.

text.NRSV: and their kindred the Levites were appointed for all the service of the tabernacle of the house of God.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, their brethren, the Levites, were given, for all the work of the habitation, of the house of God.
text.KJV: Their brethren also the Levites were appointed unto all manner of service of the tabernacle of the house of God.
text.NABRE: The clans of the Merarites obtained twelve cities by lot from the tribes of Reuben, Gad, and Zebulun.
text.DRB: Their brethren also the Levites, which were ordained for all the ministry of the tabernacle of the house of our Lord.

text.NRSV: But Aaron and his sons made offerings on the altar of burnt offering and on the altar of incense, performing all the work of the most holy place, to make atonement for Israel, according to all that Moses the servant of God had commanded.
text.Rotherham: But, Aaron and his sons, were to make perfume upon the altar of ascending-sacrifice, and upon the altar of incense, according to all the service of the holy of holies,—even for putting a propitiatory-covering over Israel, according go all that, Moses, the servant of God, commanded.
text.KJV: But Aaron and his sons offered upon the altar of the burnt offering, and on the altar of incense, and were appointed for all the work of the place most holy, and to make an atonement for Israel, according to all that Moses the servant of God had commanded.
text.NABRE: The Israelites assigned these cities with their pasture lands to the Levites,
text.DRB: But Aaron, and his sons burnt incense upon the altar of holocaust, and upon the altar of incense, for every work of Sancta Sanctorum: and to pray for Israel according to all things, which Moyses the servant of God commanded.

text.NRSV: These are the sons of Aaron: Eleazar his son, Phinehas his son, Abishua his son,
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the sons of Aaron,—Eleazar his son, Phinehas his son, Abishua his son,—
text.KJV: And these are the sons of Aaron; Eleazar his son, Phinehas his son, Abishua his son,
text.NABRE: designating them by name and assigning them by lot from the tribes of the Judahites, Simeonites, and Benjaminites.
text.DRB: And these are the sons of Aaron: Eleazar his son, Phinees his son, Abisue his son.

text.NRSV: Bukki his son, Uzzi his son, Zerahiah his son,
text.Rotherham: Bukki his son, Uzzi his son, Zerahiah his son,—
text.KJV: Bukki his son, Uzzi his son, Zerahiah his son,
text.NABRE: The clans of the Kohathites obtained cities by lot from the tribe of Ephraim.
text.DRB: Bocci his son, Ozi his son, Zarahia his son,

text.NRSV: Meraioth his son, Amariah his son, Ahitub his son,
text.Rotherham: Meraioth his son, Amariah his son, Ahitub his son,—
text.KJV: Meraioth his son, Amariah his son, Ahitub his son,
text.NABRE: They were assigned cities of refuge: Shechem in the mountain region of Ephraim, with its pasture lands, Gezer with its pasture lands,
text.DRB: Meraioth his son, Amarias his son, Achitob his son,

text.NRSV: Zadok his son, Ahimaaz his son.
text.Rotherham: Zadok his son, Ahimaaz his son.
text.KJV: Zadok his son, Ahimaaz his son.
text.NABRE: Kibzaim with its pasture lands, and Beth-horon with its pasture lands.
text.DRB: Sadoc his son, Achimaas his son.

text.NRSV: These are their dwelling places according to their settlements within their borders: to the sons of Aaron of the families of Kohathites—for the lot fell to them first—
text.Rotherham: And, these, are their dwelling-places, according to their encampments, in their boundary,—pertaining to the sons of Aaron, of the families of the Kohathites, for, theirs, was the lot:
text.KJV: Now these are their dwelling places throughout their castles in their coasts, of the sons of Aaron, of the families of the Kohathites: for theirs was the lot.
text.NABRE: From the tribe of Dan: Elteke with its pasture lands, Gibbethon with its pasture lands, Aijalon with its pasture lands, and Gath-rimmon with its pasture lands.
text.DRB: And these are their habitations by the towns and confines, to wit, of the sons of Aaron, according to the kindreds of the Caathites: for they were fallen to them by lot.

text.NRSV: to them they gave Hebron in the land of Judah and its surrounding pasture lands,
text.Rotherham: so they gave them Hebron, in the land of Judah, and the pasture lands thereof, round about it;
text.KJV: And they gave them Hebron in the land of Judah, and the suburbs thereof round about it.
text.NABRE: From the half-tribe of Manasseh: Taanach with its pasture lands and Ibleam with its pasture lands. These belonged to the rest of the Kohathite clan.
text.DRB: They gave therefore to them Hebron in the Land of Juda, and the suburbs thereof round about:

text.NRSV: but the fields of the city and its villages they gave to Caleb son of Jephunneh.
text.Rotherham: but, the field-land of the city, and the villages thereof, gave they to Caleb son of Jephunneh.
text.KJV: But the fields of the city, and the villages thereof, they gave to Caleb the son of Jephunneh.
text.NABRE: The clans of the Gershonites received from the half-tribe of Manasseh: Golan in Bashan with its pasture lands and Ashtaroth with its pasture lands.
text.DRB: but the fields of the city, and the towns to Caleb the son of Jephone.

text.NRSV: To the sons of Aaron they gave the cities of refuge: Hebron, Libnah with its pasture lands, Jattir, Eshtemoa with its pasture lands,
text.Rotherham: And, to the sons of Aaron, gave they, the cities of refuge—Hebron, and Libnah, with her pasture lands,—and Jattir and Eshtemoa, with her pasture lands;
text.KJV: And to the sons of Aaron they gave the cities of Judah, namely, Hebron, the city of refuge, and Libnah with her suburbs, and Jattir, and Eshtemoa, with their suburbs,
text.NABRE: From the tribe of Issachar: Kedesh with its pasture lands, Daberath with its pasture lands,
text.DRB: Moreover to the sons of Aaron they gave cities, to fly unto, Hebron, and Lobna, and the suburbs thereof,

text.NRSV: Hilen with its pasture lands, Debir with its pasture lands,
text.Rotherham: and Hilen with her pasture lands, Debir, with her pasture lands;
text.KJV: And Hilen with her suburbs, Debir with her suburbs,
text.NABRE: Ramoth with its pasture lands, and Engannim with its pasture lands.
text.DRB: Jether also and Esthemo with the suburbs thereof, yea and Helon, and Dabir with their suburbs:

text.NRSV: Ashan with its pasture lands, and Beth-shemesh with its pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: and Ashan, with her pasture lands, and Bethshemesh, with her pasture lands;
text.KJV: And Ashan with her suburbs, and Beth–shemesh with her suburbs:
text.NABRE: From the tribe of Asher: Mashal with its pasture lands, Abdon with its pasture lands,
text.DRB: Asan also and Bethsemes, & their suburbs.

text.NRSV: From the tribe of Benjamin, Geba with its pasture lands, Alemeth with its pasture lands, and Anathoth with its pasture lands. All their towns throughout their families were thirteen.
text.Rotherham: and, out of the tribe of Benjamin, Geba, with her pasture lands, and Allemeth, with her pasture lands,—and Anathoth, with her pasture lands;—all their cities, were thirteen cities, throughout their families.
text.KJV: And out of the tribe of Benjamin; Geba with her suburbs, and Alemeth with her suburbs, and Anathoth with her suburbs. All their cities throughout their families were thirteen cities.
text.NABRE: Hilkath with its pasture lands, and Rehob with its pasture lands.
text.DRB: And of the tribe of Benjamin: Gabee and the suburbs thereof, Almath with the suburbs thereof, Anothoth also with the suburbs thereof. All the cities, thirteen, by their kindreds.

text.NRSV: To the rest of the Kohathites were given by lot out of the family of the tribe, out of the half-tribe, the half of Manasseh, ten towns.
text.Rotherham: And, unto the rest of the sons of Kohath—of the families of the tribe of Ephraim and of the tribe of Dan, and of the half-tribe of Manasseh, by lot ten cities.
text.KJV: And unto the sons of Kohath, which were left of the family of that tribe, were cities given out of the half tribe, namely, out of the half tribe of Manasseh, by lot, ten cities.
text.NABRE: From the tribe of Naphtali: Kedesh in Galilee with its pasture lands, Hammon with its pasture lands, and Kiriathaim with its pasture lands.
text.DRB: And to the children of Caath the residue of their kindred they gave of the half tribe of Manasses in possession ten cities.

text.NRSV: To the Gershomites according to their families were given thirteen towns out of the tribes of Issachar, Asher, Naphtali, and Manasseh in Bashan.
text.Rotherham: And, to the sons of Gershom, by their families—out of the tribe of Issachar, and out of the tribe of Asher, and of the tribe of Naphtali, and out of the tribe of Manasseh in Bashan, thirteen cities.
text.KJV: And to the sons of Gershom throughout their families out of the tribe of Issachar, and out of the tribe of Asher, and out of the tribe of Naphtali, and out of the tribe of Manasseh in Bashan, thirteen cities.
text.NABRE: The rest of the Merarites received from the tribe of Zebulun: Jokneam with its pasture lands, Kartah with its pasture lands, Rimmon with its pasture lands, and Tabor with its pasture lands.
text.DRB: Moreover to the children of Gerson by their kindreds of the tribe of Issachar, and of the tribe of Aser, and of the tribe of Nephthali, and of the tribe of Manasses in Basan, thirteen cities.

text.NRSV: To the Merarites according to their families were given by lot twelve towns out of the tribes of Reuben, Gad, and Zebulun.
text.Rotherham: And, unto sons of Merari, by their families—out of the tribe of Reuben, and out of the tribe of Gad, and out of the tribe of Zebulun, by lot twelve cities.
text.KJV: Unto the sons of Merari were given by lot, throughout their families, out of the tribe of Reuben, and out of the tribe of Gad, and out of the tribe of Zebulun, twelve cities.
text.NABRE: Across the Jordan at Jericho (that is, east of the Jordan) they received from the tribe of Reuben: Bezer in the desert with its pasture lands, Jahzah with its pasture lands,
text.DRB: And to the sons of Merari by their kindreds of the tribe of Ruben, and of the tribe of Gad, & of the tribe of Zabulon, they gave by lot twelve cities.

text.NRSV: So the people of Israel gave the Levites the towns with their pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: So then the sons of Israel gave, to the Levites,—the cities and the pasture lands thereof;
text.KJV: And the children of Israel gave to the Levites these cities with their suburbs.
text.NABRE: Kedemoth with its pasture lands, and Mephaath with its pasture lands.
text.DRB: Also the children of Israel gave to the Levites cities, and their suburbs:

text.NRSV: They also gave them by lot out of the tribes of Judah, Simeon, and Benjamin these towns that are mentioned by name.
text.Rotherham: yea they gave, by lot, out of the tribe of the sons of Judah, and out of the tribe of the sons of Simeon, and out of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin, these cities which are mentioned by name.
text.KJV: And they gave by lot out of the tribe of the children of Judah, and out of the tribe of the children of Simeon, and out of the tribe of the children of Benjamin, these cities, which are called by their names.
text.NABRE: From the tribe of Gad: Ramoth in Gilead with its pasture lands, Mahanaim with its pasture lands,
text.DRB: and they gave by lot, of the tribe of the children of Juda, and of the tribe of the children of Simeon, & of the tribe of the children of Benjamin, these cities, which they called by their names,

text.NRSV: And some of the families of the sons of Kohath had towns of their territory out of the tribe of Ephraim.
text.Rotherham: But, as for some of the families of the sons of Kohath, they had the cities of their lot, out of the tribe of Ephraim;
text.KJV: And the residue of the families of the sons of Kohath had cities of their coasts out of the tribe of Ephraim.
text.NABRE: Heshbon with its pasture lands, and Jazer with its pasture lands.
text.DRB: and to them, that were of the kindred of the sons of Caath, & the cities in their borders were of the tribe of Ephraim.

text.NRSV: They were given the cities of refuge: Shechem with its pasture lands in the hill country of Ephraim, Gezer with its pasture lands,
text.Rotherham: and they gave unto them the cities of refuge—Shechem and her pasture lands, in the hill country of Ephraim,—and Gezer, with her pasture lands;
text.KJV: And they gave unto them, of the cities of refuge, Shechem in mount Ephraim with her suburbs; they gave also Gezer with her suburbs,
text.DRB: They therefore gave them cities, to fly unto, Sichem with the suburbs thereof in mount Ephraim, and Gazer with the suburbs thereof,

text.NRSV: Jokmeam with its pasture lands, Beth-horon with its pasture lands,
text.Rotherham: and Jokmeam, with her pasture lands, and Beth-heron, with her pasture lands;
text.KJV: And Jokmeam with her suburbs, and Beth–horon with her suburbs,
text.DRB: Jecmaan also with the suburbs thereof, & Bethhoron in like manner,

text.NRSV: Aijalon with its pasture lands, Gath-rimmon with its pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: and Aijalon, with her pasture lands, and Gath-rimmon, with her pasture lands;
text.KJV: And Aijalon with her suburbs, and Gath–rimmon with her suburbs:
text.DRB: moreover Helon also with the suburbs thereof, and Gethremmon in like manner.

text.NRSV: And out of the half-tribe of Manasseh, Aner with its pasture lands, and Bileam with its pasture lands, for the rest of the families of the Kohathites.
text.Rotherham: and, out of the half-tribe of Manasseh, Aner, with her pasture lands, and Bileam, with her pasture lands,—for the rest of the family of the sons of Kohath.
text.KJV: And out of the half tribe of Manasseh; Aner with her suburbs, and Bileam with her suburbs, for the family of the remnant of the sons of Kohath.
text.DRB: Moreover of the half tribe of Manasses Aner & the suburbs thereof, Balaam & the suburbs thereof: to wit, to them, which were left of the kindred of the sons of Caath.

text.NRSV: To the Gershomites: out of the half-tribe of Manasseh: Golan in Bashan with its pasture lands and Ashtaroth with its pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: Unto the sons of Gershom—out of the family of the half tribe of Manasseh, Golan in Bashan, with her pasture lands,—and Ashtaroth, with her pasture lands.
text.KJV: Unto the sons of Gershom were given out of the family of the half tribe of Manasseh, Golan in Bashan with her suburbs, and Ashtaroth with her suburbs:
text.DRB: And to the sons of Gerson of the kindred of the half tribe of Manasses, Gaulon in Basan, & the suburbs thereof, & Astaroth with the suburbs thereof.

text.NRSV: Out of the tribe of Issachar: Kedesh with its pasture lands, Daberath with its pasture lands,
text.Rotherham: And, out of the tribe of Issachar, Kedesh, with her pasture lands,—Daberath, with her pasture lands;
text.KJV: And out of the tribe of Issachar; Kedesh with her suburbs, Daberath with her suburbs,
text.DRB: Of the tribe of Issachar, Cedes & the suburbs thereof, & Dabereth with the suburbs thereof,

text.NRSV: Ramoth with its pasture lands, and Anem with its pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: and Ramoth, with her pasture lands, and Anem, with her pasture lands.
text.KJV: And Ramoth with her suburbs, and Anem with her suburbs:
text.DRB: Ramoth also & the suburbs thereof, & Anem with the suburbs thereof.

text.NRSV: Out of the tribe of Asher: Mashal with its pasture lands, Abdon with its pasture lands,
text.Rotherham: And, out of the tribe of Asher, Mashal, with her pasture lands,—and Abdon, with her pasture lands;
text.KJV: And out of the tribe of Asher; Mashal with her suburbs, and Abdon with her suburbs,
text.DRB: And of the tribe of Aser: Masal with the suburbs thereof, and Abdon in like manner,

text.NRSV: Hukok with its pasture lands, and Rehob with its pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: and Hukok, with her pasture lands,—and Rehob, with her pasture lands.
text.KJV: And Hukok with her suburbs, and Rehob with her suburbs:
text.DRB: Hucac also and the suburbs thereof, & Rohob with the suburbs thereof.

text.NRSV: Out of the tribe of Naphtali: Kedesh in Galilee with its pasture lands, Hammon with its pasture lands, and Kiriathaim with its pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: And, out of the tribe of Naphtali, Kedesh in Galilee, with her pasture lands, and Hammon, with her pasture lands,—and Kiryathaim, with her pasture lands.
text.KJV: And out of the tribe of Naphtali; Kedesh in Galilee with her suburbs, and Hammon with her suburbs, and Kirjathaim with her suburbs.
text.DRB: Moreover of the tribe of Nephthali, Cedes in Galilee and the suburbs thereof, Hamon with the suburbs thereof, and Cariathaim, and the suburbs thereof.

text.NRSV: To the rest of the Merarites: out of the tribe of Zebulun: Rimmono with its pasture lands, Tabor with its pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: Unto the rest of the sons of Merari—out of the tribe of Zebulun, Rimmono, with her pasture lands,—Tabor, with her pasture lands.
text.KJV: Unto the rest of the children of Merari were given out of the tribe of Zebulun, Rimmon with her suburbs, Tabor with her suburbs:
text.DRB: And to the rest of the sons of Merari: of the tribe of Zabulon Remmono and the suburbs thereof, and Thabor with the suburbs thereof:

text.NRSV: Across the Jordan from Jericho, on the east side of the Jordan, out of the tribe of Reuben: Bezer in the steppe with its pasture lands, Jahzah with its pasture lands,
text.Rotherham: And, beyond the Jordan at Jericho, on the east of the Jordan—out of the tribe of Reuben, Bezer in the wilderness, with her pasture lands,—and Jahzah, with her pasture lands;
text.KJV: And on the other side Jordan by Jericho, on the east side of Jordan, were given them out of the tribe of Reuben, Bezer in the wilderness with her suburbs, and Jahzah with her suburbs,
text.DRB: beyond Jordan also over against Jericho, against the East of Jordan, of the tribe of Ruben, Bosor in the wilderness with the suburbs thereof, and Jassa with the suburbs thereof,

text.NRSV: Kedemoth with its pasture lands, and Mephaath with its pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: and Kedemoth, with her pasture lands, and Mephaath, with her pasture lands.
text.KJV: Kedemoth also with her suburbs, and Mephaath with her suburbs:
text.DRB: Cademoth also and the suburbs thereof, and Mephaath with the suburbs thereof.

text.NRSV: And out of the tribe of Gad: Ramoth in Gilead with its pasture lands, Mahanaim with its pasture lands,
text.Rotherham: And, out of the tribe of Gad, Ramoth in Gilead, with her pasture lands,—and Mahanaim, with her pasture lands;
text.KJV: And out of the tribe of Gad; Ramoth in Gilead with her suburbs, and Mahanaim with her suburbs,
text.DRB: Moreover also of the tribe of Gad, Ramoth in Galaad and the suburbs thereof, and Manaim with the suburbs thereof,

text.NRSV: Heshbon with its pasture lands, and Jazer with its pasture lands.
text.Rotherham: and Heshbon, with her pasture lands, and Jazer, with her pasture lands.
text.KJV: And Heshbon with her suburbs, and Jazer with her suburbs.
text.DRB: Yea and Hesebon with the suburbs thereof, and Jezer with the suburbs thereof.

text.NRSV: The sons of Issachar: Tola, Puah, Jashub, and Shimron, four.
text.Rotherham: And, to the sons of Issachar, belonged, Tola and Push, Jashib and Shimron, four.
text.KJV: Now the sons of Issachar were, Tola, and Puah, Jashub, and Shimron, four.
text.NABRE: Chapter 7 Issachar. The sons of Issachar were Tola, Puah, Jashub, and Shimron: four.
text.DRB: Moreover the sons of Issachar: Thola, and Phua, Jasub & Simeron, four,

text.NRSV: The sons of Tola: Uzzi, Rephaiah, Jeriel, Jahmai, Ibsam, and Shemuel, heads of their ancestral houses, namely of Tola, mighty warriors of their generations, their number in the days of David being twenty-two thousand six hundred.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Tola, were Uzzi and Rephaiah and Jeriel and Jahmai and Ibsam and Shemuel, chiefs of their ancestral house, pertaining to Tola, heroes of valour, in their generations,—their number, in the days of David, twenty-two thousand and six hundred.
text.KJV: And the sons of Tola; Uzzi, and Rephaiah, and Jeriel, and Jahmai, and Jibsam, and Shemuel, heads of their father’s house, to wit, of Tola: they were valiant men of might in their generations; whose number was in the days of David two and twenty thousand and six hundred.
text.NABRE: The sons of Tola were Uzzi, Rephaiah, Jeriel, Jahmai, Ibsam, and Shemuel, heads of the ancestral houses of Tola, mighty warriors in their generations. In the time of David they numbered twenty-two thousand six hundred.
text.DRB: The sons of Thola: Ozi and Raphaia, and Jeriel, and Jemai, and Jebsem, and Samuel. Princes by the houses of their kindreds. Of the stock of Thola were numbered most valiant men in the days of David, two and twenty thousand six hundred.

text.NRSV: The son of Uzzi: Izrahiah. And the sons of Izrahiah: Michael, Obadiah, Joel, and Isshiah, five, all of them chiefs;
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Uzzi, Izrahiah,—and, the sons of Izrahiah, Michael and Obadiah and Joel, Isshiah—five, chiefs, all of them;
text.KJV: And the sons of Uzzi; Izrahiah: and the sons of Izrahiah; Michael, and Obadiah, and Joel, Ishiah, five: all of them chief men.
text.NABRE: The sons of Uzzi: Izarahiah. The sons of Izarahiah were Michael, Obadiah, Joel, and Isshiah. All five of these were chiefs.
text.DRB: The sons of Ozi: Izrahia, of whom were born Michael, and Obadia, and Joel, and Jesia, five, all Princes.

text.NRSV: and along with them, by their generations, according to their ancestral houses, were units of the fighting force, thirty-six thousand, for they had many wives and sons.
text.Rotherham: and, with them by their generations, pertaining to their ancestral house, were bands of a host for war, thirty-six thousand,—for they had many wives and sons;
text.KJV: And with them, by their generations, after the house of their fathers, were bands of soldiers for war, six and thirty thousand men: for they had many wives and sons.
text.NABRE: Along with them, in their generations, according to ancestral houses, were thirty-six thousand men in organized military troops, since they had more wives and children
text.DRB: And with them by their families and peoples, ready girded to battle, most valiant men, six and thirty thousand: for they had many wives, and children.

text.NRSV: Their kindred belonging to all the families of Issachar were in all eighty-seven thousand mighty warriors, enrolled by genealogy.
text.Rotherham: and, their brethren, of all the families of Issachar, heroes of great valour, were eighty-seven thousand, when they had, all, registered themselves.
text.KJV: And their brethren among all the families of Issachar were valiant men of might, reckoned in all by their genealogies fourscore and seven thousand.
text.NABRE: than their fellow tribesmen. In all the clans of Issachar there was a total of eighty-seven thousand warriors listed in their family records.
text.DRB: Their brethren also through all the kindred of Issachar, most strong to fight, were numbered four score and seven thousand.

text.NRSV: The sons of Benjamin: Bela, Becher, and Jediael, three.
text.Rotherham: Benjamin, Bela and Becher and Jediael, three.
text.KJV: The sons of Benjamin; Bela, and Becher, and Jediael, three.
text.NABRE: Benjamin. The sons of Benjamin were Bela, Becher, and Jediael—three.
text.DRB: The sons of Benjamin: Bela, and Bechor, and Jadihel, three.

text.NRSV: The sons of Bela: Ezbon, Uzzi, Uzziel, Jerimoth, and Iri, five, heads of ancestral houses, mighty warriors; and their enrollment by genealogy was twenty-two thousand thirty-four.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Bela, Ezbon and Uzzi and Uzziel and Jerimoth and Iri, five, chiefs of the ancestral house, heroes of great valour,—who, when they had registered themselves, were twenty-two thousand and thirty-four,
text.KJV: And the sons of Bela; Ezbon, and Uzzi, and Uzziel, and Jerimoth, and Iri, five; heads of the house of their fathers, mighty men of valour; and were reckoned by their genealogies twenty and two thousand and thirty and four.
text.NABRE: The sons of Bela were Ezbon, Uzzi, Uzziel, Jerimoth, and Iri—five. They were heads of their ancestral houses and warriors. Their family records listed twenty-two thousand and thirty-four.
text.DRB: The sons of Bela: Esbon, and Ozi, and Oziel, and Jerimoth, and Urai, five Princes of their families, and most strong to fight, and their number was twenty two thousand and thirty four.

text.NRSV: The sons of Becher: Zemirah, Joash, Eliezer, Elioenai, Omri, Jeremoth, Abijah, Anathoth, and Alemeth. All these were the sons of Becher.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Becher, Zemirah and Joash and Eliezer and Elioenai and Omri, and Jeremoth and Abijah, and Anathoth, and Alemeth. All these, were sons of Becher.
text.KJV: And the sons of Becher; Zemira, and Joash, and Eliezer, and Elioenai, and Omri, and Jerimoth, and Abiah, and Anathoth, and Alameth. All these are the sons of Becher.
text.NABRE: The sons of Becher were Zemirah, Joash, Eliezer, Elioenai, Omri, Jeremoth, Abijah, Anathoth, and Alemeth—all these were sons of Becher.
text.DRB: Moreover the sons of Bechor: Zamira, and Joas, and Eliezer, and Elioenai, and Amri, and Jerimoth, and Abia, and Anathoth, and Almath. All these the sons of Bechor.

text.NRSV: Their enrollment by genealogy, according to their generations, as heads of their ancestral houses, mighty warriors, was twenty thousand two hundred.
text.Rotherham: And, when they had registered themselves by their generations, the chiefs of their ancestral house, the heroes of valour, were twenty thousand and two hundred.
text.KJV: And the number of them, after their genealogy by their generations, heads of the house of their fathers, mighty men of valour, was twenty thousand and two hundred.
text.NABRE: Their family records listed twenty thousand two hundred of their kindred who were heads of their ancestral houses and warriors.
text.DRB: And there were numbered by their families Princes of their kindreds most valiant unto battle, twenty thousand and two hundred.

text.NRSV: The son of Jediael: Bilhan. And the sons of Bilhan: Jeush, Benjamin, Ehud, Chenaanah, Zethan, Tarshish, and Ahishahar.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Jediael, Bilhan,—and, the sons of Bilhan, Jeish and Benjamin and Ehud and Chenaanah, and Zethan, and Tarshish, and Ahishahah.
text.KJV: The sons also of Jediael; Bilhan: and the sons of Bilhan; Jeush, and Benjamin, and Ehud, and Chenaanah, and Zethan, and Tharshish, and Ahishahar.
text.NABRE: The sons of Jediael: Bilhan. The sons of Bilhan were Jeush, Benjamin, Ehud, Chenaanah, Zethan, Tarshish, and Ahishahar.
text.DRB: Moreover the sons of Jadihel: Balan. And the sons of Balan: Jehus, and Benjamin, and Aod, and Chanana, and Zethan, and Tharsis, Ahisahar.

text.NRSV: All these were the sons of Jediael, according to the heads of their ancestral houses, mighty warriors, seventeen thousand two hundred, ready for service in war.
text.Rotherham: All these—sons of Jediael, by ancestral chiefs, heroes of great valour, were seventeen thousand and two hundred, ready to go forth as a host to war:—
text.KJV: All these the sons of Jediael, by the heads of their fathers, mighty men of valour, were seventeen thousand and two hundred soldiers, fit to go out for war and battle.
text.NABRE: All these were sons of Jediael, heads of ancestral houses and warriors. They numbered seventeen thousand two hundred men fit for military service…
text.DRB: All these the sons of Jadihel, Princes of their kindreds, most valiant men, seventeen thousand, & two hundred going forth to battle.

text.NRSV: And Shuppim and Huppim were the sons of Ir, Hushim the son of Aher.
text.Rotherham: Shuppim also and Huppim, sons of Ir, Hushim sons of Aher.
text.KJV: Shuppim also, and Huppim, the children of Ir, and Hushim, the sons of Aher.
text.NABRE: Shupham and Hupham. Dan, Naphtali and Manasseh. The sons of Dan: Hushim.
text.DRB: Sepham also, & Hapham, the sons of Hir: and Hasim the sons of Aher.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Naphtali: Jahziel, Guni, Jezer, and Shallum, the descendants of Bilhah.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Naphtali, Jahziel and Guni, and Jezer and Shallum, sons of Bilhah.
text.KJV: The sons of Naphtali; Jahziel, and Guni, and Jezer, and Shallum, the sons of Bilhah.
text.NABRE: The sons of Naphtali were Jahziel, Guni, Jezer, and Shallum. These were sons of Bilhah.
text.DRB: And the sons of Nephthali: Jasiel, and Guni, and Jezer, and Sellum, the sons of Bala.

text.NRSV: The sons of Manasseh: Asriel, whom his Aramean concubine bore; she bore Machir the father of Gilead.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Manasseh, Asriel, whom, [his wife] bare,—his concubine, the Syrian, bare Machir the father of Gilead;
text.KJV: The sons of Manasseh; Ashriel, whom she bare: (but his concubine the Aramitess bare Machir the father of Gilead:
text.NABRE: The sons of Manasseh, whom his Aramean concubine bore: she bore Machir, the father of Gilead.
text.DRB: Moreover the son of Manasses, Ezriel: & his concubine the Syrian bare Machir the father of Galaad.

text.NRSV: And Machir took a wife for Huppim and for Shuppim. The name of his sister was Maacah. And the name of the second was Zelophehad; and Zelophehad had daughters.
text.Rotherham: and, Machir, took a wife pertaining to Huppim and Shuppim, and, the name of his sister, was Maacah, and, the name of the second, Zelophehad,—and, Zelophehad, had, daughters.
text.KJV: And Machir took to wife the sister of Huppim and Shuppim, whose sister’s name was Maachah;) and the name of the second was Zelophehad: and Zelophehad had daughters.
text.NABRE: Machir took a wife whose name was Maacah; his sister’s name was Molecheth. Manasseh’s second son was named Zelophehad, who had only daughters.
text.DRB: And Machir took wives for his sons Happhim, & Saphan: & he had a sister named Maacha: the name of the second Salphaad, and to Salphaad were born daughters.

text.NRSV: Maacah the wife of Machir bore a son, and she named him Peresh; the name of his brother was Sheresh; and his sons were Ulam and Rakem.
text.Rotherham: So then Maacah, wife of Machir, bare a son, and called his name Peresh, and, the name of his brother, was Sheresh,—and, his sons, were Ulam and Rakem;
text.KJV: And Maachah the wife of Machir bare a son, and she called his name Peresh; and the name of his brother was Sheresh; and his sons were Ulam and Rakem.
text.NABRE: Maacah, Machir’s wife, bore a son whom she named Peresh. He had a brother named Sheresh, whose sons were Ulam and Rakem.
text.DRB: And Maacha the wife of Machir bare a son, and she called his name Phares: moreover the name of his brother, was Sares: and his sons, Ulam and Recen.

text.NRSV: The son of Ulam: Bedan. These were the sons of Gilead son of Machir, son of Manasseh.
text.Rotherham: and, the sons of Ulam, Bedan. These, were the sons of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh.
text.KJV: And the sons of Ulam; Bedan. These were the sons of Gilead, the son of Machir, the son of Manasseh.
text.NABRE: The sons of Ulam: Bedan. These were the sons of Gilead, the son of Machir, the son of Manasseh.
text.DRB: And the son of Ulam, Badan. These are the children of Galaad, the son of Machir, the son of Manasses.

text.NRSV: And his sister Hammolecheth bore Ishhod, Abiezer, and Mahlah.
text.Rotherham: And, his sister, Hammolecheth, bare Ishhod, and Abiezer, and Mahlah.
text.KJV: And his sister Hammoleketh bare Ishod, and Abiezer, and Mahalah.
text.NABRE: His sister Molecheth bore Ishhod, Abiezer, and Mahlah.
text.DRB: And his sister Queen bare Goodlieman, & Abiezer, & Mohola.

text.NRSV: The sons of Shemida were Ahian, Shechem, Likhi, and Aniam.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Shemida, were Ahian and Shechem, and Likhi, and Aniam.
text.KJV: And the sons of Shemida were, Ahian, and Shechem, and Likhi, and Aniam.
text.NABRE: The sons of Shemida were Ahian, Shechem, Likhi, and Aniam.
text.DRB: And the sons of Semida were, Ahin, & Sechem, and Leci, & Anian.

text.NRSV: The sons of Ephraim: Shuthelah, and Bered his son, Tahath his son, Eleadah his son, Tahath his son,
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Ephraim, Shuthelah,—and Bered his son, and Tahath his son, and Eleadah his son, and Tahath his son;
text.KJV: And the sons of Ephraim; Shuthelah, and Bered his son, and Tahath his son, and Eladah his son, and Tahath his son,
text.NABRE: Ephraim. The sons of Ephraim: Shuthelah, whose son was Bered, whose son was Tahath, whose son was Eleadah, whose son was Tahath,
text.DRB: And the sons of Ephraim: Suthala, Bared his son, Thahath his son, Elada his son, Thahath his son, and this man's son Zabad,

text.NRSV: Zabad his son, Shuthelah his son, and Ezer and Elead. Now the people of Gath, who were born in the land, killed them, because they came down to raid their livestock.
text.Rotherham: and Zabad his son and Shuthelah his son, and Ezer and Elead,—but the men of Gath who had been born in the land slew them, because they had come down to take away their cattle.
text.KJV: And Zabad his son, and Shuthelah his son, and Ezer, and Elead, whom the men of Gath that were born in that land slew, because they came down to take away their cattle.
text.NABRE: whose son was Zabad. Ephraim’s son Shuthelah, and Ezer and Elead, who were born in the land, were killed by the inhabitants of Gath because they had gone down to take away their livestock.
text.DRB: and this man's son Suthala, and this man's son Ezer, and Elad: and the men of Geth born in the land slew them, because they came down to invade their possessions.

text.NRSV: And their father Ephraim mourned many days, and his kindred came to comfort him.
text.Rotherham: And Ephraim their father mourned many days,—and his brethren came to comfort him.
text.KJV: And Ephraim their father mourned many days, and his brethren came to comfort him.
text.NABRE: Their father Ephraim mourned a long time, but after his relatives had come and comforted him,
