text.DRB: Ephraim therefore their father mourned many days, and his brethren came to comfort him.

text.NRSV: Ephraim went in to his wife, and she conceived and bore a son; and he named him Beriah, because disaster had befallen his house.
text.Rotherham: And he went in unto his wife, and she conceived, and bare a son,—and he called his name, Beriah, because, in misfortune, was she in his house.
text.KJV: And when he went in to his wife, she conceived, and bare a son, and he called his name Beriah, because it went evil with his house.
text.NABRE: he had relations with his wife, who conceived and bore a son whom he named Beriah, since evil had befallen his house.
text.DRB: And he went in unto his wife: who conceived and bare a son, and he called his name Beria, for that he was born in the evils of his house:

text.NRSV: His daughter was Sheerah, who built both Lower and Upper Beth-horon, and Uzzen-sheerah.
text.Rotherham: And, his daughter, was Sheerah, who built Beth-horon the nether and the upper,—and Uzzen-sheerah.
text.KJV: (And his daughter was Sherah, who built Beth–horon the nether, and the upper, and Uzzen–sherah.)
text.NABRE: He had a daughter, Sheerah, who built Lower and Upper Beth-horon and Uzzen-sheerah.
text.DRB: and his daughter was Sara, who built Bethhoron, the nether and the upper, and Ozensara.

text.NRSV: Rephah was his son, Resheph his son, Telah his son, Tahan his son,
text.Rotherham: And Rephah his son, and Resheph, and Telah his son, and Tahan his son,—
text.KJV: And Rephah was his son, also Resheph, and Telah his son, and Tahan his son,
text.NABRE: Zabad’s son was Rephah, whose son was Resheph, whose son was Telah, whose son was Tahan,
text.DRB: Moreover his son Rapha, and Reseph, and Thale, of whom was born Thaan,

text.NRSV: Ladan his son, Ammihud his son, Elishama his son,
text.Rotherham: Ladan his son, Ammihud his son, Elishama his son,—
text.KJV: Laadan his son, Ammihud his son, Elishama his son,
text.NABRE: whose son was Ladan, whose son was Ammihud, whose son was Elishama,
text.DRB: who begat Laadan: this man's son also was Ammiud, who begat Elisama,

text.NRSV: Nun his son, Joshua his son.
text.Rotherham: Non his son, Joshua his son.
text.KJV: Non his son, Jehoshua his son.
text.NABRE: whose son was Nun, whose son was Joshua.
text.DRB: of whom was born Nun, who had Josue his son.

text.NRSV: Their possessions and settlements were Bethel and its towns, and to the east Naaran, and to the west Gezer and its towns, Shechem and its towns, as far as Ayyah and its towns;
text.Rotherham: And, their possessions, and their dwellings, were Bethel, and the villages thereof; and, eastward, Naaran, and, westward, Gezer, and the villages thereof, and Shechem, and the villages thereof,—as far as Aiyah, and the villages thereof;—
text.KJV: And their possessions and habitations were, Beth–el and the towns thereof, and eastward Naaran, and westward Gezer, with the towns thereof; Shechem also and the towns thereof, unto Gaza and the towns thereof:
text.NABRE: Their property and their dwellings were in Bethel and its towns, Naaran to the east, Gezer and its towns to the west, and also Shechem and its towns as far as Ayyah and its towns.
text.DRB: And their possession and habitation was Bethel with her daughters, and against the east of Noran, and on the west quarter of Gazer and her daughters, Sichem also with her daughters, as far as Asa with her daughters.

text.NRSV: also along the borders of the Manassites, Beth-shean and its towns, Taanach and its towns, Megiddo and its towns, Dor and its towns. In these lived the sons of Joseph son of Israel.
text.Rotherham: and, on the sides of the sons of Manasseh, Beth-shean and the villages thereof, Taanach and the villages thereof, Megiddo and the villages thereof, Dor and the villages thereof. In these, dwelt the sons of Joseph, son of Israel.
text.KJV: And by the borders of the children of Manasseh, Beth–shean and her towns, Taanach and her towns, Megiddo and her towns, Dor and her towns. In these dwelt the children of Joseph the son of Israel.
text.NABRE: Manasseh, however, had possession of Beth-shean and its towns, Taanach and its towns, Megiddo and its towns, and Dor and its towns. In these dwelt the sons of Joseph, the son of Israel.
text.DRB: Also near the children of Manasses Bethsan & her daughters, Thanach and her daughters, Mageddo and her daughters: Dor & her daughters. In these dwelt the children of Joseph, the son of Israel.

text.NRSV: The sons of Asher: Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi, Beriah, and their sister Serah.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Asher, Imnah, and Ishvah, and Ishvi and Beriah,—and Serah their sister.
text.KJV: The sons of Asher; Imnah, and Isuah, and Ishuai, and Beriah, and Serah their sister.
text.NABRE: Asher. The sons of Asher were Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi, and Beriah; their sister was Serah.
text.DRB: The children of Aser: Jemna, and Jesua, and Jessui, and Baria, and Sara their sister.

text.NRSV: The sons of Beriah: Heber and Malchiel, who was the father of Birzaith.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Beriah, Heber, and Malchiel,—the same, was the father of Birzaith.
text.KJV: And the sons of Beriah; Heber, and Malchiel, who is the father of Birzavith.
text.NABRE: Beriah’s sons were Heber and Malchiel, who was the father of Birzaith.
text.DRB: And the sons of Baria: Heber, & Melchiel: he is the father of Barsaith.

text.NRSV: Heber became the father of Japhlet, Shomer, Hotham, and their sister Shua.
text.Rotherham: And, Heber, begat Japhlet, and Shomer, and Hotham,—and, Shua, their sister.
text.KJV: And Heber begat Japhlet, and Shomer, and Hotham, and Shua their sister.
text.NABRE: Heber became the father of Japhlet, Shomer, Hotham, and their sister Shua.
text.DRB: And Heber begat Jephlat, & Somer, and Hotham, & Suaa their sister.

text.NRSV: The sons of Japhlet: Pasach, Bimhal, and Ashvath. These are the sons of Japhlet.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Japhlet, Pasach, and Bimhal, and Ashvath. These, were the sons of Japhlet.
text.KJV: And the sons of Japhlet; Pasach, and Bimhal, and Ashvath. These are the children of Japhlet.
text.NABRE: The sons of Japhlet were Pasach, Bimhal, and Ashvath; these were the sons of Japhlet.
text.DRB: The son of Jephlat: Phosech, & Chamaal, and Asoth: these be the sons of Jephlat.

text.NRSV: The sons of Shemer: Ahi, Rohgah, Hubbah, and Aram.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Shemer, Ahi, and Rohgah, Jahbah, and Aram.
text.KJV: And the sons of Shamer; Ahi, and Rohgah, Jehubbah, and Aram.
text.NABRE: The sons of Shomer were Ahi, Rohgah, Jehubbah, and Aram.
text.DRB: Moreover the sons of Somer: Ahi, and Roaga, and Haba, and Aram.

text.NRSV: The sons of Helem his brother: Zophah, Imna, Shelesh, and Amal.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Helem, his brother, Zophah, and Imna, and Shelesh and Amal.
text.KJV: And the sons of his brother Helem; Zophah, and Imna, and Shelesh, and Amal.
text.NABRE: The sons of his brother Hotham were Zophah, Imna, Shelesh, and Amal.
text.DRB: and the sons of Helem his brother: Supha, and Jemna, and Selles, and Amal.

text.NRSV: The sons of Zophah: Suah, Harnepher, Shual, Beri, Imrah,
text.Rotherham: The sons of Zophah, Suah and Harnepher, and Shual and Beri, and Imrah,—
text.KJV: The sons of Zophah; Suah, and Harnepher, and Shual, and Beri, and Imrah,
text.NABRE: The sons of Zophah were Suah, Harnepher, Shual, Beri, Imrah,
text.DRB: The sons of Supha: Sue, Harnapher, and Sual, and Beri, and Jamra,

text.NRSV: Bezer, Hod, Shamma, Shilshah, Ithran, and Beera.
text.Rotherham: Bezer and Hod, and Shamma and Shilshah, and Ithran and Beera.
text.KJV: Bezer, and Hod, and Shamma, and Shilshah, and Ithran, and Beera.
text.NABRE: Bezer, Hod, Shamma, Shilshah, Ithran, and Beera.
text.DRB: Bosor, and Hod, & Samma, Salusa, & Jethran, & Bera.

text.NRSV: The sons of Jether: Jephunneh, Pispa, and Ara.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Jether, Jephunneh, and Pispa, and Ara.
text.KJV: And the sons of Jether; Jephunneh, and Pispah, and Ara.
text.NABRE: The sons of Jether were Jephunneh, Pispa, and Ara.
text.DRB: The sons of Jether: Jephone, and Phaspha, and Ara.

text.NRSV: The sons of Ulla: Arah, Hanniel, and Rizia.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Ulla, Arah, and Hanniel, and Rizia.
text.KJV: And the sons of Ulla; Arah, and Haniel, and Rezia.
text.NABRE: The sons of Ulla were Arah, Hanniel, and Rizia.
text.DRB: And the sons of Olla: Aree, and Haniel and Resia.

text.NRSV: All these were the sons of Asher, heads of ancestral houses, select mighty warriors, chief of the princes. Their number enrolled by genealogy, for service in war, was twenty-six thousand men.
text.Rotherham: All these, were sons of Asher, chiefs of the ancestral house, choice men, heroes of great valour, chiefs of the leaders,—and, when they registered themselves, in host, for war, the number of the men, was twenty-six thousand.
text.KJV: All these were the children of Asher, heads of their father’s house, choice and mighty men of valour, chief of the princes. And the number throughout the genealogy of them that were apt to the war and to battle was twenty and six thousand men.
text.NABRE: All these were sons of Asher, heads of ancestral houses, distinguished men, warriors, and chiefs among the princes. Their family records numbered twenty-six thousand men fit for military service.
text.DRB: All these be the sons of Aser, Princes of their kindreds, the chosen and most valiant dukes of dukes: and their number of the age that was fit for battle, was six and twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: Benjamin became the father of Bela his firstborn, Ashbel the second, Aharah the third,
text.Rotherham: And, Benjamin, begat Bela his firstborn,—Ashbel, the second, and Aharah, the third,—
text.KJV: Now Benjamin begat Bela his firstborn, Ashbel the second, and Aharah the third,
text.NABRE: Chapter 8 Benjamin. Benjamin became the father of Bela, his firstborn, Ashbel, the second son, Aharah, the third,
text.DRB: And Benjamin begat Bale his first begotten, Asbel the second, Ahara the third,

text.NRSV: Nohah the fourth, and Rapha the fifth.
text.Rotherham: Nohah, the fourth, and Rapha, the fifth.
text.KJV: Nohah the fourth, and Rapha the fifth.
text.NABRE: Nohah, the fourth, and Rapha, the fifth.
text.DRB: Nohaa the fourth, and Rapha the fifth.

text.NRSV: And Bela had sons: Addar, Gera, Abihud,
text.Rotherham: And Bela had sons—Addar, and Gera, and Abihud,—
text.KJV: And the sons of Bela were, Addar, and Gera, and Abihud,
text.NABRE: The sons of Bela were Addar and Gera, the father of Ehud.
text.DRB: And the sons of Bale were: Addar, and Gera, and Abiud,

text.NRSV: Abishua, Naaman, Ahoah,
text.Rotherham: and Abishua, and Naaman, and Ahoah,—
text.KJV: And Abishua, and Naaman, and Ahoah,
text.NABRE: The sons of Ehud were Abishua, Naaman, Ahoah,
text.DRB: Abisue also, and Naaman, and Ahoe,

text.NRSV: Gera, Shephuphan, and Huram.
text.Rotherham: and Gera, and Shephuphan, and Huram.
text.KJV: And Gera, and Shephuphan, and Huram.
text.NABRE: Gera, Shephuphan, and Huram.
text.DRB: and also Gera, and Sephuphan, and Huram.

text.NRSV: These are the sons of Ehud (they were heads of ancestral houses of the inhabitants of Geba, and they were carried into exile to Manahath):
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the sons of Ehud,—these, are the ancestral chiefs to the inhabitants of Geba, but they were carried away captive, unto Manahath;
text.KJV: And these are the sons of Ehud: these are the heads of the fathers of the inhabitants of Geba, and they removed them to Manahath:
text.NABRE: These were the sons of Ehud, family heads over those who dwelt in Geba and were deported to Manahath.
text.DRB: These are the sons of Ahod, Princes of their kindreds that dwelt in Gabaa, which were transported into Manahath.

text.NRSV: Naaman, Ahijah, and Gera, that is, Heglam, who became the father of Uzza and Ahihud.
text.Rotherham: and, as for Naaman and Ahijah and Gera, the same, carried them captive,—and he begat Uzza and Ahihud.
text.KJV: And Naaman, and Ahiah, and Gera, he removed them, and begat Uzza, and Ahihud.
text.NABRE: Also Naaman, Ahijah, and Gera. The last, who led them into exile, became the father of Uzza and Ahihud.
text.DRB: And Naaman, and Achia, and Gera he transported them, and begat Oza, and Ahiud.

text.NRSV: And Shaharaim had sons in the country of Moab after he had sent away his wives Hushim and Baara.
text.Rotherham: And, Shaharaim, begat children in the field of Moab, after he had sent away Hushim and Baara his wives.
text.KJV: And Shaharaim begat children in the country of Moab, after he had sent them away; Hushim and Baara were his wives.
text.NABRE: Shaharaim became a father on the Moabite plateau after he had put away his wives Hushim and Baara.
text.DRB: Moreover he begat Saharaim in the country of Moab, after he dismissed Husim and Bara his wives.

text.NRSV: He had sons by his wife Hodesh: Jobab, Zibia, Mesha, Malcam,
text.Rotherham: Then begat he, of Hodesh his wife,—Jobab, and Zibia, and Mesha, and Malcam;
text.KJV: And he begat of Hodesh his wife, Jobab, and Zibia, and Mesha, and Malcham,
text.NABRE: By his wife Hodesh he begot Jobab, Zibia, Mesha, Malcam,
text.DRB: And of Hodes his wife he begat Jobab, and Sebia, and Mosa, and Molchom,

text.NRSV: Jeuz, Sachia, and Mirmah. These were his sons, heads of ancestral houses.
text.Rotherham: and Jeuz, and Sachia, and Mirmah,—these, were his sons, ancestral chiefs.
text.KJV: And Jeuz, and Shachia, and Mirma. These were his sons, heads of the fathers.
text.NABRE: Jeuz, Sachia, and Mirmah. These were his sons, family heads.
text.DRB: Jehus also, and Sechia, and Marma. These are his sons Princes in their families.

text.NRSV: He also had sons by Hushim: Abitub and Elpaal.
text.Rotherham: And, of Hushim, he begat Abitub, and Elpaal.
text.KJV: And of Hushim he begat Abitub, and Elpaal.
text.NABRE: By Hushim he begot Abitub and Elpaal.
text.DRB: And Mehusim begat Abitob, and Elphaal.

text.NRSV: The sons of Elpaal: Eber, Misham, and Shemed, who built Ono and Lod with its towns,
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Elpaal, Eber, and Misham, and Shemed,—he, built Ono, and Lod, and the villages thereof.
text.KJV: The sons of Elpaal; Eber, and Misham, and Shamed, who built Ono, and Lod, with the towns thereof:
text.NABRE: The sons of Elpaal were Eber, Misham, Shemed (who built Ono and Lod with its nearby towns),
text.DRB: Moreover the sons of Elphaal Heber, and Misaam, and Samad: this man built Ono, and Lod, and her daughters.

text.NRSV: and Beriah and Shema (they were heads of ancestral houses of the inhabitants of Aijalon, who put to flight the inhabitants of Gath);
text.Rotherham: And, as for Beriah and Shema, they, were ancestral chiefs to the inhabitants of Aijalon,—they, put to flight the inhabitants of Gath;
text.KJV: Beriah also, and Shema, who were heads of the fathers of the inhabitants of Aijalon, who drove away the inhabitants of Gath:
text.NABRE: and Beriah, and Shema. They were family heads of those who dwelt in Aijalon, and they put the inhabitants of Gath to flight.
text.DRB: And Baria, and Sama Princes of their kindreds that dwelt in Aialon: these drove away the inhabitants of Geth.

text.NRSV: and Ahio, Shashak, and Jeremoth.
text.Rotherham: and Ahio, Shashak, and Jeremoth,—
text.KJV: And Ahio, Shashak, and Jeremoth,
text.NABRE: Their relatives were Elpaal, Shashak, and Jeremoth.
text.DRB: And Ahio, & Sesac, and Jerimoth,

text.NRSV: Zebadiah, Arad, Eder,
text.Rotherham: and Zebadiah, and Arad, and Eder,—
text.KJV: And Zebadiah, and Arad, and Ader,
text.NABRE: Zebadiah, Arad, Eder,
text.DRB: and Sabadia, and Arod, and Heder,

text.NRSV: Michael, Ishpah, and Joha were sons of Beriah.
text.Rotherham: and Michael, and Ishpah, and Joha, sons of Beriah,—
text.KJV: And Michael, and Ispah, and Joha, the sons of Beriah;
text.NABRE: Michael, Ishpah, and Joha were the sons of Beriah.
text.DRB: Michael also, and Jespha, & Joha the sons of Baria.

text.NRSV: Zebadiah, Meshullam, Hizki, Heber,
text.Rotherham: and Zebadiah, and Meshullam, and Hizki, and Heber,—
text.KJV: And Zebadiah, and Meshullam, and Hezeki, and Heber,
text.NABRE: Zebadiah, Meshullam, Hizki, Heber,
text.DRB: And Zabadia, and Mosollam, and Hezeci, and Heber,

text.NRSV: Ishmerai, Izliah, and Jobab were sons of Elpaal.
text.Rotherham: and Ishmerai, and Izliah, and Jobab, sons of Elpaal,—
text.KJV: Ishmerai also, and Jezliah, and Jobab, the sons of Elpaal;
text.NABRE: Ishmerai, Izliah, and Jobab were the sons of Elpaal.
text.DRB: and Jesamari, and Jezlia, and Jobab sons of Elphaal,

text.NRSV: Jakim, Zichri, Zabdi,
text.Rotherham: and Jakim, and Zichri, and Zabdi—
text.KJV: And Jakim, and Zichri, and Zabdi,
text.NABRE: Jakim, Zichri, Zabdi,
text.DRB: and Jacim, and Zechri, and Zabdi

text.NRSV: Elienai, Zillethai, Eliel,
text.Rotherham: and Elienai, and Zillethai, and Eliel,—
text.KJV: And Elienai, and Zilthai, and Eliel,
text.NABRE: Elienai, Zillethai, Eliel,
text.DRB: and Elioenai, and Selethai, and Eliel,

text.NRSV: Adaiah, Beraiah, and Shimrath were sons of Shimei.
text.Rotherham: and Adaiah, and Beraiah, and Shimrath, sons of Shimei,—
text.KJV: And Adaiah, and Beraiah, and Shimrath, the sons of Shimhi;
text.NABRE: Adaiah, Beraiah, and Shimrath were the sons of Shimei.
text.DRB: and Adaia, and Baraia, and Samarath the sons of Semei.

text.NRSV: Ishpan, Eber, Eliel,
text.Rotherham: and Ishpan, and Eber, and Eliel,—
text.KJV: And Ishpan, and Heber, and Eliel,
text.NABRE: Ishpan, Eber, Eliel,
text.DRB: And Jespham, and Heber, and Eliel.

text.NRSV: Abdon, Zichri, Hanan,
text.Rotherham: and Abdon, and Zichri, and Hanan,—
text.KJV: And Abdon, and Zichri, and Hanan,
text.NABRE: Abdon, Zichri, Hanan,
text.DRB: and Abdon, and Zechri, and Hanan,

text.NRSV: Hananiah, Elam, Anthothijah,
text.Rotherham: and Hananiah, and Elam, and Anthothijah,—
text.KJV: And Hananiah, and Elam, and Antothijah,
text.NABRE: Hananiah, Elam, Anthothijah,
text.DRB: and Hanania, and Ælam, and Anathothia,

text.NRSV: Iphdeiah, and Penuel were sons of Shashak.
text.Rotherham: and Iphdeiah, and Penuel, sons of Shashak,—
text.KJV: And Iphedeiah, and Penuel, the sons of Shashak;
text.NABRE: Iphdeiah, and Penuel were the sons of Shashak.
text.DRB: and Jephdaia, and Phanuel the sons of Sesac.

text.NRSV: Shamsherai, Shehariah, Athaliah,
text.Rotherham: and Shamsherai, and Shehariah, and Athaliah,—
text.KJV: And Shamsherai, and Shehariah, and Athaliah,
text.NABRE: Shamsherai, Shehariah, Athaliah,
text.DRB: and Samsari, and Sohoria and Otholia.

text.NRSV: Jaareshiah, Elijah, and Zichri were sons of Jeroham.
text.Rotherham: and Jaareshiah, and Elijah, and Zichri, sons of Jeroham:
text.KJV: And Jaresiah, and Eliah, and Zichri, the sons of Jeroham.
text.NABRE: Jaareshiah, Elijah, and Zichri were the sons of Jeroham.
text.DRB: and Jersia, and Elia, and Zechri, the sons of Jeroham.

text.NRSV: These were heads of ancestral houses, according to their generations, chiefs. These lived in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: these, were ancestral chiefs to their generations, chief men,—these, dwelt in Jerusalem;
text.KJV: These were heads of the fathers, by their generations, chief men. These dwelt in Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: These were family heads in their generations, chiefs who dwelt in Jerusalem.
text.DRB: these be the Patriarchs, and Princes of their kindreds, which dwelt in Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Jeiel the father of Gibeon lived in Gibeon, and the name of his wife was Maacah.
text.Rotherham: And, in Gibeon, dwelt the father of Gibeon [Jeiel], the name of whose wife, was Maacah,—
text.KJV: And at Gibeon dwelt the father of Gibeon; whose wife’s name was Maachah:
text.NABRE: In Gibeon dwelt Jeiel, the founder of Gibeon, whose wife’s name was Maacah;
text.DRB: And in Gabaon dwelt Abigabaon, and the name of his wife Maacha:

text.NRSV: His firstborn son: Abdon, then Zur, Kish, Baal, Nadab,
text.Rotherham: and his firstborn son, Abdon, and Zur and Kish, and Baal and Ner and Nadab,—
text.KJV: And his firstborn son Abdon, and Zur, and Kish, and Baal, and Nadab,
text.NABRE: also his firstborn son, Abdon, and Zur, Kish, Baal, Ner, Nadab,
text.DRB: And his first-begotten son Abdon, and Sur, and Cis, and Baal, and Nadab.

text.NRSV: Gedor, Ahio, Zecher,
text.Rotherham: and Gedor, and Ahio, and Zecher.
text.KJV: And Gedor, and Ahio, and Zacher.
text.NABRE: Gedor, Ahio, Zecher, and Mikloth.
text.DRB: Gedor also, and Ahio, and Zacher, and Macelloth:

text.NRSV: and Mikloth, who became the father of Shimeah. Now these also lived opposite their kindred in Jerusalem, with their kindred.
text.Rotherham: And, Mikloth, begat Shimeah,—moreover also, they, over against their brethren, did dwell in Jerusalem, with their own brethren.
text.KJV: And Mikloth begat Shimeah. And these also dwelt with their brethren in Jerusalem, over against them.
text.NABRE: Mikloth became the father of Shimeah. These, too, dwelt with their relatives in Jerusalem, opposite their fellow tribesmen.
text.DRB: and Macelloth begat Samaa: and they dwelt over against their brethren in Jerusalem with their brethren.

text.NRSV: Ner became the father of Kish, Kish became the father of Saul, Saul became the father of Jonathan, Malchi-shua, Abinadab, and Esh-baal;
text.Rotherham: So then, Ner, begat Kish, and, Kish, begat Saul,—and, Saul, begat Jonathan, and Malchishua, and Abinadab, and Eshbaal.
text.KJV: And Ner begat Kish, and Kish begat Saul, and Saul begat Jonathan, and Malchi–shua, and Abinadab, and Esh–baal.
text.NABRE: Ner became the father of Kish, and Kish became the father of Saul. Saul became the father of Jonathan, Malchishua, Abinadab, and Eshbaal.
text.DRB: And Ner begat Cis, and Cis begat Saul. Moreover Saul begat Jonathas, and Melchisua, and Abinadab, and Esbaal.

text.NRSV: and the son of Jonathan was Merib-baal; and Merib-baal became the father of Micah.
text.Rotherham: And, the son of Jonathan, was Merib-baal,—and, Merib-baal, begat Micah.
text.KJV: And the son of Jonathan was Merib–baal; and Merib–baal begat Micah.
text.NABRE: The son of Jonathan was Meribbaal, and Meribbaal became the father of Micah.
text.DRB: And the son of Jonathas, Meribbaal: and Meribbaal begat Micha.

text.NRSV: The sons of Micah: Pithon, Melech, Tarea, and Ahaz.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Micah, were Pithon, and Melech, and Tarea, and Ahaz.
text.KJV: And the sons of Micah were, Pithon, and Melech, and Tarea, and Ahaz.
text.NABRE: The sons of Micah were Pithon, Melech, Tarea, and Ahaz.
text.DRB: The sons of Micha, Phithon, and Melech, and Tharaa, and Ahaz.

text.NRSV: Ahaz became the father of Jehoaddah; and Jehoaddah became the father of Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri; Zimri became the father of Moza.
text.Rotherham: And, Ahaz, begat Jehoaddah, and, Jehoaddah, begat Alemeth, and Azmaveth, and Zimri,—and, Zimri, begat Moza;
text.KJV: And Ahaz begat Jehoadah; and Jehoadah begat Alemeth, and Azmaveth, and Zimri; and Zimri begat Moza,
text.NABRE: Ahaz became the father of Jehoaddah, and Jehoaddah became the father of Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri. Zimri became the father of Moza.
text.DRB: And Ahaz begat Joada and Joada begat Alamath, and Azmoth, and Zamri: moreover Zamri begat Mosa,

text.NRSV: Moza became the father of Binea; Raphah was his son, Eleasah his son, Azel his son.
text.Rotherham: and, Moza, begat Binea,—Raphah was his son, Eleasah his son, Azel his son.
text.KJV: And Moza begat Binea: Rapha was his son, Eleasah his son, Azel his son:
text.NABRE: Moza became the father of Binea, whose son was Raphah, whose son was Eleasah, whose son was Azel.
text.DRB: and Mosa begat Banaa, whose son was Rapha, of whom was born Elasa, who begat Asel.

text.NRSV: Azel had six sons, and these are their names: Azrikam, Bocheru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan. All these were the sons of Azel.
text.Rotherham: And, Azel, had six sons, and, these, were their names—Azrikam, Bocheru, and Ishmael, and Sheariah, and Obediah, and Hanan and Asah,—all these, were sons of Azel.
text.KJV: And Azel had six sons, whose names are these, Azrikam, Bocheru, and Ishmael, and Sheariah, and Obadiah, and Hanan. All these were the sons of Azel.
text.NABRE: Azel had six sons, whose names were Azrikam, his firstborn, Ishmael, Sheariah, Azariah, Obadiah, and Hanan; all these were the sons of Azel.
text.DRB: Moreover Asel had six sons of these names, Ezricam, Bochru, Ismahel, Saria, Obdia, and Hanan. All these the sons of Asel.

text.NRSV: The sons of Eshek his brother: Ulam his firstborn, Jeush the second, and Eliphelet the third.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Eshek his brother, were Ulam his firstborn, Jeush the second, and Eliphelet the third.
text.KJV: And the sons of Eshek his brother were, Ulam his firstborn, Jehush the second, and Eliphelet the third.
text.NABRE: The sons of Eshek, his brother, were Ulam, his firstborn, Jeush, the second son, and Eliphelet, the third.
text.DRB: And the sons of Esec his brother, Ulam the first-begotten, and Jehus the second, and Eliphalet the third.

text.NRSV: The sons of Ulam were mighty warriors, strong archers, having many sons and grandsons, one hundred fifty. All these were Benjaminites.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Ulam became men that were heroes of valour, archers, with many sons, and sons’ sons, a hundred and fifty. All these, were of the sons of Benjamin.
text.KJV: And the sons of Ulam were mighty men of valour, archers, and had many sons, and sons’ sons, an hundred and fifty. All these are of the sons of Benjamin.
text.NABRE: The sons of Ulam were warriors, skilled with the bow, and they had many sons and grandsons: one hundred and fifty. All these were the sons of Benjamin.
text.DRB: And the sons of Ulam were most strong men, and archers of great force: and having many sons and nephews, unto an hundred fifty. All these the children of Benjamin.

text.NRSV: So all Israel was enrolled by genealogies; and these are written in the Book of the Kings of Israel. And Judah was taken into exile in Babylon because of their unfaithfulness.
text.Rotherham: And, all Israel, registered themselves, and lo! they are written, in the Book of the Kings of Israel, and, Judah, was carried away captive to Babylon, for their faithlessness.
text.KJV: So all Israel were reckoned by genealogies; and, behold, they were written in the book of the kings of Israel and Judah, who were carried away to Babylon for their transgression.
text.NABRE: Chapter 9 Thus all Israel was listed in family lists, and these are recorded in the book of the kings of Israel. Now Judah had been exiled to Babylon because of its treachery.
text.DRB: All Israel therefore was numbered: and the sum of them was written in the Book of the kings of Israel & Juda and they were transported into Babylon for their sin.

text.NRSV: Now the first to live again in their possessions in their towns were Israelites, priests, Levites, and temple servants.
text.Rotherham: Now, the first inhabitants, who were in their possessions, in their cities, were Israel, the priests, the Levites, and the Nethinim.
text.KJV: Now the first inhabitants that dwelt in their possessions in their cities were, the Israelites, the priests, Levites, and the Nethinims.
text.NABRE: The first to settle again in their cities and dwell there were certain Israelites, the priests, the Levites, and the temple servants.
text.DRB: And they that dwelt first in their possessions and in their cities: Israel, and the Priests, and the Levites, and the Nathineans.

text.NRSV: And some of the people of Judah, Benjamin, Ephraim, and Manasseh lived in Jerusalem:
text.Rotherham: And, in Jerusalem, there dwelt, of the sons of Judah, and of the sons of Benjamin,—and of the sons of Ephraim, and Manasseh:
text.KJV: And in Jerusalem dwelt of the children of Judah, and of the children of Benjamin, and of the children of Ephraim, and Manasseh;
text.NABRE: Jerusalemites. In Jerusalem lived Judahites and Benjaminites; also Ephraimites and Manassites.
text.DRB: There dwelt in Jerusalem of the children of Juda, and of the children of Benjamin, also of the children of Ephraim, and Manasses.

text.NRSV: Uthai son of Ammihud, son of Omri, son of Imri, son of Bani, from the sons of Perez son of Judah.
text.Rotherham: Uthai son of Ammihud, son of Omri, son of Imri, son of Bani, of the sons of Perez, son of Judah.
text.KJV: Uthai the son of Ammihud, the son of Omri, the son of Imri, the son of Bani, of the children of Pharez the son of Judah.
text.NABRE: Among the Judahites was Uthai, son of Ammihud, son of Omri, son of Imri, son of Bani, one of the sons of Perez, son of Judah.
text.DRB: Othei the son of Ammiud, the son of Amri, the son of Omrai, the son of Bonni, of the sons of Phares the son of Juda.

text.NRSV: And of the Shilonites: Asaiah the firstborn, and his sons.
text.Rotherham: And, of the Shilonites, Asaiah the firstborn, and his sons.
text.KJV: And of the Shilonites; Asaiah the firstborn, and his sons.
text.NABRE: Among the Shelanites were Asaiah, the firstborn, and his sons.
text.DRB: And of Siloni: Asaia the first-begotten, and his sons.

text.NRSV: Of the sons of Zerah: Jeuel and their kindred, six hundred ninety.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Zerah, Jeuel,—and their brethren, six hundred and ninety.
text.KJV: And of the sons of Zerah; Jeuel, and their brethren, six hundred and ninety.
text.NABRE: Among the Zerahites were Jeuel and six hundred and ninety of their relatives.
text.DRB: And of the sons of Zara: Jehuel, and their brethren, six hundred ninety.

text.NRSV: Of the Benjaminites: Sallu son of Meshullam, son of Hodaviah, son of Hassenuah,
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Benjamin, Sallu, son of Meshullam, son of Hodaviah, son of Hassenuah;
text.KJV: And of the sons of Benjamin; Sallu the son of Meshullam, the son of Hodaviah, the son of Hasenuah,
text.NABRE: Among the Benjaminites were Sallu, son of Meshullam, son of Hodaviah, son of Hassenuah,
text.DRB: Moreover of the sons of Benjamin: Salo the son of Mosollam, the son of Oduia the son of Asana:

text.NRSV: Ibneiah son of Jeroham, Elah son of Uzzi, son of Michri, and Meshullam son of Shephatiah, son of Reuel, son of Ibnijah;
text.Rotherham: and Ibneiah, son of Jeroham, and Elah, son of Uzzi, son of Michri,—and Meshullam, son of Shephatiah, son of Reuel, son of Ibnijah;
text.KJV: And Ibneiah the son of Jeroham, and Elah the son of Uzzi, the son of Michri, and Meshullam the son of Shephathiah, the son of Reuel, the son of Ibnijah;
text.NABRE: as well as Ibneiah, son of Jeroham; Elah, son of Uzzi, son of Michri; Meshullam, son of Shephatiah, son of Reuel, son of Ibnijah.
text.DRB: and Jobania the son of Jeroham: and Ela the son of Ozi the son of Mochori: and Mosollam the son of Saphatias, the son of Rahuel, the son of Jebania,

text.NRSV: and their kindred according to their generations, nine hundred fifty-six. All these were heads of families according to their ancestral houses.
text.Rotherham: and their brethren, by their generations, nine hundred and fifty-six,—all these men, were ancestral chiefs, to their ancestral house.
text.KJV: And their brethren, according to their generations, nine hundred and fifty and six. All these men were chief of the fathers in the house of their fathers.
text.NABRE: Their kindred of various families were nine hundred and fifty-six. All those named were heads of their ancestral houses.
text.DRB: and their brethren by their families, nine hundred fifty six. All these Princes of their kindreds by the houses of their fathers.

text.NRSV: Of the priests: Jedaiah, Jehoiarib, Jachin,
text.Rotherham: And, of the priests, Jedaiah, and Jehoiarib, and Jachin,—
text.KJV: And of the priests; Jedaiah, and Jehoiarib, and Jachin,
text.NABRE: Among the priests were Jedaiah; Jehoiarib; Jachin;
text.DRB: And of the Priests: Jedaia, Joiarib, and Jachin:

text.NRSV: and Azariah son of Hilkiah, son of Meshullam, son of Zadok, son of Meraioth, son of Ahitub, the chief officer of the house of God;
text.Rotherham: and Azariah son of Hilkiah, son of Meshullam, son of Zadok, son of Meraioth, son of Ahitub, chief ruler of the house of God;
text.KJV: And Azariah the son of Hilkiah, the son of Meshullam, the son of Zadok, the son of Meraioth, the son of Ahitub, the ruler of the house of God;
text.NABRE: Azariah, son of Hilkiah, son of Meshullam, son of Zadok, son of Meraioth, son of Ahitub, the ruler of the house of God;
text.DRB: Azarias also the son of Helcias, the son of Mosollam, the son of Sadoc, the son of Maraioth, the son of Achitob, high Priest of the house of God.

text.NRSV: and Adaiah son of Jeroham, son of Pashhur, son of Malchijah, and Maasai son of Adiel, son of Jahzerah, son of Meshullam, son of Meshillemith, son of Immer;
text.Rotherham: and Adaiah, son of Jeroham, son of Pashhur, son of Malchijah,—and Maasai son of Adiel, son of Jahzerah, son of Meshullam, son of Meshillemith, son of Immer;
text.KJV: And Adaiah the son of Jeroham, the son of Pashur, the son of Malchijah, and Maasiai the son of Adiel, the son of Jahzerah, the son of Meshullam, the son of Meshillemith, the son of Immer;
text.NABRE: Adaiah, son of Jeroham, son of Pashhur, son of Malchijah; Maasai, son of Adiel, son of Jahzerah, son of Meshullam, son of Meshillemith, son of Immer.
text.DRB: Moreover Adaias the son of Jeroham, the son of Phassur, the son of Melchias: and Maasai the son of Adiel, the son of Jezra, the son of Mosollam, the son of Mosollamith, the son of Emmer.

text.NRSV: besides their kindred, heads of their ancestral houses, one thousand seven hundred sixty, qualified for the work of the service of the house of God.
text.Rotherham: and their brethren, chief men of their ancestral house, a thousand and seven hundred and sixty,—able men, for the business of the service of the house of God.
text.KJV: And their brethren, heads of the house of their fathers, a thousand and seven hundred and threescore; very able men for the work of the service of the house of God.
text.NABRE: Their brothers, heads of their ancestral houses, were one thousand seven hundred and sixty, valiant in the work of the service of the house of God.
text.DRB: their brethren also Princes by their families a thousand seven hundred three score, most able men of strength to the work of the ministry in the house of God.

text.NRSV: Of the Levites: Shemaiah son of Hasshub, son of Azrikam, son of Hashabiah, of the sons of Merari;
text.Rotherham: And, of the Levites, Shemaiah, son of Hasshub, son of Azrikam, son of Hashabiah, of the sons of Merari;
text.KJV: And of the Levites; Shemaiah the son of Hasshub, the son of Azrikam, the son of Hashabiah, of the sons of Merari;
text.NABRE: Among the Levites were Shemaiah, son of Hasshub, son of Azrikam, son of Hashabiah, one of the sons of Merari;
text.DRB: And of the Levites: Semeia the son of Hassub the son of Ezricam, the son of Hasebia of the sons of Merari.

text.NRSV: and Bakbakkar, Heresh, Galal, and Mattaniah son of Mica, son of Zichri, son of Asaph;
text.Rotherham: and Bakbakkar, Heresh, and Galal,—and Mattaniah, son of Mica, son of Zichri, son of Asaph;
text.KJV: And Bakbakkar, Heresh, and Galal, and Mattaniah the son of Micah, the son of Zichri, the son of Asaph;
text.NABRE: Bakbakkar; Heresh; Galal; Mattaniah, son of Mica, son of Zichri, a descendant of Asaph;
text.DRB: Bacbacar also a carpenter, and Galal, and Mathania the son of Micha, the son of Zechri the son of Asaph:

text.NRSV: and Obadiah son of Shemaiah, son of Galal, son of Jeduthun, and Berechiah son of Asa, son of Elkanah, who lived in the villages of the Netophathites.
text.Rotherham: and Obadiah, son of Shemaiah, son of Galal, son of Jeduthun,—and Berechiah son of Asa, son of Elkanah, who dwelt in the villages of the Netophathites.
text.KJV: And Obadiah the son of Shemaiah, the son of Galal, the son of Jeduthun, and Berechiah the son of Asa, the son of Elkanah, that dwelt in the villages of the Netophathites.
text.NABRE: Obadiah, son of Shemaiah, son of Galal, a descendant of Jeduthun; and Berechiah, son of Asa, son of Elkanah, whose family lived in the villages of the Netophathites.
text.DRB: and Obdia the son of Semei, the son of Galal, the son of Idithun: and Barachia the son of Asa, the son of Elcana, who dwelt in the courts of Netophati.

text.NRSV: The gatekeepers were: Shallum, Akkub, Talmon, Ahiman, and their kindred (Shallum was the chief),
text.Rotherham: And, the keepers of the gates, were Shallum, and Akkub, and Talmon, and Ahiman,—and their brethren—Shallum the chief;
text.KJV: And the porters were, Shallum, and Akkub, and Talmon, and Ahiman, and their brethren: Shallum was the chief;
text.NABRE: The gatekeepers were Shallum, Akkub, Talmon, Ahiman, and their brothers; Shallum was the chief.
text.DRB: And the porters: Sellum, and Accub, and Telmon, and Ahimam: and their brother Sellum the Prince,

text.NRSV: stationed until now at the King’s Gate on the east side. These were the gatekeepers of the camp of the Levites.
text.Rotherham: and, hitherto, they were in the gate of the king, eastward,—the same, were the keepers of the gate, for the camps of the sons of Levi.
text.KJV: Who hitherto waited in the king’s gate eastward: they were porters in the companies of the children of Levi.
text.NABRE: Previously they had stood guard at the king’s gate on the east side; now they became gatekeepers for the encampments of the Levites.
text.DRB: until that time, in the king's gate toward the east, waited by their courses of the children of Levi.

text.NRSV: Shallum son of Kore, son of Ebiasaph, son of Korah, and his kindred of his ancestral house, the Korahites, were in charge of the work of the service, guardians of the thresholds of the tent, as their ancestors had been in charge of the camp of the Lord, guardians of the entrance.
text.Rotherham: And, Shallum, son of Kore, son of Ebiasaph, son of Korah and his brethren of his ancestral house—the Korahites, were over the business of the service, watchers at the vestibule of the tent,—and, their fathers, had been over the camp of Yahweh, watchers at the entrance.
text.KJV: And Shallum the son of Kore, the son of Ebiasaph, the son of Korah, and his brethren, of the house of his father, the Korahites, were over the work of the service, keepers of the gates of the tabernacle: and their fathers, being over the host of the Lord, were keepers of the entry.
text.NABRE: Shallum, son of Kore, son of Ebiasaph, a descendant of Korah, and his brothers of the same ancestral house of the Korahites had as their assigned task the guarding of the threshold of the tent, just as their fathers had guarded the entrance to the encampment of the Lord.
text.DRB: But Sellum the son of Core the son of Abiasaph, the son of Core, with his brethren, and his father's house, these are the Corites over the works of the ministry, keepers of the entrances of the tabernacle: and their families in course keeping the entrance of the camp of our Lord.

text.NRSV: And Phinehas son of Eleazar was chief over them in former times; the Lord was with him.
text.Rotherham: And, Phinehas son of Eleazar, was, chief ruler, over them aforetime, Yahweh, being with him.
text.KJV: And Phinehas the son of Eleazar was the ruler over them in time past, and the Lord was with him.
text.NABRE: Phinehas, son of Eleazar, had been their chief in times past; the Lord was with him.
text.DRB: And Phinees the son of Eleasar, was their Prince before our Lord.

text.NRSV: Zechariah son of Meshelemiah was gatekeeper at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: Zechariah son of Meshelemiah, was door-keeper at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.KJV: And Zechariah the son of Meshelemiah was porter of the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
text.NABRE: Zechariah, son of Meshelemiah, guarded the gate of the tent of meeting.
text.DRB: Moreover Zacharias the son of Mosollamia, porter of the gate of the tabernacle of testimony.

text.NRSV: All these, who were chosen as gatekeepers at the thresholds, were two hundred twelve. They were enrolled by genealogies in their villages. These David and the seer Samuel established in their office of trust.
text.Rotherham: All those who were chosen for door-keepers in the vestibule, were two hundred and twelve,—the same, in their villages, had registered themselves, the same, did David and Samuel the seer establish in their trust.
text.KJV: All these which were chosen to be porters in the gates were two hundred and twelve. These were reckoned by their genealogy in their villages, whom David and Samuel the seer did ordain in their set office.
text.NABRE: In all, those who were chosen for gatekeepers at the threshold were two hundred and twelve. They were inscribed in the family records of their villages. David and Samuel the seer had established them in their position of trust.
text.DRB: All these chosen men for porters, at every gate, two hundred twelve: and appointed out in their proper towns: Whom David and Samuel the seer appointed, upon their fidelity.

text.NRSV: So they and their sons were in charge of the gates of the house of the Lord, that is, the house of the tent, as guards.
text.Rotherham: So, they and their sons, were over the gates of the house of Yahweh, of the house of the tent, by watches.
text.KJV: So they and their children had the oversight of the gates of the house of the Lord, namely, the house of the tabernacle, by wards.
text.NABRE: Thus they and their sons kept guard over the gates of the house of the Lord, the house which was then a tent.
text.DRB: as well them as their sons, in the doors of the house of our Lord, and in the tabernacle by their courses.

text.NRSV: The gatekeepers were on the four sides, east, west, north, and south;
text.Rotherham: Towards the four winds, were the keepers of the gates,—eastward, westward, northward, and southward.
text.KJV: In four quarters were the porters, toward the east, west, north, and south.
text.NABRE: The gatekeepers were stationed at the four sides, to the east, the west, the north, and the south.
text.DRB: By the four winds were the porters: that is to say, toward the East, and toward the West, & toward the North, & toward the South.

text.NRSV: and their kindred who were in their villages were obliged to come in every seven days, from time to time, to be with these;
text.Rotherham: And, their brethren in their villages, had to come in, every seven days, from time to time, along with these.
text.KJV: And their brethren, which were in their villages, were to come after seven days from time to time with them.
text.NABRE: Their brothers who lived in their own villages took turns in assisting them for seven-day periods,
text.DRB: And their brethren dwelt in villages, & came upon their Sabbaths from time to time.

text.NRSV: for the four chief gatekeepers, who were Levites, were in charge of the chambers and the treasures of the house of God.
text.Rotherham: For, in trust, were four mighty men of the keepers of the gates, the same, were Levites,—and they were over the chambers, and over the treasuries of the house of God.
text.KJV: For these Levites, the four chief porters, were in their set office, and were over the chambers and treasuries of the house of God.
text.NABRE: while the four chief gatekeepers were on permanent duty. These were the Levites who also had charge of the chambers and treasures of the house of God.
text.DRB: To these four Levites was committed all the number of porters, & they were over the chambers, and treasures of the house of our Lord.

text.NRSV: And they would spend the night near the house of God; for on them lay the duty of watching, and they had charge of opening it every morning.
text.Rotherham: And, round about the house of God, used they to lodge,—for, upon them, was the charge, and they were over the setting open, morning by morning.
text.KJV: And they lodged round about the house of God, because the charge was upon them, and the opening thereof every morning pertained to them.
text.NABRE: They would spend the night near the house of God, for it was in their charge and they had the duty of opening it each morning.
text.DRB: Also round about the temple of our Lord they abode in their watches: that when it was time, they in the morning might open the doors.

text.NRSV: Some of them had charge of the utensils of service, for they were required to count them when they were brought in and taken out.
text.Rotherham: And, some from among them, were over the utensils of the service,—for, by number, used they to bring them in, and, by number, used they to take them forth.
text.KJV: And certain of them had the charge of the ministering vessels, that they should bring them in and out by tale.
text.NABRE: Some of them had charge of the vessels used there, tallying them as they were brought in and taken out.
text.DRB: Of these men's stock there were also over the vessels of the ministry: for by number the vessels were both brought in and carried out.

text.NRSV: Others of them were appointed over the furniture, and over all the holy utensils, also over the choice flour, the wine, the oil, the incense, and the spices.
text.Rotherham: And, some from among them, were appointed over the utensils, yea over all the vessels of the holy place,—and over the fine meal, and the wine, and the oil, and the frankincense, and the spices.
text.KJV: Some of them also were appointed to oversee the vessels, and all the instruments of the sanctuary, and the fine flour, and the wine, and the oil, and the frankincense, and the spices.
text.NABRE: Others were appointed to take care of the utensils and all the sacred vessels, as well as the fine flour, the wine, the oil, the frankincense, and the spices.
text.DRB: Of them also they that had the implements of the sanctuary committed unto them, did oversee the flour, and wine, and oil, and frankincense, and spices.

text.NRSV: Others, of the sons of the priests, prepared the mixing of the spices,
text.Rotherham: And, some from among the sons of the priests, were compounders of perfumes, with the spices.
text.KJV: And some of the sons of the priests made the ointment of the spices.
text.NABRE: It was the sons of priests, however, who mixed the spiced ointments.
text.DRB: And the sons of Priests made ointments of the spices.

text.NRSV: and Mattithiah, one of the Levites, the firstborn of Shallum the Korahite, was in charge of making the flat cakes.
text.Rotherham: And, Mattithiah, from among the Levites,—the same, was the firstborn of Shallum the Korahite—was in trust over the making of the flat cakes.
text.KJV: And Mattithiah, one of the Levites, who was the firstborn of Shallum the Korahite, had the set office over the things that were made in the pans.
text.NABRE: Mattithiah, one of the Levites, the firstborn of Shallum the Korahite, was entrusted with preparing the cakes.
text.DRB: And Mathathias a Levite the first-begotten of Sellum the Corite, was overseer of those things, which were fried in the frying pan.

text.NRSV: Also some of their kindred of the Kohathites had charge of the rows of bread, to prepare them for every sabbath.
text.Rotherham: And, some from among the Kohathites, of their brethren, were over the Bread that was set in Array,—to place it sabbath by sabbath.
text.KJV: And other of their brethren, of the sons of the Kohathites, were over the shewbread, to prepare it every sabbath.
text.NABRE: Benaiah the Kohathite, one of their brothers, was in charge of setting out the showbread each sabbath.
text.DRB: Moreover of the children of Caath their brethren, there were over the loaves of proposition, to prepare always new every Sabbath.

text.NRSV: Now these are the singers, the heads of ancestral houses of the Levites, living in the chambers of the temple free from other service, for they were on duty day and night.
text.Rotherham: These, also were the singers, ancestral chiefs of the Levites, in the chambers, free,—for, by day and by night, was there [a charge] upon them, in the business.
text.KJV: And these are the singers, chief of the fathers of the Levites, who remaining in the chambers were free: for they were employed in that work day and night.
text.NABRE: These were the singers and the gatekeepers, family heads over the Levites. They stayed in the chambers when free of duty, for day and night they had to be ready for service.
text.DRB: These are the chief of the singing men by the families of the Levites, which abode in the chambers, that they might day and night continually serve in their ministry.

text.NRSV: These were heads of ancestral houses of the Levites, according to their generations, chiefs; these lived in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: These, were the ancestral chiefs, of the Levites, by their generations, chief men,—these, dwelt in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: These chief fathers of the Levites were chief throughout their generations; these dwelt at Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: These were the levitical family heads by their generations, chiefs who dwelt in Jerusalem.
text.DRB: The heads of the Levites, Princes in their families, tarried in Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: In Gibeon lived the father of Gibeon, Jeiel, and the name of his wife was Maacah.
text.Rotherham: And, in Gibeon, dwelt the father of Gibeon, Jeiel,—the name of whose wife, was Maacah:
text.KJV: And in Gibeon dwelt the father of Gibeon, Jehiel, whose wife’s name was Maachah:
text.NABRE: II. The History of David Genealogy of Saul. Jeiel, the founder of Gibeon, dwelt in Gibeon; his wife’s name was Maacah.
text.DRB: And in Gabaon abode Jehiel the father of Gabaon, and the name of his wife Maacha.

text.NRSV: His firstborn son was Abdon, then Zur, Kish, Baal, Ner, Nadab,
text.Rotherham: and, his firstborn son, Abdon,—and Zur, and Kish, and Baal and Ner, and Nadab;
text.KJV: And his firstborn son Abdon, then Zur, and Kish, and Baal, and Ner, and Nadab,
text.NABRE: His firstborn son was Abdon; then came Zur, Kish, Baal, Ner, Nadab,
text.DRB: His first-begotten son Abdon, and Sur, and Cis, and Baal, and Ner, and Nadab,

text.NRSV: Gedor, Ahio, Zechariah, and Mikloth;
text.Rotherham: and Gedor, and Ahio, and Zechariah, and Mikloh.
text.KJV: And Gedor, and Ahio, and Zechariah, and Mikloth.
text.NABRE: Gedor, Ahio, Zechariah, and Mikloth.
text.DRB: Gedor also, and Ahio, and Zacharias, and Macelloth.

text.NRSV: and Mikloth became the father of Shimeam; and these also lived opposite their kindred in Jerusalem, with their kindred.
text.Rotherham: And, Mikloth, begat Shimeam—and, they also, over against their brethren, did dwell in Jerusalem, along with their brethren.
text.KJV: And Mikloth begat Shimeam. And they also dwelt with their brethren at Jerusalem, over against their brethren.
text.NABRE: Mikloth became the father of Shimeam. These, too, with their relatives, dwelt opposite their relatives in Jerusalem.
text.DRB: Moreover Macelloth begat Samaan, these dwelt over against their brethren in Jerusalem, with their brethren.

text.NRSV: Ner became the father of Kish, Kish became the father of Saul, Saul became the father of Jonathan, Malchi-shua, Abinadab, and Esh-baal;
text.Rotherham: And, Ner, begat Kith, and, Kish, begat Saul,—and, Saul, begat Jonathan, and Malchishua, and Abinadab, and Eshbaal;
text.KJV: And Ner begat Kish; and Kish begat Saul; and Saul begat Jonathan, and Malchi–shua, and Abinadab, and Esh–baal.
text.NABRE: Ner became the father of Kish, and Kish became the father of Saul. Saul became the father of Jonathan, Malchishua, Abinadab, and Eshbaal.
text.DRB: And Ner begat Cis: and Cis begat Saul: and Saul begat Jonathas, and Melchisua, and Abinadab, and Esbaal.

text.NRSV: and the son of Jonathan was Merib-baal; and Merib-baal became the father of Micah.
text.Rotherham: and, the son of Jonathan, was Merib-baal,—and, Merib-baal, begat Micah;
text.KJV: And the son of Jonathan was Merib–baal: and Merib–baal begat Micah.
text.NABRE: The son of Jonathan was Meribbaal, and Meribbaal became the father of Micah.
text.DRB: And the son of Jonathas, Meribbaal: & Meribbaal begat Micha.

text.NRSV: The sons of Micah: Pithon, Melech, Tahrea, and Ahaz;
text.Rotherham: and, the sons of Micah, were Pithon, and Melech, and Tahrea [and Ahaz];
text.KJV: And the sons of Micah were, Pithon, and Melech, and Tahrea, and Ahaz.
text.NABRE: The sons of Micah were Pithon, Melech, Tahrea, and Ahaz.
text.DRB: Moreover the sons of Micha, Phithon, & Melech, & Tharaa, and Ahaz.

text.NRSV: and Ahaz became the father of Jarah, and Jarah became the father of Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri; and Zimri became the father of Moza.
text.Rotherham: and, Ahaz, begat Jarah, and, Jarah begat Alemeth, and Azmaveth, and Zimri,—and Zimri, begat Moza;
text.KJV: And Ahaz begat Jarah; and Jarah begat Alemeth, and Azmaveth, and Zimri; and Zimri begat Moza;
text.NABRE: Ahaz became the father of Jehoaddah, and Jehoaddah became the father of Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri. Zimri became the father of Moza.
text.DRB: And Ahaz begat Jara, & Jara begat Alamath, & Azmoth, & Zamri. And Zamri begat Mosa.

text.NRSV: Moza became the father of Binea; and Rephaiah was his son, Eleasah his son, Azel his son.
text.Rotherham: and, Moza, begat Binea,—and Raphaiah his son, Eleasah his son, Azel his son;
text.KJV: And Moza begat Binea; and Rephaiah his son, Eleasah his son, Azel his son.
text.NABRE: Moza became the father of Binea, whose son was Rephaiah, whose son was Eleasah, whose son was Azel.
text.DRB: And Mosa begat Banaa: whose son Raphaia begat Elasa: of whom was born Asel.

text.NRSV: Azel had six sons, and these are their names: Azrikam, Bocheru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan; these were the sons of Azel.
text.Rotherham: and, Azel, had six sons, and these are their names—Azrikam, [his firstborn], and Ishmael, and Sheariah and Obadiah, and Hanan, [and Asah]. These, were the sons of Azel.
text.KJV: And Azel had six sons, whose names are these, Azrikam, Bocheru, and Ishmael, and Sheariah, and Obadiah, and Hanan: these were the sons of Azel.
text.NABRE: Azel had six sons, whose names were Azrikam, his firstborn, Ishmael, Sheariah, Azariah, Obadiah, and Hanan; these were the sons of Azel.
text.DRB: Moreover Asel had six sons of these names, Ezricam, Bochru, Ismael, Saria, Obdia, Hanan, these are the sons of Asel.

text.NRSV: Now the Philistines fought against Israel; and the men of Israel fled before the Philistines, and fell slain on Mount Gilboa.
text.Rotherham: Now, the Philistines, fought against Israel,—and the men of Israel fled from before the Philistines, and the wounded fell in Mount Gilboa.
text.KJV: Now the Philistines fought against Israel; and the men of Israel fled from before the Philistines, and fell down slain in mount Gilboa.
text.NABRE: Chapter 10 Death of Saul and His Sons. Now the Philistines went to war against Israel, and Israel fled before them, and they fell, slain on Mount Gilboa.
text.DRB: And the Philisthiims fought against Israel, and the men of Israel fled from the Palesthines, and they fell wounded in mount Gelboe.

text.NRSV: The Philistines overtook Saul and his sons; and the Philistines killed Jonathan and Abinadab and Malchi-shua, sons of Saul.
text.Rotherham: And the Philistines followed hard after Saul, and after his sons,—and the Philistines smote Jonathan, and Abinadab, and Malchishua, sons of Saul.
text.KJV: And the Philistines followed hard after Saul, and after his sons; and the Philistines slew Jonathan, and Abinadab, and Malchi–shua, the sons of Saul.
text.NABRE: The Philistines pressed hard after Saul and his sons. When the Philistines had struck down Jonathan, Abinadab, and Malchishua, sons of Saul,
text.DRB: And when the Philisthians were come near pursuing Saul and his sons, they struck Jonathas, and Abinadab, and Melchisua the sons of Saul.

text.NRSV: The battle pressed hard upon Saul; and the archers found him, and he was wounded by the archers.
text.Rotherham: And the battle, went sore, against Saul, and the archers discovered him with the bow,—and he was terrified because of the archers.
text.KJV: And the battle went sore against Saul, and the archers hit him, and he was wounded of the archers.
text.NABRE: the fury of the battle converged on Saul. Then the archers hit him, and he was severely wounded.
text.DRB: And the battle grew sore against Saul, and the archers found him, and wounded him with arrows.

text.NRSV: Then Saul said to his armor-bearer, “Draw your sword, and thrust me through with it, so that these uncircumcised may not come and make sport of me.” But his armor-bearer was unwilling, for he was terrified. So Saul took his own sword and fell on it.
text.Rotherham: Therefore said Saul unto his armour-bearer, Draw out thy sword and thrust me through therewith, lest these uncircumcised come and abuse me. But his armour-bearer would not, for he feared greatly,—so then Saul took the sword, and fell upon it.
text.KJV: Then said Saul to his armourbearer, Draw thy sword, and thrust me through therewith; lest these uncircumcised come and abuse me. But his armourbearer would not; for he was sore afraid. So Saul took a sword, and fell upon it.
text.NABRE: Saul said to his armor-bearer, “Draw your sword and run me through; otherwise these uncircumcised will come and abuse me.” But the armor-bearer, badly frightened, refused, so Saul took his own sword and fell upon it.
text.DRB: And Saul said to his esquire: Draw thy sword, and kill me: lest perhaps these uncircumcised come, and deride me. But his harness-bearer would not, being frighted with fear: Saul therefore caught his sword, and fell upon it.

text.NRSV: When his armor-bearer saw that Saul was dead, he also fell on his sword and died.
text.Rotherham: And, when his armour-bearer saw that Saul was dead, then, he also, fell upon the sword, and died.
text.KJV: And when his armourbearer saw that Saul was dead, he fell likewise on the sword, and died.
text.NABRE: When the armor-bearer saw that Saul was dead, he too fell upon his sword and died.
text.DRB: Which when his harness-bearer had seen, to wit, that Saul was dead, himself also fell upon his sword, and died.

text.NRSV: Thus Saul died; he and his three sons and all his house died together.
text.Rotherham: So Saul died, and his three sons, and, all his house, together, died.
text.KJV: So Saul died, and his three sons, and all his house died together.
text.NABRE: Thus Saul, and his three sons, his whole house, died together.
text.DRB: Saul therefore died, and his three sons, and all his house fell together.

text.NRSV: When all the men of Israel who were in the valley saw that the army had fled and that Saul and his sons were dead, they abandoned their towns and fled; and the Philistines came and occupied them.
text.Rotherham: And, when all the men of Israel that were in the vale saw, that they had fled, and that Saul and his sons were dead, then forsook they their cities, and fled, and the Philistines came and dwelt in them.
text.KJV: And when all the men of Israel that were in the valley saw that they fled, and that Saul and his sons were dead, then they forsook their cities, and fled: and the Philistines came and dwelt in them.
text.NABRE: When all the Israelites in the valley saw that Saul and his sons had fled and that they had died, they abandoned their cities and fled. Then the Philistines came and lived in those cities.
text.DRB: Which when the men of Israel had seen that dwelt in the champion, they fled: and Saul and his sons being dead, they forsook their cities, and were dispersed hither and thither: and the Philisthiims came, and dwelt in them.

text.NRSV: The next day when the Philistines came to strip the dead, they found Saul and his sons fallen on Mount Gilboa.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on the morrow, when the Philistines came to strip the slain,—that they found Saul, and his sons, lying prostrate in Mount Gilboa.
text.KJV: And it came to pass on the morrow, when the Philistines came to strip the slain, that they found Saul and his sons fallen in mount Gilboa.
text.NABRE: On the following day, when the Philistines came to strip the slain, they found Saul and his sons fallen on Mount Gilboa.
text.DRB: The next day therefore the Philisthiims taking away the spoils of them that were slain, found Saul and his sons lying on mount Gelboe.

text.NRSV: They stripped him and took his head and his armor, and sent messengers throughout the land of the Philistines to carry the good news to their idols and to the people.
text.Rotherham: So they stript him, and took his head, and his armour,—and sent throughout the land of the Philistines round about, to publish it to their idols, and to the people.
text.KJV: And when they had stripped him, they took his head, and his armour, and sent into the land of the Philistines round about, to carry tidings unto their idols, and to the people.
text.NABRE: They stripped him, and took his head and his armor; these they sent throughout the land of the Philistines to bring the good news to their idols and to the people.
text.DRB: And when they had spoiled him, and cut off his head, and stripped him of his armour, they sent into their land, that it should be carried about, & should be shewed in the temples of the idols, and to the people:

text.NRSV: They put his armor in the temple of their gods, and fastened his head in the temple of Dagon.
text.Rotherham: And they put his armour, in the house of their gods,—but, his skull, fastened they up, in the house of Dagon.
text.KJV: And they put his armour in the house of their gods, and fastened his head in the temple of Dagon.
text.NABRE: They put his armor in the temple of their gods, but his skull they impaled at the temple of Dagon.
text.DRB: & his armour they dedicated in the temple of their god, and the head they nailed up in the temple of Dagon.

text.NRSV: But when all Jabesh-gilead heard everything that the Philistines had done to Saul,
text.Rotherham: And, when all Jabesh-Gilead heard all that the Philistines had done to Saul,
text.KJV: And when all Jabesh–gilead heard all that the Philistines had done to Saul,
text.NABRE: Burial of Saul. When all the inhabitants of Jabesh-gilead heard all that the Philistines had done to Saul,
text.DRB: When the men of Jabes Galaad had heard this, to wit, all things that the Philisthiims had done upon Saul,

text.NRSV: all the valiant warriors got up and took the body of Saul and the bodies of his sons, and brought them to Jabesh. Then they buried their bones under the oak in Jabesh, and fasted seven days.
text.Rotherham: then rose up all the men of valour, and took away the corpse of Saul, and the corpses of his sons, and brought them to Jabesh,—and buried their bodies under the terebinth, in Jabesh, and fasted seven days.
text.KJV: They arose, all the valiant men, and took away the body of Saul, and the bodies of his sons, and brought them to Jabesh, and buried their bones under the oak in Jabesh, and fasted seven days.
text.NABRE: all their warriors set out, recovered the corpses of Saul and his sons, and brought them to Jabesh. They buried their bones under the oak of Jabesh, and fasted for seven days.
text.DRB: every one of the valiant men arose, and took the bodies of Saul and of his sons, and brought them into Jabes, & buried their bones under an oak, that was in Jabes, and they fasted seven days.

text.NRSV: So Saul died for his unfaithfulness; he was unfaithful to the Lord in that he did not keep the command of the Lord; moreover, he had consulted a medium, seeking guidance,
text.Rotherham: Thus died Saul, for his unfaithfulness wherewith he dealt unfaithfully against Yahweh, over the word of Yahweh, which he kept not,—and, also, in asking by necromancy when he desired to enquire;
text.KJV: So Saul died for his transgression which he committed against the Lord, even against the word of the Lord, which he kept not, and also for asking counsel of one that had a familiar spirit, to enquire of it;
text.NABRE: Thus Saul died because of his treason against the Lord in disobeying his word, and also because he had sought counsel from a ghost,
text.DRB: Saul therefore died for his iniquities, for that he transgressed the commandment of our Lord which he had commanded, and kept it not: yea and besides also consulted the Pythoness,

text.NRSV: and did not seek guidance from the Lord. Therefore the Lord put him to death and turned the kingdom over to David son of Jesse.
text.Rotherham: and enquired not of Yahweh, wherefore he slew him,—and turned round the kingdom unto David, son of Jesse.
text.KJV: And enquired not of the Lord: therefore he slew him, and turned the kingdom unto David the son of Jesse.
text.NABRE: rather than from the Lord. Therefore the Lord took his life, and turned his kingdom over to David, the son of Jesse.
text.DRB: and trusted not in our Lord: for the which he slew him, and transferred his Kingdom to David the son of Isai.

text.NRSV: Then all Israel gathered together to David at Hebron and said, “See, we are your bone and flesh.
text.Rotherham: Then did all Israel gather themselves together to David, unto Hebron, saying,—Lo! thy bone and thy flesh, are we.
text.KJV: Then all Israel gathered themselves to David unto Hebron, saying, Behold, we are thy bone and thy flesh.
text.NABRE: Chapter 11 David Is Made King. Then all Israel gathered around David in Hebron, and they said: “Look! We are your bone and your flesh.
text.DRB: All Israel therefore was gathered together to David in Hebron, saying: We are thy bone, and thy flesh.

text.NRSV: For some time now, even while Saul was king, it was you who commanded the army of Israel. The Lord your God said to you: It is you who shall be shepherd of my people Israel, you who shall be ruler over my people Israel.”
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, in time past even when Saul was king, thou, wast he that led out and brought in Israel,—and Yahweh thy God said unto thee, Thou, shalt shepherd my people Israel, Yea, thou, shalt be chief ruler, over my people Israel.
text.KJV: And moreover in time past, even when Saul was king, thou wast he that leddest out and broughtest in Israel: and the Lord thy God said unto thee, Thou shalt feed my people Israel, and thou shalt be ruler over my people Israel.
text.NABRE: In days past, when Saul was still the king, it was you who led Israel in all its battles. And now the Lord, your God, has said to you: You shall shepherd my people Israel; you shall be ruler over my people Israel.”
text.DRB: Yesterday also, and the day before, when Saul as yet reigned, thou wast he that didst lead out and lead in Israel: for to thee our Lord thy God said: Thou shalt feed my people Israel, and thou shalt be Prince over it.

text.NRSV: So all the elders of Israel came to the king at Hebron, and David made a covenant with them at Hebron before the Lord. And they anointed David king over Israel, according to the word of the Lord by Samuel.
text.Rotherham: Thus came all the elders of Israel unto the king, to Hebron, and David solemnised to them a covenant in Hebron, before Yahweh,—and they anointed David to be king over Israel, according to the word of Yahweh, through Samuel.
text.KJV: Therefore came all the elders of Israel to the king to Hebron; and David made a covenant with them in Hebron before the Lord; and they anointed David king over Israel, according to the word of the Lord by Samuel.
text.NABRE: Then all the elders of Israel came to the king at Hebron, and at Hebron David made a covenant with them in the presence of the Lord; and they anointed David king over Israel, in accordance with the word of the Lord given through Samuel.
text.DRB: All the ancients therefore of Israel came to the king into Hebron, and David entered into a league with them before our Lord: and they anointed him king over Israel, according to the word of our Lord, which he spake in the hand of Samuel.

text.NRSV: David and all Israel went to Jerusalem, that is Jebus, where the Jebusites were, the inhabitants of the land.
text.Rotherham: Then went David and all Israel to Jerusalem, the same, was Jebus,—where were the Jebusites, the inhabitants of the land.
text.KJV: And David and all Israel went to Jerusalem, which is Jebus; where the Jebusites were, the inhabitants of the land.
text.NABRE: Jerusalem Captured. Then David and all Israel went to Jerusalem, that is, Jebus, where the inhabitants of the land were called Jebusites.
text.DRB: David also went, and all Israel into Jerusalem. This is Jebus, where the Jebuseites were the inhabiters of the land.

text.NRSV: The inhabitants of Jebus said to David, “You will not come in here.” Nevertheless David took the stronghold of Zion, now the city of David.
text.Rotherham: And the inhabitants of Jebus said unto David, Thou shalt not come in hither,—But David captured the citadel of Zion, the same, is the city of David.
text.KJV: And the inhabitants of Jebus said to David, Thou shalt not come hither. Nevertheless David took the castle of Zion, which is the city of David.
text.NABRE: The inhabitants of Jebus said to David, “You shall not enter here.” David nevertheless captured the fortress of Zion, which is the City of David.
text.DRB: And they that dwelt in Jebus, said to David: Thou shalt not come in here. Moreover David took the tower of Sion, which is the City of David,

text.NRSV: David had said, “Whoever strikes the Jebusites first shall be chief and commander.” And Joab son of Zeruiah went up first, so he became chief.
text.Rotherham: And David said, Whosoever smiteth the Jebusites, first, shall become a chief, and a ruler,—So then Joab son of Zeruiah, went up first, and became a chief.
text.KJV: And David said, Whosoever smiteth the Jebusites first shall be chief and captain. So Joab the son of Zeruiah went first up, and was chief.
text.NABRE: David said, “Whoever strikes the Jebusites first shall be made chief and captain.” Joab, the son of Zeruiah, was the first to attack; and so he became chief.
text.DRB: & he said: Every one that shall among the first strike the Jebusite, shall be the Prince & chief captain. Joab therefore the son of Sarvia went up first, and was made the Prince.

text.NRSV: David resided in the stronghold; therefore it was called the city of David.
text.Rotherham: And David dwelt in the citadel,—for this cause, did they call it, The City of David.
text.KJV: And David dwelt in the castle; therefore they called it the city of David.
text.NABRE: David took up residence in the fortress, which therefore was called the City of David.
text.DRB: And David dwelt in the tower, and therefore it was called the City of David.

text.NRSV: He built the city all around, from the Millo in complete circuit; and Joab restored the rest of the city.
text.Rotherham: And he built the city round about, from Millo even as far as the circuit,—but, Joab, suffered the remainder of the city to live.
text.KJV: And he built the city round about, even from Millo round about: and Joab repaired the rest of the city.
text.NABRE: He built up the city on all sides, from the Millo all the way around, while Joab restored the rest of the city.
text.DRB: And he built the City round about from Mello unto a round compass, and Joab built the rest of the city.

text.NRSV: And David became greater and greater, for the Lord of hosts was with him.
text.Rotherham: Thus did David wax greater and yet greater,—and, Yahweh of Hosts, was with him.
text.KJV: So David waxed greater and greater: for the Lord of hosts was with him.
text.NABRE: David became ever more powerful, for the Lord of hosts was with him.
text.DRB: And David prospered going and increasing, and the Lord of hosts was with him.

text.NRSV: Now these are the chiefs of David’s warriors, who gave him strong support in his kingdom, together with all Israel, to make him king, according to the word of the Lord concerning Israel.
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the chiefs of the heroes, that pertained to David, who held strongly with him in his kingdom, with all Israel, to make him king,—according to the word of Yahweh, concerning Israel.
text.KJV: These also are the chief of the mighty men whom David had, who strengthened themselves with him in his kingdom, and with all Israel, to make him king, according to the word of the Lord concerning Israel.
text.NABRE: David’s Warriors. These were David’s chief warriors who, together with all Israel, supported him in his reign in order to make him king, according to the Lord ’s word concerning Israel.
text.DRB: These are the Princes of the valiant men of David, which holp him to be made king over all Israel according to the word of our Lord, which he spake to Israel

text.NRSV: This is the list of David’s warriors: Jashobeam son of a Hachmonite was chief of the Three; he wielded his spear against three hundred whom he killed at one time.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the number of the heroes who pertained to David,—Jashobeam son of a Hachmonite, chief of the thirty, the same, brandished his spear over three hundred—slain at one time.
text.KJV: And this is the number of the mighty men whom David had; Jashobeam, an Hachmonite, the chief of the captains: he lifted up his spear against three hundred slain by him at one time.
text.NABRE: Here is the list of David’s warriors: Ishbaal, the son of Hachamoni, chief of the Three. He brandished his spear over three hundred, whom he had slain in a single encounter.
text.DRB: And this is the number of David's strong ones: Jesbaam the son of Hachamoni Prince among thirty: this man lifted up his spear upon three hundred wounded at one time.

text.NRSV: And next to him among the three warriors was Eleazar son of Dodo, the Ahohite.
text.Rotherham: And, after him, was Eleazar son of Dodai, the Ahohite,—he, was among the three heroes;
text.KJV: And after him was Eleazar the son of Dodo, the Ahohite, who was one of the three mighties.
text.NABRE: Next to him was Eleazar, the son of Dodo the Ahohite, one of the Three warriors.
text.DRB: And after him Eleazar his uncle's son an Ahohite, who was among the three mighties.

text.NRSV: He was with David at Pas-dammim when the Philistines were gathered there for battle. There was a plot of ground full of barley. Now the people had fled from the Philistines,
text.Rotherham: he, was with David in Pas-dammim, where, the Philistines, were gathered together to battle, and there was a field-plot full of barley,—and, the people, fled, from before the Philistines;
text.KJV: He was with David at Pas–dammim, and there the Philistines were gathered together to battle, where was a parcel of ground full of barley; and the people fled from before the Philistines.
text.NABRE: He was with David at Pas-dammim, where the Philistines had massed for battle. There was a plot of land full of barley. The people were fleeing before the Philistines,
text.DRB: This was with David in Phesdomim, when the Philisthiims were gathered to that place into battle: and the field of that country was full of barley, and the people was fled from the face of the Philisthiims.

text.NRSV: but he and David stood their ground in the middle of the plot, defended it, and killed the Philistines; and the Lord saved them by a great victory.
text.Rotherham: so they took their stand in the midst of the plot and rescued it, and smote the Philistines,—and Yahweh wrought a great victory.
text.KJV: And they set themselves in the midst of that parcel, and delivered it, and slew the Philistines; and the Lord saved them by a great deliverance.
text.NABRE: but he took his stand in the middle of the plot, kept it safe, and cut down the Philistines. Thus the Lord brought about a great victory.
text.DRB: These stood in the midst of the field, and defended him: and when they had strucken the Philistheans, our Lord gave great health to his people.

text.NRSV: Three of the thirty chiefs went down to the rock to David at the cave of Adullam, while the army of the Philistines was encamped in the valley of Rephaim.
text.Rotherham: And three of the thirty chiefs went down upon the rock, unto David, into the cave of Adullam,—a host of Philistines, being encamped in the vale of Rephaim.
text.KJV: Now three of the thirty captains went down to the rock to David, into the cave of Adullam; and the host of the Philistines encamped in the valley of Rephaim.
text.NABRE: Three of the Thirty chiefs went down to the rock, to David, who was in the cave of Adullam while the Philistines were encamped in the valley of Rephaim.
text.DRB: And there went down three of the thirty Princes to a rock, wherein David was, to the cave of Odollam, when the Philisthiims had camped in the Vale Raphaim.

text.NRSV: David was then in the stronghold; and the garrison of the Philistines was then at Bethlehem.
text.Rotherham: Now, David, then was in the stronghold,—and, a garrison of Philistines, then was in Bethlehem.
text.KJV: And David was then in the hold, and the Philistines’ garrison was then at Beth–lehem.
text.NABRE: David was then in the stronghold, and a Philistine garrison was at Bethlehem.
text.DRB: Moreover David was in a hold, and the ward of the Philisthimes in Bethlehem.

text.NRSV: David said longingly, “O that someone would give me water to drink from the well of Bethlehem that is by the gate!”
text.Rotherham: And David longed, and said—Who will give me to drink of the water, out of the well of Bethlehem, that is within the gate?
text.KJV: And David longed, and said, Oh that one would give me drink of the water of the well of Beth–lehem, that is at the gate!
text.NABRE: David had a strong craving, and said, “If only someone would give me a drink of water from the cistern by the gate of Bethlehem!”
text.DRB: David therefore desired and said: O that some man would give me water of the cistern of Bethlehem, which is in the gate.

text.NRSV: Then the Three broke through the camp of the Philistines, and drew water from the well of Bethlehem that was by the gate, and they brought it to David. But David would not drink of it; he poured it out to the Lord,
text.Rotherham: And the three brake through the host of the Philistines, and drew water out of the well of Bethlehem, that was within the gate, and bare it, and brought it in unto David,—yet would not David drink it, but poured it out unto Yahweh;
text.KJV: And the three brake through the host of the Philistines, and drew water out of the well of Beth–lehem, that was by the gate, and took it, and brought it to David: but David would not drink of it, but poured it out to the Lord,
text.NABRE: Thereupon the Three broke through the encampment of the Philistines, drew water from the cistern by the gate of Bethlehem, and carried it back to David. But David refused to drink it. Instead, he poured it out to the Lord,
text.DRB: These three therefore went forward through the midst of the camp of the Philisthiims, and drew water of the cistern of Bethlehem, which was in the gate, & brought it to David to drink: who would not, but rather offered it to our Lord,

text.NRSV: and said, “My God forbid that I should do this. Can I drink the blood of these men? For at the risk of their lives they brought it.” Therefore he would not drink it. The three warriors did these things.
text.Rotherham: and said—Far be it from me, of my God, that I should do this! The blood of these men, shall I drink, with their lives? For, with their lives, have they brought it! So he would not drink it. These things, did the three heroes.
text.KJV: And said, My God forbid it me, that I should do this thing: shall I drink the blood of these men that have put their lives in jeopardy? for with the jeopardy of their lives they brought it. Therefore he would not drink it. These things did these three mightiest.
text.NABRE: saying, “God forbid that I should do such a thing! Could I drink the blood of these men who risked their lives? For at the risk of their lives they brought it.” So he refused to drink it. Such deeds as these the Three warriors performed.
