text.DRB: saying: God forbid that in the sight of my God I should do this, and should drink the blood of these men: because in the peril of their lives they have brought me the water. And for this cause he would not drink. These things did the three most valiants.

text.NRSV: Now Abishai, the brother of Joab, was chief of the Thirty. With his spear he fought against three hundred and killed them, and won a name beside the Three.
text.Rotherham: And, Abishai brother of Joab—he, was the chief of three, he, having brandished his spear against three hundred—who were slain,—and so, he, had a name among three.
text.KJV: And Abishai the brother of Joab, he was chief of the three: for lifting up his spear against three hundred, he slew them, and had a name among the three.
text.NABRE: Abishai, the brother of Joab, was the chief of the Thirty; he brandished his spear over three hundred, whom he had slain. He made a name beside the Three,
text.DRB: Abisai also the brother of Joab he was Prince of three, and he lifted up his spear against three hundred wounded, and he was among three most renowned,

text.NRSV: He was the most renowned of the Thirty, and became their commander; but he did not attain to the Three.
text.Rotherham: Of the three in the second rank, was he honourable, therefore became he their captain,—though, unto the [first] three, did he not attain.
text.KJV: Of the three, he was more honourable than the two; for he was their captain: howbeit he attained not to the first three.
text.NABRE: but was twice as famous as any of the Thirty, becoming their leader. However, he did not attain to the Three.
text.DRB: and among the second three he the noble one, and Prince of them: but yet unto the three first he raught not.

text.NRSV: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was a valiant man of Kabzeel, a doer of great deeds; he struck down two sons of Ariel of Moab. He also went down and killed a lion in a pit on a day when snow had fallen.
text.Rotherham: Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, son of a man of valour, hero of many a deed, from Kabziel—he, smote the two [sons] of Ariel of Moab, he, also went down and smote a lion in the midst of a pit, in time of snow;
text.KJV: Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, the son of a valiant man of Kabzeel, who had done many acts; he slew two lionlike men of Moab: also he went down and slew a lion in a pit in a snowy day.
text.NABRE: Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, a valiant man of mighty deeds, from Kabzeel, killed the two sons of Ariel of Moab. Also, he went down and killed the lion in the cistern on a snowy day.
text.DRB: Banaias the son of Joiada a most valiant man, of Cabseel: who had done many facts, he struck the two Ariel of Moab: & he went down, and slew the lion in the midst of the cistern in the time of snow.

text.NRSV: And he killed an Egyptian, a man of great stature, five cubits tall. The Egyptian had in his hand a spear like a weaver’s beam; but Benaiah went against him with a staff, snatched the spear out of the Egyptian’s hand, and killed him with his own spear.
text.Rotherham: he, also smote an Egyptian, a man of [great] stature, five cubits, and, in the hand of the Egyptian, was a spear like a weaver’s beam, so he went down unto him with a staff,—and wrested the spear out of the hand of the Egyptian, and slew him with his spear.
text.KJV: And he slew an Egyptian, a man of great stature, five cubits high; and in the Egyptian’s hand was a spear like a weaver’s beam; and he went down to him with a staff, and plucked the spear out of the Egyptian’s hand, and slew him with his own spear.
text.NABRE: He likewise slew the Egyptian, a huge man five cubits tall. The Egyptian carried a spear that was like a weaver’s beam, but Benaiah came against him with a staff; he wrested the spear from the Egyptian’s hand, and killed him with that spear.
text.DRB: And he struck the Ægyptian, whose stature was of five cubits, and which had a spear as the weaver's beam: he therefore went down to him with a rod, and by force took away the spear that he held in his hand, and slew him with his own spear.

text.NRSV: Such were the things Benaiah son of Jehoiada did, and he won a name beside the three warriors.
text.Rotherham: These things, did Benaiah son of Jehoiada, so, he, had a name among three heroes.
text.KJV: These things did Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and had the name among the three mighties.
text.NABRE: Such deeds as these Benaiah, the son of Jehoiada, performed, and he made a name beside the Three warriors,
text.DRB: These things did Banaias the son of Joiada, who was among the three valiants most renowned,

text.NRSV: He was renowned among the Thirty, but he did not attain to the Three. And David put him in charge of his bodyguard.
text.Rotherham: Among the thirty, lo! honourable, was he, but, unto the [first] three, did he not attain,—howbeit David set him over his council.
text.KJV: Behold, he was honourable among the thirty, but attained not to the first three: and David set him over his guard.
text.NABRE: but was more famous than any of the Thirty. However, he did not attain to the Three. David put him in charge of his bodyguard.
text.DRB: the first among thirty, but yet the three he raught not: and David made him of his counsel.

text.NRSV: The warriors of the armies were Asahel brother of Joab, Elhanan son of Dodo of Bethlehem,
text.Rotherham: Now, the heroes of the forces, were,—Asahel, brother of Joab, Elhanan, son of Dodo, of Bethlehem;
text.KJV: Also the valiant men of the armies were, Asahel the brother of Joab, Elhanan the son of Dodo of Beth–lehem,
text.NABRE: Also these warriors: Asahel, the brother of Joab; Elhanan, son of Dodo, from Bethlehem;
text.DRB: Moreover the most valiant men in the army, Asahel the brother of Joab, and Elchanan the son of his uncle of Bethlehem,

text.NRSV: Shammoth of Harod, Helez the Pelonite,
text.Rotherham: Shammoth, the Harorite, Helez the Paltite;
text.KJV: Shammoth the Harorite, Helez the Pelonite,
text.NABRE: Shammoth, from En-harod; Helez, from Beth-pelet;
text.DRB: Sammoth an Arorite, Helles a Phalonite,

text.NRSV: Ira son of Ikkesh of Tekoa, Abiezer of Anathoth,
text.Rotherham: Ira, son of Ikkesh, the Tekoite, Abiezer, the Anathothite;
text.KJV: Ira the son of Ikkesh the Tekoite, Abi–ezer the Antothite,
text.NABRE: Ira, son of Ikkesh, from Tekoa; Abiezer, from Anathoth;
text.DRB: Ira the son of Acces a Thecaite, Abiezer an Anathothite,

text.NRSV: Sibbecai the Hushathite, Ilai the Ahohite,
text.Rotherham: Sibbecai, the Hushathite, Ilai, the Ahohite;
text.KJV: Sibbecai the Hushathite, Ilai the Ahohite,
text.NABRE: Sibbecai, from Husha; Ilai, from Ahoh;
text.DRB: Sobbochai an Husathite, Ilai an Ahohite,

text.NRSV: Maharai of Netophah, Heled son of Baanah of Netophah,
text.Rotherham: Maharai, the Netophathite, Heled, son of Baanah, the Netophathite;
text.KJV: Maharai the Netophathite, Heled the son of Baanah the Netophathite,
text.NABRE: Maharai, from Netophah; Heled, son of Baanah, from Netophah;
text.DRB: Maharai a Netophathite, Heled the son of Baana a Netophathite.

text.NRSV: Ithai son of Ribai of Gibeah of the Benjaminites, Benaiah of Pirathon,
text.Rotherham: Ithai, son of Ribai, of Gibeah, of the sons of Benjamin,—Benaiah, the Pirathonite;
text.KJV: Ithai the son of Ribai of Gibeah, that pertained to the children of Benjamin, Benaiah the Pirathonite,
text.NABRE: Ithai, son of Ribai, from Gibeah of Benjamin; Benaiah, from Pirathon;
text.DRB: Ethai the son of Ribai of Gabaath the children of Benjamin, Banaia a Pharatonite,

text.NRSV: Hurai of the wadis of Gaash, Abiel the Arbathite,
text.Rotherham: Hurai, of the torrents of Gaash, Abiel the Arbathite;
text.KJV: Hurai of the brooks of Gaash, Abiel the Arbathite,
text.NABRE: Hurai, from Nahale-gaash; Abiel, from Beth-arabah;
text.DRB: Hurai of the Torrent Gaas, Abiel an Arbathite, Azmoth a Bauramite, Eliaba a Salabonite.

text.NRSV: Azmaveth of Bahurim, Eliahba of Shaalbon,
text.Rotherham: Azmaveth, the Baharumite, Eliahba, the Shaalbonite;
text.KJV: Azmaveth the Baharumite, Eliahba the Shaalbonite,
text.NABRE: Azmaveth, from Bahurim; Eliahba, from Shaalbon;
text.DRB: The sons of Assem a Gezonite, Jonathan the son of Sage an Ararite,

text.NRSV: Hashem the Gizonite, Jonathan son of Shagee the Hararite,
text.Rotherham: the sons of Hashem, the Gizonite, Jonathan, son of Shageh, the Hararite;
text.KJV: The sons of Hashem the Gizonite, Jonathan the son of Shage the Hararite,
text.NABRE: Jashen the Gunite; Jonathan, son of Shagee the Hararite;
text.DRB: Ahiam the son of Sachar an Ararite,

text.NRSV: Ahiam son of Sachar the Hararite, Eliphal son of Ur,
text.Rotherham: Ahiam, son of Sacar, the Hararite, Eliphal, son of Ur;
text.KJV: Ahiam the son of Sacar the Hararite, Eliphal the son of Ur,
text.NABRE: Ahiam, son of Sachar the Hararite; Elipheleth, son of
text.DRB: Eliphal the son of Ur,

text.NRSV: Hepher the Mecherathite, Ahijah the Pelonite,
text.Rotherham: Hepher, the Mecherathite, Ahijah the Pelonite;
text.KJV: Hepher the Mecherathite, Ahijah the Pelonite,
text.NABRE: Ahasbai, from Beth-maacah; Ahijah, from Gilo;
text.DRB: Hepher a Mecherathite, Ahia Phelonite,

text.NRSV: Hezro of Carmel, Naarai son of Ezbai,
text.Rotherham: Hezro, the Carmelite, Naarai, son of Ezbai;
text.KJV: Hezro the Carmelite, Naarai the son of Ezbai,
text.NABRE: Hezro, from Carmel; Naarai, the son of Ezbai;
text.DRB: Hesro a Carmelite, Naarai the son of Azbai,

text.NRSV: Joel the brother of Nathan, Mibhar son of Hagri,
text.Rotherham: Joel, brother of Nathan, Mibhar son of Hagri;
text.KJV: Joel the brother of Nathan, Mibhar the son of Haggeri,
text.NABRE: Joel, brother of Nathan, from Rehob, the Gadite;
text.DRB: Joel the brother of Nathan, Mibahar the son of Agarai.

text.NRSV: Zelek the Ammonite, Naharai of Beeroth, the armor-bearer of Joab son of Zeruiah,
text.Rotherham: Zelek, the Ammonite,—Naharai, the Berothite, the armour-bearer of Joab son of Zeruiah;
text.KJV: Zelek the Ammonite, Naharai the Berothite, the armourbearer of Joab the son of Zeruiah,
text.NABRE: Zelek the Ammonite; Naharai, from Beeroth, the armor-bearer of Joab, son of Zeruiah;
text.DRB: Selec an Ammonite, Naharai a Berothite the armour-bearer of Joab the son of Sarvia,

text.NRSV: Ira the Ithrite, Gareb the Ithrite,
text.Rotherham: Ira, the Ithrite, Gareb, the Ithrite;
text.KJV: Ira the Ithrite, Gareb the Ithrite,
text.NABRE: Ira, from Jattir; Gareb, from Jattir;
text.DRB: Ira a Jethreite, Gareb a Jethreite,

text.NRSV: Uriah the Hittite, Zabad son of Ahlai,
text.Rotherham: Uriah, the Hittite, Zabad son of Ahlai;
text.KJV: Uriah the Hittite, Zabad the son of Ahlai,
text.NABRE: Uriah the Hittite; Zabad, son of Ahlai,
text.DRB: Urias the Hetheite, Zabad the son of Oholi,

text.NRSV: Adina son of Shiza the Reubenite, a leader of the Reubenites, and thirty with him,
text.Rotherham: Adina, son of Shiza, the Reubenite, a chief of the Reubenites and over the thirty;
text.KJV: Adina the son of Shiza the Reubenite, a captain of the Reubenites, and thirty with him,
text.NABRE: and, in addition to the Thirty, Adina, son of Shiza, the Reubenite, chief of the tribe of Reuben;
text.DRB: Adina the son of Siza a Rubenite the Prince of the Rubenites, and with him thirty:

text.NRSV: Hanan son of Maacah, and Joshaphat the Mithnite,
text.Rotherham: Hanan, son of Maacah, and Joshaphat, the Mithnite;
text.KJV: Hanan the son of Maachah, and Joshaphat the Mithnite,
text.NABRE: Hanan, son of Maacah; Joshaphat the Mithnite;
text.DRB: Hanan the son of Maacha, and Josaphat a Mathanite,

text.NRSV: Uzzia the Ashterathite, Shama and Jeiel sons of Hotham the Aroerite,
text.Rotherham: Uzzia, the Ashterathite,—Shama, and Jeiel, sons of Hotham, the Aroerite;
text.KJV: Uzzia the Ashterathite, Shama and Jehiel the sons of Hothan the Aroerite,
text.NABRE: Uzzia, the Ashterathite; Shama and Jeiel, sons of Hotham, from Aroer;
text.DRB: Ozia an Astarothite, Samma, and Jehiel the sons of Hotham an Arorite,

text.NRSV: Jediael son of Shimri, and his brother Joha the Tizite,
text.Rotherham: Jediael, son of Shimri, and Joha, his brother, the Tizite;
text.KJV: Jediael the son of Shimri, and Joha his brother, the Tizite,
text.NABRE: Jediael, son of Shimri, and Joha, his brother, the Tizite;
text.DRB: Jedihel the son of Zamri, and Joha his brother a Thosaite,

text.NRSV: Eliel the Mahavite, and Jeribai and Joshaviah sons of Elnaam, and Ithmah the Moabite,
text.Rotherham: Eliel, the Mahavite, and Jeribai and Joshaviah, sons of Elnaam,—and Ithmah the Moabite;
text.KJV: Eliel the Mahavite, and Jeribai, and Joshaviah, the sons of Elnaam, and Ithmah the Moabite,
text.NABRE: Eliel the Mahavite; Jeribai and Joshaviah, sons of Elnaam; Ithmah, from Moab;
text.DRB: Eliel a Mahumite, and Jeribai, and Josaia the sons of Elnaim, and Jethma a Moabite, Eliel, and Obed, and Jasiel of Masobia.

text.NRSV: Eliel, and Obed, and Jaasiel the Mezobaite.
text.Rotherham: Eliel, and Obed, and Jaasiel, of Zobah.
text.KJV: Eliel, and Obed, and Jasiel the Mesobaite.
text.NABRE: Eliel, Obed, and Jaasiel the Mezobian.

text.NRSV: The following are those who came to David at Ziklag, while he could not move about freely because of Saul son of Kish; they were among the warriors who helped him in war.
text.Rotherham: Now these, are they who came unto David, to Ziklag, he yet being shut up, because of Saul son of Kish,—and, they, were among the heroes, helpers in the war;
text.KJV: Now these are they that came to David to Ziklag, while he yet kept himself close because of Saul the son of Kish: and they were among the mighty men, helpers of the war.
text.NABRE: Chapter 12 David’s Early Followers. The following men came to David in Ziklag while he was still under banishment from Saul, son of Kish; they, too, were among the warriors who helped him in his battles.
text.DRB: These also came to David into Siceleg, when as yet he fled from Saul the son of Cis, the which were most valiant and excellent warriors,

text.NRSV: They were archers who could shoot arrows and sling stones with either the right hand or the left; they were Benjaminites, Saul’s kindred.
text.Rotherham: armed with the bow, using right hand or left with stones, and with arrows in the bow,—of the brethren of Saul, out of Benjamin:
text.KJV: They were armed with bows, and could use both the right hand and the left in hurling stones and shooting arrows out of a bow, even of Saul’s brethren of Benjamin.
text.NABRE: They were archers who could use either the right or the left hand, both in slinging stones and in shooting arrows with the bow. They were some of Saul’s kinsmen, from Benjamin.
text.DRB: drawing the bow, & hurling with both hands stones in slings, & shooting arrows directly: of the brethren of Saul of Benjamin.

text.NRSV: The chief was Ahiezer, then Joash, both sons of Shemaah of Gibeah; also Jeziel and Pelet sons of Azmaveth; Beracah, Jehu of Anathoth,
text.Rotherham: the chief, Ahiezer, and Joash, sons of Shemaah the Gibeathite, and Jeziel and Pelet, sons of Azmaveth,—and Beracah, and Jehu the Anathothite;
text.KJV: The chief was Ahiezer, then Joash, the sons of Shemaah the Gibeathite; and Jeziel, and Pelet, the sons of Azmaveth; and Berachah, and Jehu the Antothite,
text.NABRE: Ahiezer was their chief, along with Joash, both sons of Shemaah of Gibeah; also Jeziel and Pelet, sons of Azmaveth; Beracah; Jehu, from Anathoth;
text.DRB: The Prince Ahiezer, and Joas the sons of Samaa a Gabaathite, and Jaziel, and Phallet the sons of Azmoth, and Beracha, and Jehu an Anothothite.

text.NRSV: Ishmaiah of Gibeon, a warrior among the Thirty and a leader over the Thirty; Jeremiah, Jahaziel, Johanan, Jozabad of Gederah,
text.Rotherham: and Ishmaiah, the Gibeonite, a hero among the thirty, and over the thirty,—and Jeremiah and Jahaziel, and Johanan, and Jozabad, the Gederathite;
text.KJV: And Ismaiah the Gibeonite, a mighty man among the thirty, and over the thirty; and Jeremiah, and Jahaziel, and Johanan, and Josabad the Gederathite,
text.NABRE: Ishmaiah the Gibeonite, a warrior among the Thirty, and over the Thirty;
text.DRB: Samaias also a Gabaonite the most valiant amongst the thirty and above the thirty. Jeremias, and Jeheziel, and Johanan, & Jezabad a Gaderothite.

text.NRSV: Eluzai, Jerimoth, Bealiah, Shemariah, Shephatiah the Haruphite;
text.Rotherham: Eluzai and Jerimoth and Bealiah and Shemariah, and Shephatiah, the Haruphite;
text.KJV: Eluzai, and Jerimoth, and Bealiah, and Shemariah, and Shephatiah the Haruphite,
text.NABRE: Jeremiah; Jahaziel; Johanan; Jozabad from Gederah;
text.DRB: And Eluzai, and Jerimuth, and Baalia, & Samaria, and Saphatia an Haruphite.

text.NRSV: Elkanah, Isshiah, Azarel, Joezer, and Jashobeam, the Korahites;
text.Rotherham: Elkanah and Isshiah, and Azarel, and Joezer and Jashobeam, the Korahites;
text.KJV: Elkanah, and Jesiah, and Azareel, and Joezer, and Jashobeam, the Korhites,
text.NABRE: Eluzai; Jerimoth; Bealiah; Shemariah; Shephatiah the Haruphite;
text.DRB: Elcana, and Jesia, and Azareel, and Joezer, and Jesbaam of Carehim:

text.NRSV: and Joelah and Zebadiah, sons of Jeroham of Gedor.
text.Rotherham: and Joelah and Zebadiah, sons of Jeroham, of Gedor.
text.KJV: And Joelah, and Zebadiah, the sons of Jeroham of Gedor.
text.NABRE: Elkanah, Isshiah, Azarel, Joezer, and Jashobeam, who were Korahites;
text.DRB: Joela also, and Zabadia the sons of Jeroham of Gedor.

text.NRSV: From the Gadites there went over to David at the stronghold in the wilderness mighty and experienced warriors, expert with shield and spear, whose faces were like the faces of lions, and who were swift as gazelles on the mountains:
text.Rotherham: And, of the Gadites, there separated themselves unto David, to the stronghold towards the desert, heroes of valour, men of war, for battle, men that could handle shield and spear,—and, faces of lions, were their faces, and, like gazelles upon the mountains, were they, for swiftness:
text.KJV: And of the Gadites there separated themselves unto David into the hold to the wilderness men of might, and men of war fit for the battle, that could handle shield and buckler, whose faces were like the faces of lions, and were as swift as the roes upon the mountains;
text.NABRE: Joelah and Zebadiah, sons of Jeroham, from Gedor.
text.DRB: Yea and of Gaddi also there fled to David, when he lay hid in the desert most valiant men, & the best warriors, holding shield and spear: their faces as the faces of a lion, & swift as the roe-bucks on the mountains:

text.NRSV: Ezer the chief, Obadiah second, Eliab third,
text.Rotherham: Ezer, the chief, Obadiah, the second,—Eliab, the third;
text.KJV: Ezer the first, Obadiah the second, Eliab the third,
text.NABRE: Some of the Gadites also went over to David when he was at the stronghold in the wilderness. They were valiant warriors, experienced soldiers equipped with shield and spear, fearsome as lions, swift as gazelles on the mountains.
text.DRB: Ezer the Prince, Obdias the second, Eliab the third,

text.NRSV: Mishmannah fourth, Jeremiah fifth,
text.Rotherham: Mashmannah, the fourth, Jeremiah, the fifth;
text.KJV: Mishmannah the fourth, Jeremiah the fifth,
text.NABRE: Ezer was their chief, Obadiah was second, Eliab third,
text.DRB: Masmana the fourth, Jeremias the fifth,

text.NRSV: Attai sixth, Eliel seventh,
text.Rotherham: Attai, the sixth, Eliel the seventh;
text.KJV: Attai the sixth, Eliel the seventh,
text.NABRE: Mishmannah fourth, Jeremiah fifth,
text.DRB: Ethi the sixth, Eliel the seventh,

text.NRSV: Johanan eighth, Elzabad ninth,
text.Rotherham: Johanan, the eighth, Elzabad, the ninth;
text.KJV: Johanan the eighth, Elzabad the ninth,
text.NABRE: Attai sixth, Eliel seventh,
text.DRB: Johanan the eight, Elzebad the ninth,

text.NRSV: Jeremiah tenth, Machbannai eleventh.
text.Rotherham: Jeremiah, the tenth, Machbannai, the eleventh.
text.KJV: Jeremiah the tenth, Machbanai the eleventh.
text.NABRE: Johanan eighth, Elzabad ninth,
text.DRB: Jeremias the tenth, Machbanai the eleventh,

text.NRSV: These Gadites were officers of the army, the least equal to a hundred and the greatest to a thousand.
text.Rotherham: These, were of the sons of Gad, chiefs of the host,—one to a hundred, the least, and, the greatest, to a thousand.
text.KJV: These were of the sons of Gad, captains of the host: one of the least was over an hundred, and the greatest over a thousand.
text.NABRE: Jeremiah tenth, and Machbannai eleventh.
text.DRB: these of the children of Gad were the Princes of his army. The meanest was captain over an hundred soldiers, & the greatest, over a thousand.

text.NRSV: These are the men who crossed the Jordan in the first month, when it was overflowing all its banks, and put to flight all those in the valleys, to the east and to the west.
text.Rotherham: These, are they who passed over the Jordan, in the first month, when, it, was full, over all its banks,—and they put to flight all them of the vales, to the east and to the west.
text.KJV: These are they that went over Jordan in the first month, when it had overflown all his banks; and they put to flight all them of the valleys, both toward the east, and toward the west.
text.NABRE: These Gadites were army commanders, the lesser over hundreds and the greater over thousands.
text.DRB: These are they which passed Jordan the first month, when it used to flow over his banks: & they chased away all that dwelt in the valleys toward the east quarter and the west.

text.NRSV: Some Benjaminites and Judeans came to the stronghold to David.
text.Rotherham: And there came, of the sons of Benjamin, and Judah, as far as the stronghold, unto David.
text.KJV: And there came of the children of Benjamin and Judah to the hold unto David.
text.NABRE: It was they who crossed over the Jordan in the first month, when it was overflowing both its banks, and chased away all who were in the valleys to the east and to the west.
text.DRB: And there came also of Benjamin, & of Juda to the hold, wherein David abode.

text.NRSV: David went out to meet them and said to them, “If you have come to me in friendship to help me, then my heart will be knit to you; but if you have come to betray me to my adversaries, though my hands have done no wrong, then may the God of our ancestors see and give judgment.”
text.Rotherham: And David went forth to meet them, and responded, and said to them, If, peaceably, ye are come unto me, to help me, I shall have towards you, a heart for unity,—but, if to betray me to mine adversaries, there being no violence in my hands, may the God of our fathers see and rebuke!
text.KJV: And David went out to meet them, and answered and said unto them, If ye be come peaceably unto me to help me, mine heart shall be knit unto you: but if ye be come to betray me to mine enemies, seeing there is no wrong in mine hands, the God of our fathers look thereon, and rebuke it.
text.NABRE: Some Benjaminites and Judahites also came to David at the stronghold.
text.DRB: And David went out to meet them, & said: If you be come peaceably to me for to help me, my heart be joined to you, but if you lie in wait against me for my adversaries, whereas I have no iniquity in my hands, the God of our fathers see, and judge.

text.NRSV: Then the spirit came upon Amasai, chief of the Thirty, and he said, “We are yours, O David; and with you, O son of Jesse! Peace, peace to you, and peace to the one who helps you! For your God is the one who helps you.” Then David received them, and made them officers of his troops.
text.Rotherham: Then, the spirit, clothed Amasai, chief of the thirty, Thine, O David, Yea, with, thee, O son of Jesse! Prosperity, prosperity to thee, And prosperity to thy helpers, For thy God, hath helped thee: So David accepted them, and set them among the chiefs of the band.
text.KJV: Then the spirit came upon Amasai, who was chief of the captains, and he said, Thine are we, David, and on thy side, thou son of Jesse: peace, peace be unto thee, and peace be to thine helpers; for thy God helpeth thee. Then David received them, and made them captains of the band.
text.NABRE: David went out to meet them and addressed them in these words: “If you come peacefully, to help me, I am of a mind to have you join me. But if you have come to betray me to my enemies though my hands have done no wrong, may the God of our ancestors see and punish you.”
text.DRB: But the spirit came on Amasai the Prince among thirty, & he said: We are thine, o David, & with thee, o son of Isai: peace, peace be to thee, and peace to thy helpers, for thy God helpeth thee. David therefore received them, and appointed them Princes of the band.

text.NRSV: Some Manassites deserted to David when he came with the Philistines for the battle against Saul. (Yet he did not help them, for the lords of the Philistines took counsel and sent him away, saying, “He will desert to his master Saul at the cost of our heads.”)
text.Rotherham: And, of Manasseh, there fell away unto David, when he came with the Philistines against Saul to battle, but they helped them not,—for the lords of the Philistines advisedly sent him away, saying, At the price of our heads, he might fall away to his master, Saul.
text.KJV: And there fell some of Manasseh to David, when he came with the Philistines against Saul to battle: but they helped them not: for the lords of the Philistines upon advisement sent him away, saying, He will fall to his master Saul to the jeopardy of our heads.
text.NABRE: Then a spirit clothed Amasai, the chief of the Thirty, and he answered David: “We are yours, O David, we are with you, son of Jesse. Peace, peace to you, and peace to him who helps you; may your God be your helper!” So David received them and placed them among the leaders of his troops.
text.DRB: Moreover of Manasses there fled to David, when he came with the Philisthiims against Saul to fight, and he fought not with them: because the Princes of the Philisthiims taking counsel sent him back, saying: with the peril of our head will he return to his lord Saul.

text.NRSV: As he went to Ziklag these Manassites deserted to him: Adnah, Jozabad, Jediael, Michael, Jozabad, Elihu, and Zillethai, chiefs of thousands in Manasseh.
text.Rotherham: When he went into Ziklag, there fell away unto him, of Manasseh, Adnah and Jozabad, and Jediael and Michael and Jozabad, and Elihu and Zillethai,—chiefs of the thousands that pertained to Manasseh;
text.KJV: As he went to Ziklag, there fell to him of Manasseh, Adnah, and Jozabad, and Jediael, and Michael, and Jozabad, and Elihu, and Zilthai, captains of the thousands that were of Manasseh.
text.NABRE: Men from Manasseh also deserted to David when he came with the Philistines to battle against Saul. However, he did not help the Philistines, for their lords took counsel and sent him home, saying, “At the cost of our heads he will desert to his master Saul.”
text.DRB: Therefore when he returned into Siceleg, there fled to him of Manasses, Ednas, and Jozabab, and Jedihel, and Michael, and Ednas, & Jozabad, and Eliu, and Salathi, the Princes of a thousand in Manasses.

text.NRSV: They helped David against the band of raiders, for they were all mighty warriors and were commanders in the army.
text.Rotherham: and, they, helped with David, against the [marauding] band, for, heroes of valour, were they all,—and they became captains in the host;
text.KJV: And they helped David against the band of the rovers: for they were all mighty men of valour, and were captains in the host.
text.NABRE: As he was returning to Ziklag, therefore, these deserted to him from Manasseh: Adnah, Jozabad, Jediael, Michael, Jozabad, Elihu, and Zillethai, chiefs of thousands of Manasseh.
text.DRB: These did aid David against the rovers: for they were all most valiant men, & were made commanders in the army.

text.NRSV: Indeed from day to day people kept coming to David to help him, until there was a great army, like an army of God.
text.Rotherham: for, from day to day, there came unto David, to help him,—until it was a great camp, like a camp of God.
text.KJV: For at that time day by day there came to David to help him, until it was a great host, like the host of God.
text.NABRE: They helped David by taking charge of his troops, for they were all warriors and became commanders of his army.
text.DRB: Yea & there came every day to David to help him, till it became a great number, as it were the army of God.

text.NRSV: These are the numbers of the divisions of the armed troops who came to David in Hebron to turn the kingdom of Saul over to him, according to the word of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the numbers of the chiefs armed for war, they came unto David, to Hebron,—to turn round the kingdom of Saul unto him, according to the bidding of Yahweh:
text.KJV: And these are the numbers of the bands that were ready armed to the war, and came to David to Hebron, to turn the kingdom of Saul to him, according to the word of the Lord.
text.NABRE: And from day to day men kept coming to David’s help until there was a vast encampment, like God’s own encampment.
text.DRB: This also is the number of the commanders of the army, which came to David, when he was in Hebron, to transfer the kingdom of Saul to him, according to the word of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Of the Judeans carrying shield and spear, six thousand eight hundred armed troops.
text.Rotherham: Sons of Judah, bearers of shield and spear, six thousand and eight hundred, armed for war.
text.KJV: The children of Judah that bare shield and spear were six thousand and eight hundred, ready armed to the war.
text.NABRE: The Assembly at Hebron. This is the muster of the detachments of armed troops that came to David at Hebron to bring Saul’s kingdom over to him, as the Lord had ordained.
text.DRB: The children of Juda bearing shield and spear, six thousand eight hundred well appointed to battle.

text.NRSV: Of the Simeonites, mighty warriors, seven thousand one hundred.
text.Rotherham: Of sons of Simeon, heroes of valour for the war, seven thousand, and one hundred.
text.KJV: Of the children of Simeon, mighty men of valour for the war, seven thousand and one hundred.
text.NABRE: Judahites bearing shields and spears: six thousand eight hundred armed troops.
text.DRB: Of the children of Simeon valiant men to fight, seven thousand one hundred.

text.NRSV: Of the Levites, four thousand six hundred.
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Levi, four thousand, and six hundred.
text.KJV: Of the children of Levi four thousand and six hundred.
text.NABRE: Of the Simeonites, warriors fit for battle: seven thousand one hundred.
text.DRB: Of the children of Levi, four thousand six hundred.

text.NRSV: Jehoiada, leader of the house of Aaron, and with him three thousand seven hundred.
text.Rotherham: And Jehoiada, was chief ruler for Aaron,—and, with him, three thousand and seven hundred;
text.KJV: And Jehoiada was the leader of the Aaronites, and with him were three thousand and seven hundred;
text.NABRE: Of the Levites: four thousand six hundred,
text.DRB: Joiada also Prince of the stock of Aaron, and with him three thousand seven hundred.

text.NRSV: Zadok, a young warrior, and twenty-two commanders from his own ancestral house.
text.Rotherham: and Zadok, a young man, a hero of valour,—and the house of his father, captains twenty and two.
text.KJV: And Zadok, a young man mighty of valour, and of his father’s house twenty and two captains.
text.NABRE: along with Jehoiada, leader of the line of Aaron, with another three thousand seven hundred,
text.DRB: Sadoc also a young man of goodly towardness, and the house of his father, Princes twenty two.

text.NRSV: Of the Benjaminites, the kindred of Saul, three thousand, of whom the majority had continued to keep their allegiance to the house of Saul.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Benjamin, brethren of Saul, three thousand,—and, hitherto, the greater part of them, had been keeping the charge of the house of Saul.
text.KJV: And of the children of Benjamin, the kindred of Saul, three thousand: for hitherto the greatest part of them had kept the ward of the house of Saul.
text.NABRE: and Zadok, a young warrior, with twenty-two princes of his father’s house.
text.DRB: And of the children of Benjamin the brethren of Saul, three thousand: for a great part of them as yet followed the house of Saul.

text.NRSV: Of the Ephraimites, twenty thousand eight hundred, mighty warriors, famous in their ancestral houses.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Ephraim, twenty thousand, and eight hundred,—heroes of valour, men of renown, to their ancestral house.
text.KJV: And of the children of Ephraim twenty thousand and eight hundred, mighty men of valour, famous throughout the house of their fathers.
text.NABRE: Of the Benjaminites, the kinsmen of Saul: three thousand—until this time, most of them had kept their allegiance to the house of Saul.
text.DRB: Moreover of the children of Ephraim twenty thousand eight hundred valiant of strength, men renowned in their kindreds.

text.NRSV: Of the half-tribe of Manasseh, eighteen thousand, who were expressly named to come and make David king.
text.Rotherham: And, of the half tribe of Manasseh, eighteen thousand, who were distinguished by name, coming in to make David king.
text.KJV: And of the half tribe of Manasseh eighteen thousand, which were expressed by name, to come and make David king.
text.NABRE: Of the Ephraimites: twenty thousand eight hundred warriors, men renowned in their ancestral houses.
text.DRB: And of the half tribe of Manasses, eighteen thousand, every one by their names, came to make David king.

text.NRSV: Of Issachar, those who had understanding of the times, to know what Israel ought to do, two hundred chiefs, and all their kindred under their command.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Issachar, such as were of good understanding of the times, to know what Israel, should do, their chiefs, were two hundred, and, all their brethren, were at their bidding.
text.KJV: And of the children of Issachar, which were men that had understanding of the times, to know what Israel ought to do; the heads of them were two hundred; and all their brethren were at their commandment.
text.NABRE: Of the half-tribe of Manasseh: eighteen thousand, designated by name to come and make David king.
text.DRB: Also of the children of Issachar men of understanding, that knew all times to command what Israel should do, Princes two hundred: and all the rest of the tribe did follow their counsel.

text.NRSV: Of Zebulun, fifty thousand seasoned troops, equipped for battle with all the weapons of war, to help David with singleness of purpose.
text.Rotherham: Of Zebulun—such as were ready to go forth in host, expert for battle with all weapons of war, fifty thousand,—and for setting in array, not of two minds!
text.KJV: Of Zebulun, such as went forth to battle, expert in war, with all instruments of war, fifty thousand, which could keep rank: they were not of double heart.
text.NABRE: Of the Issacharites, their chiefs who were endowed with an understanding of the times and who knew what Israel had to do: two hundred chiefs, together with all their kinsmen under their command.
text.DRB: Moreover of Zabulon such as went forth to battle, and stood in array well appointed with armour of war, there came fifty thousand to aid, not in a double heart.

text.NRSV: Of Naphtali, a thousand commanders, with whom there were thirty-seven thousand armed with shield and spear.
text.Rotherham: And, of Naphtali, a thousand captains,—and, with them, with shield and spear, thirty-seven thousand.
text.KJV: And of Naphtali a thousand captains, and with them with shield and spear thirty and seven thousand.
text.NABRE: From Zebulun, men fit for military service, set in battle array with every kind of weapon for war: fifty thousand men rallying with a single purpose.
text.DRB: And of Nephthali, commanders a thousand: & with them furnished with shield & spear, seven & thirty thousand.

text.NRSV: Of the Danites, twenty-eight thousand six hundred equipped for battle.
text.Rotherham: And, of the Danite, expert for war, twenty-eight thousand, and six hundred.
text.KJV: And of the Danites expert in war twenty and eight thousand and six hundred.
text.NABRE: From Naphtali: one thousand captains, and with them, armed with shield and lance, thirty-seven thousand men.
text.DRB: Of Dan also prepared to battle, twenty eight thousand six hundred.

text.NRSV: Of Asher, forty thousand seasoned troops ready for battle.
text.Rotherham: And, of Asher, such as were ready to go forth in host to set in array for battle, forty thousand.
text.KJV: And of Asher, such as went forth to battle, expert in war, forty thousand.
text.NABRE: Of the Danites, set in battle array: twenty-eight thousand six hundred.
text.DRB: And of Aser going forth to fight, & provoking in battle, forty thousand.

text.NRSV: Of the Reubenites and Gadites and the half-tribe of Manasseh from beyond the Jordan, one hundred twenty thousand armed with all the weapons of war.
text.Rotherham: And, from over the Jordan, of the Reubenites and Gadites and half tribe of Manasseh, with all manner of weapons of war for battle, a hundred and twenty thousand.
text.KJV: And on the other side of Jordan, of the Reubenites, and the Gadites, and of the half tribe of Manasseh, with all manner of instruments of war for the battle, an hundred and twenty thousand.
text.NABRE: From Asher, fit for military service and set in battle array: forty thousand.
text.DRB: And beyond Jordan of the children of Ruben & of Gad, & the half part of the tribe of Manasses, furnished with armour of war, an hundred twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: All these, warriors arrayed in battle order, came to Hebron with full intent to make David king over all Israel; likewise all the rest of Israel were of a single mind to make David king.
text.Rotherham: All these, being men of war, expert in setting in battle-array, with a perfect heart, came to Hebron, to make David king over all Israel,—moreover also, all the rest of Israel, were of one heart to make David king.
text.KJV: All these men of war, that could keep rank, came with a perfect heart to Hebron, to make David king over all Israel: and all the rest also of Israel were of one heart to make David king.
text.NABRE: From the other side of the Jordan, of the Reubenites, Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh, men equipped with every kind of weapon of war: one hundred and twenty thousand.
text.DRB: All these men of war well appointed to fight, with perfect heart came into Hebron, to make David king over all Israel: yea & all the rest of Israel were of one heart, that David should be made king.

text.NRSV: They were there with David for three days, eating and drinking, for their kindred had provided for them.
text.Rotherham: So they were there, with David, three days, eating and drinking,—for their brethren had made preparation for them.
text.KJV: And there they were with David three days, eating and drinking: for their brethren had prepared for them.
text.NABRE: All these soldiers, drawn up in battle order, came to Hebron with the resolute intention of making David king over all Israel. The rest of Israel was likewise of one mind to make David king.
text.DRB: And they were there with David three days eating and drinking: for their brethren had prepared for them.

text.NRSV: And also their neighbors, from as far as Issachar and Zebulun and Naphtali, were bringing food on donkeys, camels, mules, and oxen—abundant provisions of meal, cakes of figs, clusters of raisins, wine, oil, oxen, and sheep, for there was joy in Israel.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, they who were near of kin unto them, as far as Issachar and Zebulun and Naphtali, were bringing bread, on asses and on camels and on mules and on oxen, meal-food, cakes of figs and cakes of raisins, and wine, and oil, and oxen, and sheep—in abundance,—for there was joy Israel.
text.KJV: Moreover they that were nigh them, even unto Issachar and Zebulun and Naphtali, brought bread on asses, and on camels, and on mules, and on oxen, and meat, meal, cakes of figs, and bunches of raisins, and wine, and oil, and oxen, and sheep abundantly: for there was joy in Israel.
text.NABRE: They remained with David for three days, eating and drinking, for their relatives had prepared for them.
text.DRB: Yea and they that were near them as far as Issachar, and Zabulon, and Nephthali, brought loaves on Asses, and on camels, and on mules, and upon oxen, to eat: meal, figs, raisins, wine, oil, beeves, muttons, in all aboundance, for there was joy in Israel.

text.NRSV: David consulted with the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, with every leader.
text.Rotherham: And David consulted with the rulers of thousands and hundreds, even with every chief ruler.
text.KJV: And David consulted with the captains of thousands and hundreds, and with every leader.
text.NABRE: Chapter 13 Transfer of the Ark. After David had taken counsel with his commanders of thousands and of hundreds, that is, with every leader,
text.DRB: And David took counsel with the tribunes, and centurions, and all the commanders,

text.NRSV: David said to the whole assembly of Israel, “If it seems good to you, and if it is the will of the Lord our God, let us send abroad to our kindred who remain in all the territories of Israel, and with them to the priests and Levites in the towns that have pasture lands, that they may come together to us.
text.Rotherham: And David said to all the convocation of Israel, If, unto you, it seemeth to be good, and from Yahweh our God, we will urgently send unto our brethren who remain in all the lands of Israel, and, with them, the priests and the Levites in their pasture-land cities,—that they may gather together unto us;
text.KJV: And David said unto all the congregation of Israel, If it seem good unto you, and that it be of the Lord our God, let us send abroad unto our brethren every where, that are left in all the land of Israel, and with them also to the priests and Levites which are in their cities and suburbs, that they may gather themselves unto us:
text.NABRE: he said to the whole assembly of Israel: “If it seems good to you, and is so decreed by the Lord our God, let us send to the rest of our kindred from all the districts of Israel, and also the priests and the Levites from their cities with pasture lands, that they may join us;
text.DRB: and he said to all the assembly of Israel: If it please you, & if the word which I speak proceed from our Lord God, let us send to the rest of our brethren into all the countries of Israel, & to the Priests, & Levites, that dwell in the suburbs of the cities, that they may be gathered together unto us,

text.NRSV: Then let us bring again the ark of our God to us; for we did not seek it in the days of Saul.”
text.Rotherham: and let us bring round the ark of our God, unto us,—For they sought it not, in the days of Saul.
text.KJV: And let us bring again the ark of our God to us: for we enquired not at it in the days of Saul.
text.NABRE: and let us bring the ark of our God here among us, for in the days of Saul we did not consult it.”
text.DRB: and we may bring again unto us the Ark of our God: for we sought it not in the days of Saul.

text.NRSV: The whole assembly agreed to do so, for the thing scarcely seemed right in the eyes of all the people.
text.Rotherham: Than said all the convocation, that it should be done thus,—for right was the thing, in the eyes of all the people.
text.KJV: And all the congregation said that they would do so: for the thing was right in the eyes of all the people.
text.NABRE: And the whole assembly agreed to do it, for it seemed right in the eyes of all the people.
text.DRB: And the whole multitude answered that it should be so done: for the word had pleased all the people.

text.NRSV: So David assembled all Israel from the Shihor of Egypt to Lebo-hamath, to bring the ark of God from Kiriath-jearim.
text.Rotherham: So David convoked all Israel, from Shihor of Egypt, even unto the entering in of Hamath,—to bring the ark of God from Kiriath-jearim.
text.KJV: So David gathered all Israel together, from Shihor of Egypt even unto the entering of Hemath, to bring the ark of God from Kirjath–jearim.
text.NABRE: Then David assembled all Israel, from Shihor of Egypt to Lebo-hamath, to bring the ark of God from Kiriath-jearim.
text.DRB: David therefore assembled all Israel from Sihor of Ægypt, till thou enter into Emath, to bring the Ark of God from Cariathiarim.

text.NRSV: And David and all Israel went up to Baalah, that is, to Kiriath-jearim, which belongs to Judah, to bring up from there the ark of God, which is called by the name of the Lord who is enthroned on the cherubim.
text.Rotherham: And David went up and all Israel to Baalah, unto Kiriath-jearim, which pertaineth to Judah,—to bring up from thence, the ark of God—Yahweh who inhabiteth the cherubim, on which is called the Name.
text.KJV: And David went up, and all Israel, to Baalah, that is, to Kirjath–jearim, which belonged to Judah, to bring up thence the ark of God the Lord, that dwelleth between the cherubims, whose name is called on it.
text.NABRE: David and all Israel went up to Baalah, that is, to Kiriath-jearim, of Judah, to bring up from there the ark of God, which was known by the name “ Lord enthroned upon the cherubim.”
text.DRB: And David went up, and every man of Israel to the hill of Cariathiarim which is in Juda, to fetch thence the Ark of our God sitting upon the Cherubim, where his name is invocated.

text.NRSV: They carried the ark of God on a new cart, from the house of Abinadab, and Uzzah and Ahio were driving the cart.
text.Rotherham: And they carried the ark of God, upon a new waggon, out of the house of Aminadab,—and, Uzza and Ahio, were driving the waggon.
text.KJV: And they carried the ark of God in a new cart out of the house of Abinadab: and Uzza and Ahio drave the cart.
text.NABRE: They transported the ark of God on a new cart from the house of Abinadab; Uzzah and Ahio were guiding the cart,
text.DRB: And they laid the Ark of God upon a new wain, out of the house of Abinadab. And Oza and his brother did drive the wain.

text.NRSV: David and all Israel were dancing before God with all their might, with song and lyres and harps and tambourines and cymbals and trumpets.
text.Rotherham: And, David and all Israel, were dancing for joy before God, with all boldness, and with songs and with lyres, and with harps and with timbrels, and with cymbals, and with trumpets.
text.KJV: And David and all Israel played before God with all their might, and with singing, and with harps, and with psalteries, and with timbrels, and with cymbals, and with trumpets.
text.NABRE: while David and all Israel danced before God with all their might, with singing, and with lyres, harps, tambourines, cymbals, and trumpets.
text.DRB: Moreover David, & all Israel played before our Lord with all their might in songs, and on harps, and psalteries, and timbrels, and cymbals, and trumpets.

text.NRSV: When they came to the threshing floor of Chidon, Uzzah put out his hand to hold the ark, for the oxen shook it.
text.Rotherham: And, when they came as far as the threshing-floor of Chidon, Uzza thrust forth his hand, to seize the ark, for the oxen were restive.
text.KJV: And when they came unto the threshingfloor of Chidon, Uzza put forth his hand to hold the ark; for the oxen stumbled.
text.NABRE: As they reached the threshing floor of Chidon, Uzzah stretched out his hand to steady the ark, for the oxen were tipping it.
text.DRB: And when they were come to the Floor of Chidon, Oza stretched forth his hand, to hold up the Ark: for the oxe being wanton had made it lean a little a-side.

text.NRSV: The anger of the Lord was kindled against Uzzah; he struck him down because he put out his hand to the ark; and he died there before God.
text.Rotherham: Then was kindled the anger of Yahweh against Uzza, and he smote him, because he thrust forth his hand unto the ark,—and he died there, before God.
text.KJV: And the anger of the Lord was kindled against Uzza, and he smote him, because he put his hand to the ark: and there he died before God.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord became angry with Uzzah and struck him, because he had laid his hand on the ark; he died there in God’s presence.
text.DRB: Our Lord therefore was angry against Oza, & struck him, for that he had touched the Ark: and he died there before our Lord.

text.NRSV: David was angry because the Lord had burst out against Uzzah; so that place is called Perez-uzzah to this day.
text.Rotherham: And it was a sadness unto David, that Yahweh had broken in with a breach against Uzza,—and he called that place Perez-uzza, [as it is called] unto this day.
text.KJV: And David was displeased, because the Lord had made a breach upon Uzza: wherefore that place is called Perez–uzza to this day.
text.NABRE: David was angry because the Lord ’s anger had broken out against Uzzah. Therefore that place has been called Perez-uzzah even to this day.
text.DRB: And David was strucken sad, because our Lord had divided Oza: and he called that place, the Division of Oza until this present day.

text.NRSV: David was afraid of God that day; he said, “How can I bring the ark of God home to me?”
text.Rotherham: And David was afraid of God, on that day, saying,—How can I bring unto me, the ark of God?
text.KJV: And David was afraid of God that day, saying, How shall I bring the ark of God home to me?
text.NABRE: David was afraid of God that day, and he said, “How can I bring in the ark of God to me?”
text.DRB: And he feared God at that time, saying: How may I bring in the Ark of God unto me?

text.NRSV: So David did not take the ark into the city of David; he delivered it into the house of Obed-edom the Gittite.
text.Rotherham: So David removed not the ark unto him, into the city of David,—but took it aside into the house of Obed-edom, the Gittite.
text.KJV: So David brought not the ark home to himself to the city of David, but carried it aside into the house of Obed–edom the Gittite.
text.NABRE: Therefore he did not take the ark with him into the City of David, but deposited it instead at the house of Obed-edom the Gittite.
text.DRB: And for this cause he brought it not unto himself, that is, into the city of David, but turned it away into the house of Obededom the Getheite.

text.NRSV: The ark of God remained with the family of Obed-edom in his house three months; and the Lord blessed the house of Obed-edom and all that he had.
text.Rotherham: And the ark of God remained with the household of Obed-edom, in his house, three months,—and Yahweh blessed the household of Obed-edom, and all that he had.
text.KJV: And the ark of God remained with the family of Obed–edom in his house three months. And the Lord blessed the house of Obed–edom, and all that he had.
text.NABRE: The ark of God remained in the house of Obed-edom with his family for three months, and the Lord blessed Obed-edom’s household and all that he possessed.
text.DRB: Therefore the Ark of God remained in the house of Obededom three months: & our Lord blessed his house, and all things that he had.

text.NRSV: King Hiram of Tyre sent messengers to David, along with cedar logs, and masons and carpenters to build a house for him.
text.Rotherham: And Hiram king of Tyre sent messengers unto David, and timber of cedars, with masons and artificers, to build for him a house.
text.KJV: Now Hiram king of Tyre sent messengers to David, and timber of cedars, with masons and carpenters, to build him an house.
text.NABRE: Chapter 14 David in Jerusalem. Hiram, king of Tyre, sent envoys to David along with cedar wood, and masons and carpenters to build him a house.
text.DRB: Hiram also the king of Tyre sent messengers to David, and cedar trees, and artificers for walls, and timber, to build him a house.

text.NRSV: David then perceived that the Lord had established him as king over Israel, and that his kingdom was highly exalted for the sake of his people Israel.
text.Rotherham: So David perceived, that Yahweh, had confirmed him, as king over Israel,—that his kingship was exalted, for the sake of his people Israel.
text.KJV: And David perceived that the Lord had confirmed him king over Israel, for his kingdom was lifted up on high, because of his people Israel.
text.NABRE: David now knew that the Lord had truly established him as king over Israel, for his kingdom was greatly exalted for the sake of his people Israel.
text.DRB: And David knew that our Lord had established him to be king over Israel, and that his Kingdom was exalted over his people Israel.

text.NRSV: David took more wives in Jerusalem, and David became the father of more sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: And David took more wives, in Jerusalem,—and David begat more sons and daughters.
text.KJV: And David took more wives at Jerusalem: and David begat more sons and daughters.
text.NABRE: David took other wives in Jerusalem and became the father of more sons and daughters.
text.DRB: David also took other wives in Jerusalem: and he begat sons, and daughters.

text.NRSV: These are the names of the children who were born to him in Jerusalem: Shammua, Shobab, Nathan, Solomon,
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the names of them who were born, whom he had in Jerusalem,—Shammua and Shobab, Nathan, and Solomon;
text.KJV: Now these are the names of his children which he had in Jerusalem; Shammua, and Shobab, Nathan, and Solomon,
text.NABRE: These are the names of those who were born to him in Jerusalem: Shammua, Shobab, Nathan, Solomon,
text.DRB: And these be their names, that were born to him in Jerusalem: Samua, and Sobab, Nathan, and Salomon.

text.NRSV: Ibhar, Elishua, Elpelet,
text.Rotherham: and Ibhar and Elishua, and Elpelet;
text.KJV: And Ibhar, and Elishua, and Elpalet,
text.NABRE: Ibhar, Elishua, Elpelet,
text.DRB: Jebahar, and Elisua, and Eliphalet,

text.NRSV: Nogah, Nepheg, Japhia,
text.Rotherham: and Nogah and Nepheg, and Japhia;
text.KJV: And Nogah, and Nepheg, and Japhia,
text.NABRE: Nogah, Nepheg, Japhia,
text.DRB: Noga also, and Napheg, and Japhia,

text.NRSV: Elishama, Beeliada, and Eliphelet.
text.Rotherham: and Elishama and Beeliada, and Eliphelet.
text.KJV: And Elishama, and Beeliada, and Eliphalet.
text.NABRE: Elishama, Beeliada, and Eliphelet.
text.DRB: Elisama, and Baalaida, and Eliphalet.

text.NRSV: When the Philistines heard that David had been anointed king over all Israel, all the Philistines went up in search of David; and David heard of it and went out against them.
text.Rotherham: And, when the Philistines heard that David had been anointed king over all Israel, all the Philistines came up to seek to secure David,—and David, hearing, went out against them.
text.KJV: And when the Philistines heard that David was anointed king over all Israel, all the Philistines went up to seek David. And David heard of it, and went out against them.
text.NABRE: The Philistine Wars. When the Philistines had heard that David was anointed king over all Israel, they marched out in force looking for him. But when David heard of this, he went out against them.
text.DRB: And the Philisthiims hearing that David was anointed for king over all Israel, they went up all to seek him: which when David had heard, he went out to meet them.

text.NRSV: Now the Philistines had come and made a raid in the valley of Rephaim.
text.Rotherham: Now, the Philistines, had come and spread themselves out, in the vale of Rephaim.
text.KJV: And the Philistines came and spread themselves in the valley of Rephaim.
text.NABRE: Meanwhile the Philistines had come and raided the valley of Rephaim.
text.DRB: Moreover the Philisthiims coming, were spread abroad in the Vale Raphaim.

text.NRSV: David inquired of God, “Shall I go up against the Philistines? Will you give them into my hand?” The Lord said to him, “Go up, and I will give them into your hand.”
text.Rotherham: Then David asked of God, saying, Shall I go up against the Philistines? and wilt thou deliver them into my hand? And Yahweh said to him, Go up, and I will deliver them into thy hand.
text.KJV: And David enquired of God, saying, Shall I go up against the Philistines? and wilt thou deliver them into mine hand? And the Lord said unto him, Go up; for I will deliver them into thine hand.
text.NABRE: David inquired of God, “Shall I attack the Philistines, and will you deliver them into my power?” The Lord answered him, “Attack, for I have delivered them into your power.”
text.DRB: And David consulted our Lord, saying: Shall I go up to the Philistians, and wilt thou deliver them into my hand? And our Lord said to him: Go up, and I will deliver them into thy hand.

text.NRSV: So they went up to Baal-perazim, and David defeated them there. David said, “God has burst out against my enemies by my hand, like a bursting flood.” Therefore that place is called Baal-perazim.
text.Rotherham: So they came up in Baal-perazim, and David smote them there, and David said, God hath broken forth against mine enemies by my hand, like the breaking forth of waters,—For this cause, called they the name of that place, Baal-perazim.
text.KJV: So they came up to Baal–perazim; and David smote them there. Then David said, God hath broken in upon mine enemies by mine hand like the breaking forth of waters: therefore they called the name of that place Baal–perazim.
text.NABRE: So they attacked, at Baal-perazim, and David defeated them there. Then David said, “By my hand God has broken through my enemies just as water breaks through a dam.” Therefore that place was called Baal-perazim.
text.DRB: And when they were come up into Baalpharasim, David struck them there, and said: God hath divided mine enemies by my hand, as waters are divided: and therefore the name of that place was called Baal Pharasim.

text.NRSV: They abandoned their gods there, and at David’s command they were burned.
text.Rotherham: And they left their gods there,—and David gave the word, and they were burned up in fire.
text.KJV: And when they had left their gods there, David gave a commandment, and they were burned with fire.
text.NABRE: The Philistines abandoned their gods there, and David ordered them to be burnt.
text.DRB: And they left there their gods, which David commanded to be burnt.

text.NRSV: Once again the Philistines made a raid in the valley.
text.Rotherham: And the Philistines yet again spread themselves out in the vale.
text.KJV: And the Philistines yet again spread themselves abroad in the valley.
text.NABRE: Once again the Philistines raided the valley,
text.DRB: And other time also the Philisthiims invaded, and were dispersed in the Vale.

text.NRSV: When David again inquired of God, God said to him, “You shall not go up after them; go around and come on them opposite the balsam trees.
text.Rotherham: So David, asked again, of God, and God said to him, Thou shalt not go up after them,—get thee round, away from them, so shalt thou come in upon them, over against the mulberry-trees;
text.KJV: Therefore David enquired again of God; and God said unto him, Go not up after them; turn away from them, and come upon them over against the mulberry trees.
text.NABRE: and again David inquired of God. But God answered him: Do not try to pursue them, but go around them and come against them near the balsam trees.
text.DRB: And David consulted God again, and God said to him: Go not up after them, retire from them, & thou shalt come against them over against the pear-trees.

text.NRSV: For when you hear the sound of marching in the tops of the balsam trees, then go out to battle; for God has gone out before you to strike down the army of the Philistines.”
text.Rotherham: and it shall be, when thou hearest a sound of marching in the tops of the mulberry-trees, then, shalt thou go forth into the battle,—for God will have gone forth before thee, to smite the host of the Philistines.
text.KJV: And it shall be, when thou shalt hear a sound of going in the tops of the mulberry trees, that then thou shalt go out to battle: for God is gone forth before thee to smite the host of the Philistines.
text.NABRE: When you hear the sound of marching in the tops of the balsam trees, then go forth to battle, for God has already gone before you to strike the army of the Philistines.
text.DRB: And when thou shalt hear the sound of one going in the top of the pear-trees, then shalt thou issue forth to battle. For God is gone forth before thee, to strike the camp of the Philisthiims.

text.NRSV: David did as God had commanded him, and they struck down the Philistine army from Gibeon to Gezer.
text.Rotherham: So David did as God commanded him,—and they smote the host of the Philistines, from Gibeon even unto Gezer.
text.KJV: David therefore did as God commanded him: and they smote the host of the Philistines from Gibeon even to Gazer.
text.NABRE: David did as God commanded him, and they routed the Philistine army from Gibeon to Gezer.
text.DRB: David therefore did as God had commanded him, & struck the camp of the Philisthians from Gabaon unto Gazera.

text.NRSV: The fame of David went out into all lands, and the Lord brought the fear of him on all nations.
text.Rotherham: And the name of David went forth, throughout all the lands,—and, Yahweh, put the dread of him upon all the nations.
text.KJV: And the fame of David went out into all lands; and the Lord brought the fear of him upon all nations.
text.NABRE: Thus David’s fame was spread abroad through every land, and the Lord put the fear of him on all the nations.
text.DRB: And the name of David was bruited in all countries, and our Lord gave the dread of him over all nations.

text.NRSV: David built houses for himself in the city of David, and he prepared a place for the ark of God and pitched a tent for it.
text.Rotherham: And he made him houses, in the city of David,—and prepared a place for the ark of God, and pitched for it a tent.
text.KJV: And David made him houses in the city of David, and prepared a place for the ark of God, and pitched for it a tent.
text.NABRE: Chapter 15 Preparations for Moving the Ark. David built houses for himself in the City of David and prepared a place for the ark of God, pitching a tent for it there.
text.DRB: He made also houses for himself in the city of David: and built a place for the Ark of God, and pitcht a tabernacle for it.

text.NRSV: Then David said, “No one but the Levites may carry the ark of God, for the Lord chose them to carry the ark of the Lord and to minister to him forever.”
text.Rotherham: Then, said David, None must carry the ark of God, save the Levites,—for, of them, did Yahweh make choice, to bear the ark of Yahweh, and to attend it, unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: Then David said, None ought to carry the ark of God but the Levites: for them hath the Lord chosen to carry the ark of God, and to minister unto him for ever.
text.NABRE: At that time he said, “No one may carry the ark of God except the Levites, for the Lord chose them to carry the ark of the Lord and to minister to him forever.”
text.DRB: Then said David: It is unlawful that the Ark of God be carried of any man, but of the Levites: whom our Lord chose to carry it, and to minister unto himself for ever.

text.NRSV: David assembled all Israel in Jerusalem to bring up the ark of the Lord to its place, which he had prepared for it.
text.Rotherham: So David convoked all Israel, unto Jerusalem,—to bring up the ark of Yahweh, unto the place thereof, which he had prepared for it.
text.KJV: And David gathered all Israel together to Jerusalem, to bring up the ark of the Lord unto his place, which he had prepared for it.
text.NABRE: Then David assembled all Israel to Jerusalem to bring up the ark of the Lord to its place, which he had prepared for it.
text.DRB: And he gathered together all Israel into Jerusalem, that the Ark of God might be brought into his place, which he had prepared for it.

text.NRSV: Then David gathered together the descendants of Aaron and the Levites:
text.Rotherham: And David gathered together the sons of Aaron, and the Levites:
text.KJV: And David assembled the children of Aaron, and the Levites:
text.NABRE: David also convened the sons of Aaron and the Levites:
text.DRB: Moreover also the sons of Aaron, and the Levites.

text.NRSV: of the sons of Kohath, Uriel the chief, with one hundred twenty of his kindred;
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Kohath, Uriel the chief, and his brethren, one hundred and twenty;
text.KJV: Of the sons of Kohath; Uriel the chief, and his brethren an hundred and twenty:
text.NABRE: of the sons of Kohath, Uriel, their chief, and one hundred and twenty of his brothers;
text.DRB: Of the children of Caath, Uriel was the Prince, and his brethren an hundred twenty.

text.NRSV: of the sons of Merari, Asaiah the chief, with two hundred twenty of his kindred;
text.Rotherham: of the sons of Merari, Asaiah the chief, and his brethren, two hundred and twenty;
text.KJV: Of the sons of Merari; Asaiah the chief, and his brethren two hundred and twenty:
text.NABRE: of the sons of Merari, Asaiah, their chief, and two hundred and twenty of his brothers;
text.DRB: Of the sons of Merari, Asaia the Prince: and his brethren two hundred twenty.

text.NRSV: of the sons of Gershom, Joel the chief, with one hundred thirty of his kindred;
text.Rotherham: of the sons of Gershom, Joel the chief, and his brethren, one hundred and thirty;
text.KJV: Of the sons of Gershom; Joel the chief, and his brethren an hundred and thirty:
text.NABRE: of the sons of Gershon, Joel, their chief, and one hundred and thirty of his brothers;
text.DRB: Of the sons of Gersom, Joel the Prince: and his brethren an hundred thirty.

text.NRSV: of the sons of Elizaphan, Shemaiah the chief, with two hundred of his kindred;
text.Rotherham: of the sons of Elizaphan, Shemaiah the chief, and his brethren, two hundred;
text.KJV: Of the sons of Elizaphan; Shemaiah the chief, and his brethren two hundred:
text.NABRE: of the sons of Elizaphan, Shemaiah, their chief, and two hundred of his brothers;
text.DRB: Of the sons of Elisaphan, Semeias the Prince: and his brethren two hundred.

text.NRSV: of the sons of Hebron, Eliel the chief, with eighty of his kindred;
text.Rotherham: of the sons of Hebron, Eliel the chief, and his brethren, eighty;
text.KJV: Of the sons of Hebron; Eliel the chief, and his brethren fourscore:
text.NABRE: of the sons of Hebron, Eliel, their chief, and eighty of his brothers;
text.DRB: Of the sons of Hebron, Eliel the Prince: and his brethren eighty.

text.NRSV: of the sons of Uzziel, Amminadab the chief, with one hundred twelve of his kindred.
text.Rotherham: of the son of Uzziel, Amminadab the chief, and his brethren, a hundred and twelve.
text.KJV: Of the sons of Uzziel; Amminadab the chief, and his brethren an hundred and twelve.
text.NABRE: of the sons of Uzziel, Amminadab, their chief, and one hundred and twelve of his brothers.
text.DRB: Of the sons of Oziel, Aminadab the Prince: and his brethren an hundred twelve.

text.NRSV: David summoned the priests Zadok and Abiathar, and the Levites Uriel, Asaiah, Joel, Shemaiah, Eliel, and Amminadab.
text.Rotherham: Then called David, for Zadok and for Abiathar, the priests,—and for the Levites, for Uriel, Asaiah and Joel, Shemaiah and Eliel, and Amminadab;
text.KJV: And David called for Zadok and Abiathar the priests, and for the Levites, for Uriel, Asaiah, and Joel, Shemaiah, and Eliel, and Amminadab,
text.NABRE: David summoned the priests Zadok and Abiathar, and the Levites Uriel, Asaiah, Joel, Shemaiah, Eliel, and Amminadab,
text.DRB: And David called Sadoc and Abiathar the Priests, and the Levites, Uriel, Asaia, Joel, Semeia, Eliel, and Aminadab:

text.NRSV: He said to them, “You are the heads of the ancestral houses of the Levites; sanctify yourselves, you and your kindred, so that you may bring up the ark of the Lord, the God of Israel, to the place that I have prepared for it.
text.Rotherham: and said unto them, Ye, are the chiefs of the fathers of the Levites,—Hallow yourselves, ye and your brethren, so shall ye bring up the ark of Yahweh—God of Israel, into [the place] I have prepared for it.
text.KJV: And said unto them, Ye are the chief of the fathers of the Levites: sanctify yourselves, both ye and your brethren, that ye may bring up the ark of the Lord God of Israel unto the place that I have prepared for it.
text.NABRE: and said to them: “You heads of the levitical houses, sanctify yourselves along with your brothers to bring up the ark of the Lord, the God of Israel, to the place which I have prepared for it.
text.DRB: and he said to them: You that are the Princes of the Levitical families, be sanctified with your brethren, and fetch the Ark of our Lord the God of Israel to the place, which is prepared for it:

text.NRSV: Because you did not carry it the first time, the Lord our God burst out against us, because we did not give it proper care.”
text.Rotherham: Because, at the first, ye, did not [bring it], Yahweh our God brake forth against us, because we sought him not in the appointed way.
text.KJV: For because ye did it not at the first, the Lord our God made a breach upon us, for that we sought him not after the due order.
text.NABRE: Because you were not with us the first time, the Lord our God broke out against us, for we did not seek him aright.”
text.DRB: lest as from the beginning, because you were not present, our Lord struck us: so now also it come to pass, we doing some unlawful thing.

text.NRSV: So the priests and the Levites sanctified themselves to bring up the ark of the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: So the priests and the Levites hallowed themselves,—to bring up the ark of Yahweh, God of Israel.
text.KJV: So the priests and the Levites sanctified themselves to bring up the ark of the Lord God of Israel.
text.NABRE: Accordingly, the priests and the Levites sanctified themselves to bring up the ark of the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.DRB: The Priests therefore, and the Levites were sanctified, to carry the Ark of our Lord the God of Israel.

text.NRSV: And the Levites carried the ark of God on their shoulders with the poles, as Moses had commanded according to the word of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of the Levites bare the ark of God, just as Moses commanded, according to the word of Yahweh,—on their shoulder, with the staves upon them.
text.KJV: And the children of the Levites bare the ark of God upon their shoulders with the staves thereon, as Moses commanded according to the word of the Lord.
text.NABRE: The Levites carried the ark of God on their shoulders with poles, as Moses had commanded according to the word of the Lord.
text.DRB: And the sons of Levi took the Ark of God, as Moyses had commanded, according to the word of our Lord, upon their shoulders, on bars.

text.NRSV: David also commanded the chiefs of the Levites to appoint their kindred as the singers to play on musical instruments, on harps and lyres and cymbals, to raise loud sounds of joy.
text.Rotherham: And David spake to the chiefs of the Levites, to station their brethren the singers, with instruments of song, harps and lyres, and cymbals,—sounding aloud at the lifting up of the voice, for joy.
text.KJV: And David spake to the chief of the Levites to appoint their brethren to be the singers with instruments of musick, psalteries and harps and cymbals, sounding, by lifting up the voice with joy.
text.NABRE: David commanded the commanders of the Levites to appoint their brothers as singers and to play on musical instruments, harps, lyres, and cymbals, to make a loud sound of rejoicing.
text.DRB: And David said to the Princes of the Levites, that they should appoint of their brethren singing men on musical instruments, to wit, on nables, and harps, and cymbals, that the sound of joy might resound on high.

text.NRSV: So the Levites appointed Heman son of Joel; and of his kindred Asaph son of Berechiah; and of the sons of Merari, their kindred, Ethan son of Kushaiah;
text.Rotherham: So the Levites caused to stand, Heman son of Joel, and, of his brethren, Asaph, son of Berechiah,—and, of the sons of Merari their brethren, Ethan, son of Kushaiah;
text.KJV: So the Levites appointed Heman the son of Joel; and of his brethren, Asaph the son of Berechiah; and of the sons of Merari their brethren, Ethan the son of Kushaiah;
text.NABRE: Therefore the Levites appointed Heman, son of Joel, and, among his brothers, Asaph, son of Berechiah; and among the sons of Merari, their brothers, Ethan, son of Kushaiah;
text.DRB: And they appointed Levites: Heman the son of Joel, and of his brethren Asaph the son of Barachias: and of the children of Merari, their brethren: Ethan the son of Casaia.

text.NRSV: and with them their kindred of the second order, Zechariah, Jaaziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, and Mikneiah, and the gatekeepers Obed-edom and Jeiel.
text.Rotherham: and, with them, their brethren of the second degree,—Zechariah, Ben, and Jaaziel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel and Unni, Eliab, and Benaiah, and Maaseiah, and Mattithiah, and Eliphelehu, and Mickneiah, and Obed-edom and Jeiel, the door-keepers.
text.KJV: And with them their brethren of the second degree, Zechariah, Ben, and Jaaziel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Unni, Eliab, and Benaiah, and Maaseiah, and Mattithiah, and Elipheleh, and Mikneiah, and Obed–edom, and Jeiel, the porters.
text.NABRE: and, together with these, their brothers of the second rank: the gatekeepers Zechariah, Uzziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, Mikneiah, Obed-edom, and Jeiel.
text.DRB: And with them their brethren: in the second order, Zacharias, & Ben, & Jaziel, and Semiramoth, and Jahiel, and Ani, Eliab, and Banaias, and Maasias, and Mathathias, and Eliphalu, and Macenias, and Obededom, and Jehiel, porters.

text.NRSV: The singers Heman, Asaph, and Ethan were to sound bronze cymbals;
text.Rotherham: And, the singers, Heman, Asaph and Ethan, with cymbals of bronze, were to sound aloud;
text.KJV: So the singers, Heman, Asaph, and Ethan, were appointed to sound with cymbals of brass;
text.NABRE: The singers, Heman, Asaph, and Ethan, sounded brass cymbals.
text.DRB: Moreover them that sang, Heman, Asaph and Ethan: sounding on brazen cymbals.

text.NRSV: Zechariah, Aziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Maaseiah, and Benaiah were to play harps according to Alamoth;
text.Rotherham: and Zechariah, and Aziel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Unni, and Eliab, and Maaseiah and Benaiah,—with harps, over the trebles;
text.KJV: And Zechariah, and Aziel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Unni, and Eliab, and Maaseiah, and Benaiah, with psalteries on Alamoth;
text.NABRE: Zechariah, Uzziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Maaseiah, and Benaiah played on harps set to “Alamoth.”
text.DRB: And Zacharias, and Oziel, and Semiramoth, and Jahiel, and Ani, and Eliab, and Maasias, and Banaias upon nables sang mysteries.

text.NRSV: but Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, Mikneiah, Obed-edom, Jeiel, and Azaziah were to lead with lyres according to the Sheminith.
text.Rotherham: and Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, and Mikneiah, and Obed-edom, and Jeiel and Azaziah,—with lyres over the bass, to take the lead.
text.KJV: And Mattithiah, and Elipheleh, and Mikneiah, and Obed–edom, and Jeiel, and Azaziah, with harps on the Sheminith to excel.
text.NABRE: But Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, Mikneiah, Obed-edom, and Jeiel led the song on lyres set to “sheminith.”
text.DRB: Moreover Mathathias, and Eliphalu, and Macenias, and Obededom, and Jehiel, and Ozaziu, upon harps for the octave sang a triumphant song.

text.NRSV: Chenaniah, leader of the Levites in music, was to direct the music, for he understood it.
text.Rotherham: And, Chenaniah the leader of the Levites, in the service, used to give instructions in the service, because skilful was he.
text.KJV: And Chenaniah, chief of the Levites, was for song: he instructed about the song, because he was skilful.
text.NABRE: Chenaniah was the chief of the Levites in the singing; he directed the singing, for he was skillful.
text.DRB: And Chonenias the Prince of the Levites, was chief over Prophecy, to begin the melody: for he was very cunning.

text.NRSV: Berechiah and Elkanah were to be gatekeepers for the ark.
text.Rotherham: And, Berechiah and Elkanah, were door-keepers to the ark.
text.KJV: And Berechiah and Elkanah were doorkeepers for the ark.
text.NABRE: Berechiah and Elkanah were gatekeepers before the ark.
text.DRB: And Barachias, and Elcana: door keepers of the Ark.

text.NRSV: Shebaniah, Joshaphat, Nethanel, Amasai, Zechariah, Benaiah, and Eliezer, the priests, were to blow the trumpets before the ark of God. Obed-edom and Jehiah also were to be gatekeepers for the ark.
text.Rotherham: And, Shebaniah, and Joshaphat, and Nethanel, and Amasai, and Zechariah, and Benaiah, and Eliezer, the priests, were blowing with the trumpets, before the ark of God,—and, Obed-edom and Jehiah, were door-keepers, to the ark.
text.KJV: And Shebaniah, and Jehoshaphat, and Nethaneel, and Amasai, and Zechariah, and Benaiah, and Eliezer, the priests, did blow with the trumpets before the ark of God: and Obed–edom and Jehiah were doorkeepers for the ark.
text.NABRE: The priests, Shebaniah, Joshaphat, Nethanel, Amasai, Zechariah, Benaiah, and Eliezer, sounded the trumpets before the ark of God. Obed-edom and Jeiel were also gatekeepers before the ark.
text.DRB: Moreover Sebenias, and Josaphat, and Nathanael, and Amasai, and Zacharias, and Banaias, and Eliezer Priests, sounded with trumpets, before the Ark of God: and Obededom, and Jehias kept the door of the Ark.

text.NRSV: So David and the elders of Israel and the commanders of thousands went to bring up the ark of the covenant of the Lord from the house of Obed-edom with rejoicing.
text.Rotherham: Thus, it was, David and the elders of Israel, and the rulers of thousands, who were going, to bring up the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, out of the house of Obed-edom, with joy.
text.KJV: So David, and the elders of Israel, and the captains over thousands, went to bring up the ark of the covenant of the Lord out of the house of Obed–edom with joy.
text.NABRE: The Ark Comes to Jerusalem. Thus David, the elders of Israel, and the commanders of thousands went to bring up the ark of the covenant of the Lord with joy from the house of Obed-edom.
text.DRB: Therefore David and all the ancients of Israel, and the tribunes, went to fetch the Ark of the covenant of our Lord, out of the house of Obededom with joy.

text.NRSV: And because God helped the Levites who were carrying the ark of the covenant of the Lord, they sacrificed seven bulls and seven rams.
text.Rotherham: And so it was that, when God helped the Levites, who were bearing the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, they sacrificed seven bullocks and seven rams.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when God helped the Levites that bare the ark of the covenant of the Lord, that they offered seven bullocks and seven rams.
text.NABRE: While God helped the Levites to carry the ark of the covenant of the Lord, they sacrificed seven bulls and seven rams.
text.DRB: And when God had holpen the Levites, which carried the Ark of the covenant of our Lord, there were immolated seven oxen, and seven rams.

text.NRSV: David was clothed with a robe of fine linen, as also were all the Levites who were carrying the ark, and the singers, and Chenaniah the leader of the music of the singers; and David wore a linen ephod.
