text.Rotherham: Now, David, was wrapped about with a robe of byssus, with all the Levites who were bearing the ark, and the singers, and Chenaniah the leader of the service rendered by the singers,—but, upon David, was an ephod of white linen.
text.KJV: And David was clothed with a robe of fine linen, and all the Levites that bare the ark, and the singers, and Chenaniah the master of the song with the singers: David also had upon him an ephod of linen.
text.NABRE: David was vested in a robe of fine linen, as were all the Levites who carried the ark, the singers, and Chenaniah, the leader of song; David was also wearing a linen ephod.
text.DRB: Moreover David was clothed with a robe of fine linen, & all the Levites that carried the Ark, and the singing men, and Chonenias the Prince of prophecy among the singers: and David also was clothed with an Ephod of linen.

text.NRSV: So all Israel brought up the ark of the covenant of the Lord with shouting, to the sound of the horn, trumpets, and cymbals, playing loudly on harps and lyres.
text.Rotherham: So, all Israel, were bringing up the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, with shouting, and with sound of the horn, and with trumpets, and with cymbals,—sounding aloud, with harps, and lyres.
text.KJV: Thus all Israel brought up the ark of the covenant of the Lord with shouting, and with sound of the cornet, and with trumpets, and with cymbals, making a noise with psalteries and harps.
text.NABRE: Thus all Israel brought up the ark of the covenant of the Lord with joyful shouting, to the sound of horns, trumpets, and cymbals, and the music of harps and lyres.
text.DRB: And all Israel brought the Ark of the covenant of our Lord in jubilee, and sounding with the sound of shalm, and with trumpets, and cymbals, and nables, and harps.

text.NRSV: As the ark of the covenant of the Lord came to the city of David, Michal daughter of Saul looked out of the window, and saw King David leaping and dancing; and she despised him in her heart.
text.Rotherham: And so it was that, when the ark of the covenant of Yahweh came in as far as the city of David,—and Michal daughter of Saul looked forth through the window, and saw King David, dancing and playing, that she despised him in her heart.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, as the ark of the covenant of the Lord came to the city of David, that Michal the daughter of Saul looking out at a window saw king David dancing and playing: and she despised him in her heart.
text.NABRE: But as the ark of the covenant of the Lord was entering the City of David, Michal, daughter of Saul, looked down from her window, and when she saw King David leaping and dancing, she despised him in her heart.
text.DRB: And when the Ark of the covenant of our Lord was come unto the city of David, Michol the daughter of Saul looking forth through a window, saw David the king dancing and playing, and she despised him in her heart.

text.NRSV: They brought the ark of God, and set it inside the tent that David had pitched for it; and they offered burnt offerings and offerings of well-being before God.
text.Rotherham: So they brought in the ark of God, and placed it in the midst of the tent, which David had pitched for it,—and they brought near ascending-sacrifices and peace-offerings, before God.
text.KJV: So they brought the ark of God, and set it in the midst of the tent that David had pitched for it: and they offered burnt sacrifices and peace offerings before God.
text.NABRE: Chapter 16 They brought in the ark of God and set it within the tent which David had pitched for it. Then they sacrificed burnt offerings and communion offerings to God.
text.DRB: They therefore brought the Ark of God, and set it in the midst of the tabernacle, which David had pitched for it: and they offered holocausts, and pacifics before God.

text.NRSV: When David had finished offering the burnt offerings and the offerings of well-being, he blessed the people in the name of the Lord;
text.Rotherham: And, when David had made an end of offering the ascending-sacrifices, and the peace-offerings, he blessed the people, in the name of Yahweh.
text.KJV: And when David had made an end of offering the burnt offerings and the peace offerings, he blessed the people in the name of the Lord.
text.NABRE: When David had finished sacrificing the burnt offerings and communion offerings, he blessed the people in the name of the Lord,
text.DRB: And when David had finished offering holocausts, and pacifics, he blessed the people in the name of our Lord.

text.NRSV: and he distributed to every person in Israel—man and woman alike—to each a loaf of bread, a portion of meat, and a cake of raisins.
text.Rotherham: And he apportioned, to every one of Israel, both man and woman,—to every one, a loaf of bread, and a sweet drink, and a raisin cake.
text.KJV: And he dealt to every one of Israel, both man and woman, to every one a loaf of bread, and a good piece of flesh, and a flagon of wine.
text.NABRE: and distributed to every Israelite, to every man and every woman, a loaf of bread, a piece of meat, and a raisin cake.
text.DRB: And he divided to all throughout every one, from man unto woman, a loaf of bread, and a piece of roasted beef, and flour fried with oil.

text.NRSV: He appointed certain of the Levites as ministers before the ark of the Lord, to invoke, to thank, and to praise the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And he placed before the ark of Yahweh certain of the Levites, as attendants,—and to celebrate, and to give thanks and render praise, unto Yahweh, God of Israel;
text.KJV: And he appointed certain of the Levites to minister before the ark of the Lord, and to record, and to thank and praise the Lord God of Israel:
text.NABRE: David’s Directives for the Levites. He then appointed certain Levites to minister before the ark of the Lord, to celebrate, thank, and praise the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.DRB: And he appointed before the Ark of our Lord of the Levites, that should minister, and should remember his works, and glorify, and praise our Lord the God of Israel:

text.NRSV: Asaph was the chief, and second to him Zechariah, Jeiel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Mattithiah, Eliab, Benaiah, Obed-edom, and Jeiel, with harps and lyres; Asaph was to sound the cymbals,
text.Rotherham: Asaph the chief, and, second to him, Zechariah,—Jeiel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Mattithiah, and Eliab, and Benaiah, and Obed-edom, and Jeiel, with instruments of harps, and with lyres, and Asaph with cymbals, sounding aloud:
text.KJV: Asaph the chief, and next to him Zechariah, Jeiel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Mattithiah, and Eliab, and Benaiah, and Obed–edom: and Jeiel with psalteries and with harps; but Asaph made a sound with cymbals;
text.NABRE: Asaph was their chief, and second to him were Zechariah, Uzziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Mattithiah, Eliab, Benaiah, Obed-edom, and Jeiel. These were to play on harps and lyres, while Asaph was to sound the cymbals,
text.DRB: Asaph the Prince, and the second after him Zacharias: moreover Jahiel, and Semiramoth, and Jehiel, and Mathathias, and Eliab, and Banaias, and Obededom: and Jehiel over the instruments of psaltery, and the harps: and Asaph to sound upon the cymbals:

text.NRSV: and the priests Benaiah and Jahaziel were to blow trumpets regularly before the ark of the covenant of God.
text.Rotherham: and Benaiah and Jahaziel, the priests,—with trumpets continually, before the ark of the covenant of God.
text.KJV: Benaiah also and Jahaziel the priests with trumpets continually before the ark of the covenant of God.
text.NABRE: and the priests Benaiah and Jahaziel were to be the regular trumpeters before the ark of the covenant of God.
text.DRB: but Banaias, and Jaziel Priests, to sound the trumpet continually before the Ark of the covenant of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then on that day David first appointed the singing of praises to the Lord by Asaph and his kindred.
text.Rotherham: On that day, then, David gave [these songs] in chief, to give thanks unto Yahweh,—through Asaph, and his brethren:—
text.KJV: Then on that day David delivered first this psalm to thank the Lord into the hand of Asaph and his brethren.
text.NABRE: On that same day, David appointed Asaph and his brothers to sing for the first time these praises of the Lord:
text.DRB: In that day David made Asaph, prince to confess to our Lord and his brethren.

text.NRSV: O give thanks to the Lord, call on his name, make known his deeds among the peoples.
text.Rotherham: Give ye thanks to Yahweh, Call upon his name, Make known among the peoples, his doings;
text.KJV: Give thanks unto the Lord, call upon his name, make known his deeds among the people.
text.NABRE: Give thanks to the Lord, invoke his name; make known among the peoples his deeds.
text.DRB: Confess ye to our Lord, and invocate his name: make his inventions known among the peoples.

text.NRSV: Sing to him, sing praises to him, tell of all his wonderful works.
text.Rotherham: Sing ye to him, Make music unto him, Speak ye of all his wonders.
text.KJV: Sing unto him, sing psalms unto him, talk ye of all his wondrous works.
text.NABRE: Sing praise, play music; proclaim all his wondrous deeds.
text.DRB: Chant to him, and sing to him: & tell ye all his marvelous things.

text.NRSV: Glory in his holy name; let the hearts of those who seek the Lord rejoice.
text.Rotherham: Boast yourselves in his holy name, Joyful be the heart of them who are seeking Yahweh.
text.KJV: Glory ye in his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice that seek the Lord.
text.NABRE: Glory in his holy name; rejoice, O hearts that seek the Lord!
text.DRB: Praise ye his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice, that seek our Lord.

text.NRSV: Seek the Lord and his strength, seek his presence continually.
text.Rotherham: Search out Yahweh, and his power, Seek diligently his face, at all times.
text.KJV: Seek the Lord and his strength, seek his face continually.
text.NABRE: Rely on the mighty Lord; constantly seek his face.
text.DRB: Seek ye our Lord, and his power: seek ye his face always.

text.NRSV: Remember the wonderful works he has done, his miracles, and the judgments he uttered,
text.Rotherham: Remember his wonders which he hath done, His splendid deeds, and the just decisions of his mouth.
text.KJV: Remember his marvellous works that he hath done, his wonders, and the judgments of his mouth;
text.NABRE: Recall the wondrous deeds he has done, his signs, and his words of judgment,
text.DRB: Remember his marvelous things which he hath done: his signs, and the judgements of his mouth.

text.NRSV: O offspring of his servant Israel, children of Jacob, his chosen ones.
text.Rotherham: O ye seed of Israel his servant, sons of Jacob his chosen ones:
text.KJV: O ye seed of Israel his servant, ye children of Jacob, his chosen ones.
text.NABRE: You sons of Israel, his servants, offspring of Jacob, the chosen ones!
text.DRB: The seed of Israel his servant: the children of Jacob his elect.

text.NRSV: He is the Lord our God; his judgments are in all the earth.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh himself, is our God, through all the land, are his just decisions.
text.KJV: He is the Lord our God; his judgments are in all the earth.
text.NABRE: The Lord is our God; who rules the whole earth.
text.DRB: He is the Lord our God: in all the earth are his judgements.

text.NRSV: Remember his covenant forever, the word that he commanded, for a thousand generations,
text.Rotherham: Remember yea to times age-abiding, his covenant, the word he hath commanded, to a thousand generations;
text.KJV: Be ye mindful always of his covenant; the word which he commanded to a thousand generations;
text.NABRE: He remembers forever his covenant the pact imposed for a thousand generations—
text.DRB: Remember for ever his covenant: the word, which he commanded unto a thousand generations.

text.NRSV: the covenant that he made with Abraham, his sworn promise to Isaac,
text.Rotherham: Which he solemnised with Abraham, and his oath, to Isaac;
text.KJV: Even of the covenant which he made with Abraham, and of his oath unto Isaac;
text.NABRE: Which was made with Abraham, confirmed by oath to Isaac,
text.DRB: Which he covenanted with Abraham: and his oath with Isaac.

text.NRSV: which he confirmed to Jacob as a statute, to Israel as an everlasting covenant,
text.Rotherham: And confirmed it, unto Jacob, for a statute, unto Israel, as a covenant age-abiding:
text.KJV: And hath confirmed the same to Jacob for a law, and to Israel for an everlasting covenant,
text.NABRE: And ratified as binding for Jacob, an everlasting covenant for Israel:
text.DRB: And he appointed it to Jacob for a precept: and to Israel for an everlasting covenant:

text.NRSV: saying, “To you I will give the land of Canaan as your portion for an inheritance.”
text.Rotherham: Saying, To thee, will I give the land of Canaan, as your inherited portion:
text.KJV: Saying, Unto thee will I give the land of Canaan, the lot of your inheritance;
text.NABRE: “To you will I give the land of Canaan, your own allotted heritage.”
text.DRB: Saying: To thee will I give the Land of Chanaan: the cord of your inheritance.

text.NRSV: When they were few in number, of little account, and strangers in the land,
text.Rotherham: While, as yet, ye were men who could be counted, a very few, and sojourners therein:
text.KJV: When ye were but few, even a few, and strangers in it.
text.NABRE: When they were few in number, a handful, and strangers there,
text.DRB: When they were few in number: small and sojourners thereof.

text.NRSV: wandering from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another people,
text.Rotherham: And they wandered from nation to nation, and from one kingdom, to another people:
text.KJV: And when they went from nation to nation, and from one kingdom to another people;
text.NABRE: Wandering from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another,
text.DRB: And they passed from nation into nation: and from Kingdom to another people.

text.NRSV: he allowed no one to oppress them; he rebuked kings on their account,
text.Rotherham: He suffered no man to oppress them, And reproved—for their sakes—kings!
text.KJV: He suffered no man to do them wrong: yea, he reproved kings for their sakes,
text.NABRE: He let no one oppress them; for their sake he rebuked kings:
text.DRB: He suffered not any man to calumniate them: but rebuked kings for their sake.

text.NRSV: saying, “Do not touch my anointed ones; do my prophets no harm.”
text.Rotherham: Ye may not touch mine Anointed ones, And, to my Prophets, may ye do no wrong.
text.KJV: Saying, Touch not mine anointed, and do my prophets no harm.
text.NABRE: “Do not touch my anointed, to my prophets do no harm.”
text.DRB: Touch not my anointed: and unto my Prophets be not malicious.

text.NRSV: Sing to the Lord, all the earth. Tell of his salvation from day to day.
text.Rotherham: Sing to Yahweh, all the earth, Tell the tidings, from day to day, of his salvation:
text.KJV: Sing unto the Lord, all the earth; shew forth from day to day his salvation.
text.NABRE: Sing to the Lord, all the earth, announce his salvation, day after day.
text.DRB: Sing ye to our Lord all the earth: shew forth from day to day his salvation.

text.NRSV: Declare his glory among the nations, his marvelous works among all the peoples.
text.Rotherham: Recount, among the nations, his glory, among all the peoples, his wonders.
text.KJV: Declare his glory among the heathen; his marvellous works among all nations.
text.NABRE: Tell his glory among the nations; among all peoples, his wondrous deeds.
text.DRB: Tell his glory among the gentiles: among all peoples his marvelous works.

text.NRSV: For great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised; he is to be revered above all gods.
text.Rotherham: For great is Yahweh, and worthy to be mightily praised, and to be revered is he above all gods;
text.KJV: For great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised: he also is to be feared above all gods.
text.NABRE: For great is the Lord and highly to be praised; to be feared above all gods.
text.DRB: Because our Lord is great, and laudable exceedingly: and terrible over all gods.

text.NRSV: For all the gods of the peoples are idols, but the Lord made the heavens.
text.Rotherham: For, all the gods of the peoples, are things of nought, but, Yahweh, made, the heavens.
text.KJV: For all the gods of the people are idols: but the Lord made the heavens.
text.NABRE: For the gods of the nations all do nothing, but the Lord made the heavens.
text.DRB: For all the gods of the peoples, be idols: but our Lord made the heavens.

text.NRSV: Honor and majesty are before him; strength and joy are in his place.
text.Rotherham: Praise and majesty, are before him, Strength and joy, are in his dwelling-place.
text.KJV: Glory and honour are in his presence; strength and gladness are in his place.
text.NABRE: Splendor and majesty go before him; power and rejoicing are in his holy place.
text.DRB: Confession and magnificence before him: strength and joy in his place.

text.NRSV: Ascribe to the Lord, O families of the peoples, ascribe to the Lord glory and strength.
text.Rotherham: Give to Yahweh, ye families of the peoples, Give to Yahweh, glory and strength:
text.KJV: Give unto the Lord, ye kindreds of the people, give unto the Lord glory and strength.
text.NABRE: Give to the Lord, you families of nations, give to the Lord glory and might;
text.DRB: Bring to our Lord ye families of peoples: bring to our Lord glory and empire.

text.NRSV: Ascribe to the Lord the glory due his name; bring an offering, and come before him. Worship the Lord in holy splendor;
text.Rotherham: Give to Yahweh, the glory of his name,—Bring ye a present, and enter before him, Bow down to Yahweh, in the adornment of holiness:
text.KJV: Give unto the Lord the glory due unto his name: bring an offering, and come before him: worship the Lord in the beauty of holiness.
text.NABRE: Give to the Lord the glory due his name! Bring gifts, and come before him; bow down to the Lord, splendid in holiness.
text.DRB: Give our Lord glory, to his name, elevate sacrifice, and come ye in his sight: and adore our Lord in holy honour.

text.NRSV: tremble before him, all the earth. The world is firmly established; it shall never be moved.
text.Rotherham: Tremble at his presence, all the earth, But the world, shall be established, it shall not be shaken.
text.KJV: Fear before him, all the earth: the world also shall be stable, that it be not moved.
text.NABRE: Tremble before him, all the earth; the world will surely stand fast, never to be moved.
text.DRB: Let all the earth be moved before his face: for he founded the world unmovable.

text.NRSV: Let the heavens be glad, and let the earth rejoice, and let them say among the nations, “The Lord is king!”
text.Rotherham: Let the heavens rejoice, and the earth exult, Let them say among the nations, Yahweh, hath become king!
text.KJV: Let the heavens be glad, and let the earth rejoice: and let men say among the nations, The Lord reigneth.
text.NABRE: Let the heavens be glad and the earth rejoice; let them say among the nations: The Lord is king.
text.DRB: Let the heavens be glad, and the earth rejoice: and let them say among nations, Our Lord hath reigned.

text.NRSV: Let the sea roar, and all that fills it; let the field exult, and everything in it.
text.Rotherham: Let the sea roar, and the fulness thereof, Let the field leap for joy, and all that is therein:
text.KJV: Let the sea roar, and the fulness thereof: let the fields rejoice, and all that is therein.
text.NABRE: Let the sea and what fills it resound; let the plains be joyful and all that is in them!
text.DRB: Let the sea thunder, and the fullness thereof: let the fields rejoice, and all things that are in them.

text.NRSV: Then shall the trees of the forest sing for joy before the Lord, for he comes to judge the earth.
text.Rotherham: Then, shall shout in triumph, the trees of the forest, before Yahweh, for he is coming, to judge the earth.
text.KJV: Then shall the trees of the wood sing out at the presence of the Lord, because he cometh to judge the earth.
text.NABRE: Then let all the trees of the forest exult before the Lord, who comes, who comes to rule the earth.
text.DRB: Then shall the trees of the forest praise before our Lord: because he is come to judge the earth.

text.NRSV: O give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; for his steadfast love endures forever.
text.Rotherham: Give ye thanks unto Yahweh, For he is good, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness.
text.KJV: O give thanks unto the Lord; for he is good; for his mercy endureth for ever.
text.NABRE: Give thanks to the Lord, who is good, whose love endures forever;
text.DRB: Confess ye to our Lord, because he is good: because his mercy is for ever.

text.NRSV: Say also: “Save us, O God of our salvation, and gather and rescue us from among the nations, that we may give thanks to your holy name, and glory in your praise.
text.Rotherham: And say ye, Save us, O God of our salvation, and gather us and deliver us, from among the nations,—that we may give thanks unto thy holy name, that we may triumph aloud in thy praise.
text.KJV: And say ye, Save us, O God of our salvation, and gather us together, and deliver us from the heathen, that we may give thanks to thy holy name, and glory in thy praise.
text.NABRE: And say, “Save us, O God, our savior, gather us and deliver us from among the nations, That we may give thanks to your holy name and glory in praising you.”
text.DRB: And say ye: Save us o God our Saviour: and gather us together, and deliver us out of the nations, that we may confess to thy holy name, and may rejoice in thy songs.

text.NRSV: Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting.” Then all the people said “Amen!” and praised the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Blessed be Yahweh, God of Israel, from one age even to another age. Then said all the people, Amen, And gave praise to Yahweh.
text.KJV: Blessed be the Lord God of Israel for ever and ever. And all the people said, Amen, and praised the Lord.
text.NABRE: Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting! Let all the people say, Amen! Hallelujah.
text.DRB: Blessed be our Lord the God of Israel from eternity unto eternity: and let all the people say: Amen, and hymn to God.

text.NRSV: David left Asaph and his kindred there before the ark of the covenant of the Lord to minister regularly before the ark as each day required,
text.Rotherham: So he left there, before the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, Asaph and his brethren,—to be in attendance before the ark continually, for the duty of a day on its day;
text.KJV: So he left there before the ark of the covenant of the Lord Asaph and his brethren, to minister before the ark continually, as every day’s work required:
text.NABRE: Then David left Asaph and his brothers there before the ark of the covenant of the Lord to minister before the ark regularly according to the daily ritual;
text.DRB: He therefore left Asaph there before the Ark of the covenant of our Lord, and his brethren to minister in the presence of the Ark continually day by day, & in their courses.

text.NRSV: and also Obed-edom and his sixty-eight kindred; while Obed-edom son of Jeduthun and Hosah were to be gatekeepers.
text.Rotherham: and Obed-edom with his brethren, sixty-eight,—and Obed-edom son of Jedithun and Hosah to be doorkeepers;
text.KJV: And Obed–edom with their brethren, threescore and eight; Obed–edom also the son of Jeduthun and Hosah to be porters:
text.NABRE: he also left there Obed-edom and sixty-eight of his brothers, including Obed-edom, son of Jeduthun, and Hosah, to be gatekeepers.
text.DRB: Moreover Obededom, and his brethren sixty eight: and Obededom the son of Idithun, and Hosa he appointed for porters.

text.NRSV: And he left the priest Zadok and his kindred the priests before the tabernacle of the Lord in the high place that was at Gibeon,
text.Rotherham: and Zadok the priest, and his brethren the priests, before the habitation of Yahweh,—in the high place that was in Gibeon:
text.KJV: And Zadok the priest, and his brethren the priests, before the tabernacle of the Lord in the high place that was at Gibeon,
text.NABRE: But the priest Zadok and his priestly brothers he left before the tabernacle of the Lord on the high place at Gibeon,
text.DRB: And Sadoc the Priest, and his brethren Priests, before the tabernacle of our Lord in the excelse, which was in Gabaon,

text.NRSV: to offer burnt offerings to the Lord on the altar of burnt offering regularly, morning and evening, according to all that is written in the law of the Lord that he commanded Israel.
text.Rotherham: to offer ascending-sacrifices unto Yahweh, upon the altar of ascending-sacrifice,—continually, morning and evening,—even according to all that is written in the law of Yahweh, which he commanded concerning Israel;
text.KJV: To offer burnt offerings unto the Lord upon the altar of the burnt offering continually morning and evening, and to do according to all that is written in the law of the Lord, which he commanded Israel;
text.NABRE: to make burnt offerings to the Lord on the altar for burnt offerings regularly, morning and evening, and to do all that is written in the law of the Lord which he commanded Israel.
text.DRB: that they should offer holocausts to our Lord upon the altar of holocaust continually, morning and evening, according to all things that are written in the law of our Lord, which he commanded Israel.

text.NRSV: With them were Heman and Jeduthun, and the rest of those chosen and expressly named to render thanks to the Lord, for his steadfast love endures forever.
text.Rotherham: and, with them, Heman and Jeduthun, and the rest of the chosen ones, who were distinguished by name,—to give thanks unto Yahweh, For, age-abiding, is his lovingkindness!
text.KJV: And with them Heman and Jeduthun, and the rest that were chosen, who were expressed by name, to give thanks to the Lord, because his mercy endureth for ever;
text.NABRE: With them were Heman and Jeduthun and the others who were chosen and designated by name to give thanks to the Lord, “whose love endures forever,”
text.DRB: And after him Heman, and Idithun, and the rest of the chosen men, every one by his name to confess unto our Lord: because his mercy is for ever.

text.NRSV: Heman and Jeduthun had with them trumpets and cymbals for the music, and instruments for sacred song. The sons of Jeduthun were appointed to the gate.
text.Rotherham: And, with them, Heman and Jeduthun, [with] the trumpets, and the cymbals, for sounding aloud, and the instruments for the songs of God,—and the sons of Jeduthun, for the gate.
text.KJV: And with them Heman and Jeduthun with trumpets and cymbals for those that should make a sound, and with musical instruments of God. And the sons of Jeduthun were porters.
text.NABRE: with trumpets and cymbals for accompaniment, and instruments for sacred song. The sons of Jeduthun kept the gate.
text.DRB: Heman also and Idithun sounding the trumpet, and quavering on the cymbals, and all musical instruments to sing unto God: and the sons of Idithun he made porters.

text.NRSV: Then all the people departed to their homes, and David went home to bless his household.
text.Rotherham: And all the people departed, every man to his house,—and David went round, to bless his own household.
text.KJV: And all the people departed every man to his house: and David returned to bless his house.
text.NABRE: Then all the people departed, each to their own homes, and David returned to bless his household.
text.DRB: And all the people returned into their house: and David, to bless also his house.

text.NRSV: Now when David settled in his house, David said to the prophet Nathan, “I am living in a house of cedar, but the ark of the covenant of the Lord is under a tent.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when David had taken up his abode in his house, that David said unto Nathan the prophet, Lo! I, am dwelling in a house of cedars, but, the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, is under curtains.
text.KJV: Now it came to pass, as David sat in his house, that David said to Nathan the prophet, Lo, I dwell in an house of cedars, but the ark of the covenant of the Lord remaineth under curtains.
text.NABRE: Chapter 17 The Oracle of Nathan. After David had taken up residence in his house, he said to Nathan the prophet, “See, I am living in a house of cedar, but the ark of the covenant of the Lord is under tentcloth.”
text.DRB: And when David dwelt in his house, he said to Nathan the Prophet: Behold I dwell in a house of cedar: and the Ark of the covenant of our Lord is under skins.

text.NRSV: Nathan said to David, “Do all that you have in mind, for God is with you.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Nathan unto David, All that is in thy heart, do,—for, God, is with thee.
text.KJV: Then Nathan said unto David, Do all that is in thine heart; for God is with thee.
text.NABRE: Nathan replied to David, “Whatever is in your heart, go and do, for God is with you.”
text.DRB: And Nathan said to David: All things, that are in thy heart do: for God is with thee.

text.NRSV: But that same night the word of the Lord came to Nathan, saying:
text.Rotherham: But so it was that, in that night, the word of God came unto Nathan, saying:
text.KJV: And it came to pass the same night, that the word of God came to Nathan, saying,
text.NABRE: But that same night the word of God came to Nathan:
text.DRB: Therefore that night the word of God came to Nathan, saying:

text.NRSV: “Go and tell my servant David: Thus says the Lord: You shall not build me a house to live in.
text.Rotherham: Go and say unto David my servant, Thus, saith Yahweh,—Thou, shalt not build me a house, to dwell in;
text.KJV: Go and tell David my servant, Thus saith the Lord, Thou shalt not build me an house to dwell in:
text.NABRE: Go and tell David my servant, Thus says the Lord: It is not you who are to build the house for me to dwell in.
text.DRB: Go, and speak to David my servant: Thus saith our Lord: Thou shalt not build me a house to dwell in.

text.NRSV: For I have not lived in a house since the day I brought out Israel to this day, but I have gone from tent to tent and from dwelling to dwelling.
text.Rotherham: seeing that I have not dwelt in a house, since the day that I brought up Israel, until this day,—but have been from tent to tent, and without a habitation.
text.KJV: For I have not dwelt in an house since the day that I brought up Israel unto this day; but have gone from tent to tent, and from one tabernacle to another.
text.NABRE: For I have never dwelt in a house, from the day I brought Israel up, even to this day, but I have been lodging in tent or tabernacle.
text.DRB: For neither have I remained in house from the time, that I brought out Israel, until this day: but I have been always changing places of tabernacle, & in tent,

text.NRSV: In all the places where I have moved about among all Israel, did I ever speak a word with any of the judges of Israel, whom I commanded to shepherd my people, saying, ‘Why have you not built me a house of cedar?’
text.Rotherham: Wheresoever I have wandered with all Israel, spake I ever, a word, with one of the judges of Israel, whom I charged to shepherd my people, saying,—Wherefore have ye not built me a house of cedars?
text.KJV: Wheresoever I have walked with all Israel, spake I a word to any of the judges of Israel, whom I commanded to feed my people, saying, Why have ye not built me an house of cedars?
text.NABRE: As long as I have wandered about with all Israel, did I ever say a word to any of the judges of Israel whom I commanded to shepherd my people, Why have you not built me a house of cedar?
text.DRB: abiding with all Israel. Did I speak to one, at the least, of all the judges of Israel whom I commanded to feed my people, and did I say: Why have you not built me a house of cedar?

text.NRSV: Now therefore thus you shall say to my servant David: Thus says the Lord of hosts: I took you from the pasture, from following the sheep, to be ruler over my people Israel;
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, thus, shalt thou say unto my servant David, Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts, I myself, took thee from the pasture, from after the flock,—to become leader over my people Israel;
text.KJV: Now therefore thus shalt thou say unto my servant David, Thus saith the Lord of hosts, I took thee from the sheepcote, even from following the sheep, that thou shouldest be ruler over my people Israel:
text.NABRE: Now then, speak thus to my servant David, Thus says the Lord of hosts: I took you from the pasture, from following the flock, to become ruler over my people Israel.
text.DRB: Now therefore so shalt thou speak to my servant David: Thus saith the Lord of hosts: I took thee, when in the pastures thou didest follow the flock, that thou shouldest be Prince of my people Israel.

text.NRSV: and I have been with you wherever you went, and have cut off all your enemies from before you; and I will make for you a name, like the name of the great ones of the earth.
text.Rotherham: and was with thee whithersoever thou didst go, and have cut off all thine enemies, from before thee,—and will make thee a name, like the name of the great ones, who are in the earth;
text.KJV: And I have been with thee whithersoever thou hast walked, and have cut off all thine enemies from before thee, and have made thee a name like the name of the great men that are in the earth.
text.NABRE: I was with you wherever you went, and I cut down all your enemies before you. I will make your name like that of the greatest on the earth.
text.DRB: And I have been with thee whither soever thou wentest: and I have slain all thine enemies before thee, and have made thee a name as of one of the great ones, that are renowned in the earth.

text.NRSV: I will appoint a place for my people Israel, and will plant them, so that they may live in their own place, and be disturbed no more; and evildoers shall wear them down no more, as they did formerly,
text.Rotherham: and will appoint a place for my people Israel, and will plant them, and they shall inhabit their place, and be unsettled no more,—neither shall the sons of perversity again wear them out, as at the first;
text.KJV: Also I will ordain a place for my people Israel, and will plant them, and they shall dwell in their place, and shall be moved no more; neither shall the children of wickedness waste them any more, as at the beginning,
text.NABRE: I will assign a place for my people Israel and I will plant them in it to dwell there; they will never again be disturbed, nor shall the wicked ever again oppress them, as they did at the beginning,
text.DRB: And I have given a place to my people Israel: it shall be planted, and shall dwell therein, and shall be moved no more, neither shall the children of iniquity consume them, as from the beginning,

text.NRSV: from the time that I appointed judges over my people Israel; and I will subdue all your enemies. Moreover I declare to you that the Lord will build you a house.
text.Rotherham: even from the days when I put judges in charge over my people Israel, and have subdued all thine enemies,—that I might make thee great, yea, a house, will Yahweh build for thee.
text.KJV: And since the time that I commanded judges to be over my people Israel. Moreover I will subdue all thine enemies. Furthermore I tell thee that the Lord will build thee an house.
text.NABRE: and during all the time when I appointed judges over my people Israel. And I will subdue all your enemies. Moreover, I declare to you that the Lord will build you a house:
text.DRB: since the days that I gave judges to my people Israel, & humbled all thine enemies. I therefore tell thee, that our Lord will build thee a house.

text.NRSV: When your days are fulfilled to go to be with your ancestors, I will raise up your offspring after you, one of your own sons, and I will establish his kingdom.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be that, when thy days are fulfilled, to sleep with thy fathers, then will I raise up thy seed after thee, who shall be of thy sons,—and I will establish his kingdom.
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass, when thy days be expired that thou must go to be with thy fathers, that I will raise up thy seed after thee, which shall be of thy sons; and I will establish his kingdom.
text.NABRE: when your days have been completed and you must join your ancestors, I will raise up your offspring after you who will be one of your own sons, and I will establish his kingdom.
text.DRB: And when thou shalt have accomplished thy days to go to thy fathers, I will raise up thy seed after thee, which shall be of thy children: and I will establish his Kingdom.

text.NRSV: He shall build a house for me, and I will establish his throne forever.
text.Rotherham: He, shall build me a house,—and I will establish his throne unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: He shall build me an house, and I will stablish his throne for ever.
text.NABRE: He it is who shall build me a house, and I will establish his throne forever.
text.DRB: He shall build me a house, and I will confirm his throne for ever.

text.NRSV: I will be a father to him, and he shall be a son to me. I will not take my steadfast love from him, as I took it from him who was before you,
text.Rotherham: I, will become his father, and, he, shall become my son,—and, my lovingkindness, will I not cause to depart from him,—as I caused it to depart from him who was before thee;
text.KJV: I will be his father, and he shall be my son: and I will not take my mercy away from him, as I took it from him that was before thee:
text.NABRE: I will be a father to him, and he shall be a son to me, and I will not withdraw my favor from him as I withdrew it from the one who was before you;
text.DRB: I will be to him for a father, and he shall be to me for a son: and my mercy I will not take from him, as I took from him, that was before thee.

text.NRSV: but I will confirm him in my house and in my kingdom forever, and his throne shall be established forever.”
text.Rotherham: but I will cause him to remain over my house and over my kingdom, unto times age-abiding,—and, his throne, shall be established, unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: But I will settle him in mine house and in my kingdom for ever: and his throne shall be established for evermore.
text.NABRE: but I will maintain him in my house and in my kingdom forever, and his throne shall be firmly established forever.
text.DRB: And I will establish him in my house, and in my Kingdom for ever: and his throne shall be most firm for ever.

text.NRSV: In accordance with all these words and all this vision, Nathan spoke to David.
text.Rotherham: According to all these words, and according to all this vision, so, spake Nathan unto David.
text.KJV: According to all these words, and according to all this vision, so did Nathan speak unto David.
text.NABRE: In accordance with all these words and this whole vision Nathan spoke to David.
text.DRB: According to all these words, and according to all this vision, so spake Nathan to David.

text.NRSV: Then King David went in and sat before the Lord, and said, “Who am I, O Lord God, and what is my house, that you have brought me thus far?
text.Rotherham: Then entered King David, and tarried before Yahweh,—and said, Who am, I, O Yahweh God, and what is my house, that thou hast brought me hitherto;
text.KJV: And David the king came and sat before the Lord, and said, Who am I, O Lord God, and what is mine house, that thou hast brought me hitherto?
text.NABRE: David’s Thanksgiving. Then King David came in and sat in the Lord ’s presence, and said: “Who am I, Lord God, and what is my house, that you should have brought me so far?
text.DRB: And when king David came, and sat before our Lord, he said: Who am I Lord God, and what is my house, that thou shouldest give me such things?

text.NRSV: And even this was a small thing in your sight, O God; you have also spoken of your servant’s house for a great while to come. You regard me as someone of high rank, O Lord God!
text.Rotherham: and hast made this seem little in thine eyes, O God, in that thou hast spoken concerning the house of thy servant, for a great while to come? Thus wilt thou provide for me, according to the rank of manhood, and hast exalted me, O Yahweh God!
text.KJV: And yet this was a small thing in thine eyes, O God; for thou hast also spoken of thy servant’s house for a great while to come, and hast regarded me according to the estate of a man of high degree, O Lord God.
text.NABRE: And yet, even this is too little in your sight, O God! For you have made a promise regarding your servant’s house reaching into the future, and you have looked on me as henceforth the most notable of men, Lord God.
text.DRB: But this also hath seemed little in thy sight, and therefore thou hast spoken concerning the house of thy servant for time to come also: and hast made me renowned above all men Lord God.

text.NRSV: And what more can David say to you for honoring your servant? You know your servant.
text.Rotherham: What, yet further, can David say unto thee, [thus] to honour thy servant,—seeing that, thou thyself, knowest, thine own servant?
text.KJV: What can David speak more to thee for the honour of thy servant? for thou knowest thy servant.
text.NABRE: What more can David say to you? You have known your servant.
text.DRB: What can David add farther, whereas thou hast so glorified thy servant, and known him?

text.NRSV: For your servant’s sake, O Lord, and according to your own heart, you have done all these great deeds, making known all these great things.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh, for the sake of thy servant, and according to thine own heart, hast thou done all this great thing,—making known all the great things.
text.KJV: O Lord, for thy servant’s sake, and according to thine own heart, hast thou done all this greatness, in making known all these great things.
text.NABRE: Lord, for your servant’s sake and in keeping with your purpose, you have done this great thing.
text.DRB: Lord for thy servant according to thy heart thou hast done all this magnificence, and would have all thy great wonders to be known.

text.NRSV: There is no one like you, O Lord, and there is no God besides you, according to all that we have heard with our ears.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh, there is none like unto thee, yea there is no God besides thee, according to all that we have heard with our ears.
text.KJV: O Lord, there is none like thee, neither is there any God beside thee, according to all that we have heard with our ears.
text.NABRE: Lord, there is no one like you, no God but you, just as we have always heard.
text.DRB: Lord, there is not the like to thee: and there is none other beside thee, of all whom we have heard with our ears.

text.NRSV: Who is like your people Israel, one nation on the earth whom God went to redeem to be his people, making for yourself a name for great and terrible things, in driving out nations before your people whom you redeemed from Egypt?
text.Rotherham: Who then is like thy people Israel, a nation alone in the earth,—whom God went to redeem, to be his own people, to make for thyself a name for great and fearful things, to drive out, nations, from before thy people, whom thou didst redeem out of Egypt;
text.KJV: And what one nation in the earth is like thy people Israel, whom God went to redeem to be his own people, to make thee a name of greatness and terribleness, by driving out nations from before thy people, whom thou hast redeemed out of Egypt?
text.NABRE: “Is there, like your people Israel, whom you redeemed from Egypt, another nation on earth whom a god went to redeem as his people? You won for yourself a name for great and awesome deeds by driving out the nations before your people.
text.DRB: For what other is there, as thy people Israel, one nation in the earth, to the which God went, to deliver it and make it his people, and with his greatness & terrors cast out the nations before the face of it, which he delivered out of Ægypt?

text.NRSV: And you made your people Israel to be your people forever; and you, O Lord, became their God.
text.Rotherham: and didst appoint thy people Israel for thyself as a people, unto times age-abiding,—thou thyself, also, O Yahweh, becoming their God?
text.KJV: For thy people Israel didst thou make thine own people for ever; and thou, Lord, becamest their God.
text.NABRE: You made your people Israel your own forever, and you, Lord, became their God.
text.DRB: And thou hast made thy people Israel to be thy people for ever, and thou Lord art made the God thereof.

text.NRSV: “And now, O Lord, as for the word that you have spoken concerning your servant and concerning his house, let it be established forever, and do as you have promised.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, O Yahweh, the word which thou hast spoken concerning thy servant, and concerning his house, be it steadfast unto times age-abiding,—and do as thou hast spoken:
text.KJV: Therefore now, Lord, let the thing that thou hast spoken concerning thy servant and concerning his house be established for ever, and do as thou hast said.
text.NABRE: Now, Lord, may the promise that you have spoken concerning your servant and his house remain firm forever. Bring about what you have promised,
text.DRB: Now therefore Lord, the word, which thou hast spoken to thy servant, and concerning his house, be it confirmed for ever, and do as thou hast spoken.

text.NRSV: Thus your name will be established and magnified forever in the saying, ‘The Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, is Israel’s God’; and the house of your servant David will be established before you.
text.Rotherham: yea let it be steadfast, and so let thy Name be magnified unto times age-abiding, saying, Yahweh of hosts, the God of Israel, is a God to Israel! seeing that, the house of David thy servant, is established before thee.
text.KJV: Let it even be established, that thy name may be magnified for ever, saying, The Lord of hosts is the God of Israel, even a God to Israel: and let the house of David thy servant be established before thee.
text.NABRE: that your name, Lord of hosts, God of Israel, may be great and abide forever, while the house of your servant is established in your presence.
text.DRB: And let thy name remain and be magnified for ever: and let it be said: The Lord of hosts is God of Israel, & the house of David his servant permanent before him.

text.NRSV: For you, my God, have revealed to your servant that you will build a house for him; therefore your servant has found it in his heart to pray before you.
text.Rotherham: For, thou, O my God, hast unveiled the ear of thy servant, that thou wilt build for him a house,—for this cause, hath thy servant found [it in his heart] to pray before thee.
text.KJV: For thou, O my God, hast told thy servant that thou wilt build him an house: therefore thy servant hath found in his heart to pray before thee.
text.NABRE: “Because you, my God, have revealed to your servant that you will build him a house, your servant dares to pray before you.
text.DRB: For thou Lord my God hast revealed the ear of thy servant, to build him a house: and therefore thy servant hath found confidence, to pray before thee.

text.NRSV: And now, O Lord, you are God, and you have promised this good thing to your servant;
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, O Yahweh, thou, art God,—therefore hast thou spoken concerning thy servant this goodness.
text.KJV: And now, Lord, thou art God, and hast promised this goodness unto thy servant:
text.NABRE: Since you, Lord, are truly God and have made this generous promise to your servant,
text.DRB: Now therefore Lord thou art God: & thou hast spoken to thy servant so great benefits.

text.NRSV: now therefore may it please you to bless the house of your servant, that it may continue forever before you. For you, O Lord, have blessed and are blessed forever.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, let it please thee to bless the house of thy servant, that it may be unto times age-abiding before thee,—for, thou O Yahweh, hast blessed, and it is to be blessed unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: Now therefore let it please thee to bless the house of thy servant, that it may be before thee for ever: for thou blessest, O Lord, and it shall be blessed for ever.
text.NABRE: do, then, bless the house of your servant, that it may be in your presence forever—since it is you, Lord, who blessed it, it is blessed forever.”
text.DRB: And thou hast begun to bless the house of thy servant, that it be always before thee: for thee o Lord blessing it, it shall be blessed for ever.

text.NRSV: Some time afterward, David attacked the Philistines and subdued them; he took Gath and its villages from the Philistines.
text.Rotherham: And it canto to pass, after this, that David smote the Philistines, and subdued them,—and took Gath and her villages, out of the hand of the Philistines.
text.KJV: Now after this it came to pass, that David smote the Philistines, and subdued them, and took Gath and her towns out of the hand of the Philistines.
text.NABRE: Chapter 18 David’s Victories. After this, David defeated the Philistines and subdued them; and he took Gath and its towns away from the Philistines.
text.DRB: And it came to pass after these things, that David struck the Philisthiims, and humbled them, and took away Geth, and her daughters out of the hand of the Philisthiims,

text.NRSV: He defeated Moab, and the Moabites became subject to David and brought tribute.
text.Rotherham: And he smote Moab,—and the Moabites became servants to David, bearers of a present.
text.KJV: And he smote Moab; and the Moabites became David’s servants, and brought gifts.
text.NABRE: He also defeated Moab, and the Moabites became David’s subjects, paying tribute.
text.DRB: and struck Moab; and the Moabites were made David's servants, offering him gifts.

text.NRSV: David also struck down King Hadadezer of Zobah, toward Hamath, as he went to set up a monument at the river Euphrates.
text.Rotherham: And David smote Hadarezer king of Zobah, towards Hamath,—as he went to establish his hold of the river Euphrates.
text.KJV: And David smote Hadarezer king of Zobah unto Hamath, as he went to stablish his dominion by the river Euphrates.
text.NABRE: David then defeated Hadadezer, king of Zobah, toward Hamath, who was on his way to set up his victory stele at the river Euphrates.
text.DRB: At that time David struck Adarezer also the king of Soba of the country of Hemath, when he went on to dilate his empire as far as the river Euphrates.

text.NRSV: David took from him one thousand chariots, seven thousand cavalry, and twenty thousand foot soldiers. David hamstrung all the chariot horses, but left one hundred of them.
text.Rotherham: And David captured from him a thousand chariots, and seven thousand horsemen, and twenty thousand footmen,—and David destroyed all the chariots, saving that he reserved from them a hundred chariots.
text.KJV: And David took from him a thousand chariots, and seven thousand horsemen, and twenty thousand footmen: David also houghed all the chariot horses, but reserved of them an hundred chariots.
text.NABRE: David captured from him one thousand chariots, seven thousand horsemen, and twenty thousand foot soldiers. David hamstrung all the chariot horses, but left one hundred for his chariots.
text.DRB: David therefore took a thousand chariots of his, & seven thousand horsemen, & twenty thousand footmen, & he houghsinewed all the chariot horses, saving an hundred chariots, which he reserved to himself.

text.NRSV: When the Arameans of Damascus came to help King Hadadezer of Zobah, David killed twenty-two thousand Arameans.
text.Rotherham: And, when the Syrians of Damascus came to help Hadarezer king of Zobah, David smote of the Syrians, twenty-two thousand men.
text.KJV: And when the Syrians of Damascus came to help Hadarezer king of Zobah, David slew of the Syrians two and twenty thousand men.
text.NABRE: The Arameans of Damascus came to help Hadadezer, king of Zobah, but David also defeated twenty-two thousand of their men in Aram.
text.DRB: And the Syrian also of Damascus came moreover, to give aid to Adarezer the king of Soba, but David struck also of his two & twenty thousand men.

text.NRSV: Then David put garrisons in Aram of Damascus; and the Arameans became subject to David, and brought tribute. The Lord gave victory to David wherever he went.
text.Rotherham: And David put [garrisons] in Syria of Damascus, and so it was that the Syrians became, David’s, servants, bearers of a present,—so Yahweh gave victory to David, whithersoever he went.
text.KJV: Then David put garrisons in Syria–damascus; and the Syrians became David’s servants, and brought gifts. Thus the Lord preserved David whithersoever he went.
text.NABRE: Then David set up garrisons in the Damascus region of Aram, and the Arameans became David’s subjects, paying tribute. Thus the Lord made David victorious in all his campaigns.
text.DRB: And he put soldiers in Damascus, that Syria also should serve him, & bring gifts. And our Lord holp him in all things, to the which he went.

text.NRSV: David took the gold shields that were carried by the attendants of Hadadezer, and brought them to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And David took the shields of gold, that were upon the servants of Hadarezer,—and brought them to Jerusalem;
text.KJV: And David took the shields of gold that were on the servants of Hadarezer, and brought them to Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: David took the golden shields that were carried by Hadadezer’s attendants and brought them to Jerusalem.
text.DRB: David also took the golden quivers, which the servants of Adarezer had, and he brought them into Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: From Tibhath and from Cun, cities of Hadadezer, David took a vast quantity of bronze; with it Solomon made the bronze sea and the pillars and the bronze vessels.
text.Rotherham: also, from Tibhath and from Cun, cities of Hadarezer, David took very much bronze,—therewith, did Solomon make the sea of bronze, and the pillars, and the vessels of bronze.
text.KJV: Likewise from Tibhath, and from Chun, cities of Hadarezer, brought David very much brass, wherewith Solomon made the brasen sea, and the pillars, and the vessels of brass.
text.NABRE: David likewise took away from Tibhath and Cun, cities of Hadadezer, large quantities of bronze; Solomon later used it to make the bronze sea and the pillars and the vessels of bronze.
text.DRB: Moreover of Thebath, and Chun, the cities of Adarezer, very much brass, of which Salomon made the brazen Sea, and Pillars, and brazen vessels.

text.NRSV: When King Tou of Hamath heard that David had defeated the whole army of King Hadadezer of Zobah,
text.Rotherham: And, when Tou, king of Hamath, heard that David had smitten all the force of Hadarezer king of Zobah,
text.KJV: Now when Tou king of Hamath heard how David had smitten all the host of Hadarezer king of Zobah;
text.NABRE: When Tou, king of Hamath, heard that David had defeated the entire army of Hadadezer, king of Zobah,
text.DRB: Which when Tou the king of Hemath had heard, to wit, that David had strucken all the army of Adarezer the king of Soba,

text.NRSV: he sent his son Hadoram to King David, to greet him and to congratulate him because he had fought against Hadadezer and defeated him. Now Hadadezer had often been at war with Tou. He sent all sorts of articles of gold, of silver, and of bronze;
text.Rotherham: he sent Hadoram his son unto King David, to ask after his welfare, and to bless him, because he had made war against Hadarezer and had smitten him, for, a man making wars against Tou, had been Hadarezer,—and [he had with him] all manner of vessels of gold and silver and bronze;
text.KJV: He sent Hadoram his son to king David, to enquire of his welfare, and to congratulate him, because he had fought against Hadarezer, and smitten him; (for Hadarezer had war with Tou;) and with him all manner of vessels of gold and silver and brass.
text.NABRE: he sent his son Hadoram to wish King David well and to congratulate him on having waged a victorious war against Hadadezer; for Hadadezer had been at war with Tou. He also brought gold, silver and bronze articles of every sort.
text.DRB: he sent Adoram his son to king David, to desire peace of him, and to congratulate him that he had strucken, and had overthrown Adarezer: for Tou was adversary to Adarezer.

text.NRSV: these also King David dedicated to the Lord, together with the silver and gold that he had carried off from all the nations, from Edom, Moab, the Ammonites, the Philistines, and Amalek.
text.Rotherham: them also, did King David hallow unto Yahweh, with the silver and the gold, that he bare away from all the nations,—from Edom, and from Moab, and from the sons of Ammon, and from the Philistines, and from Amalek.
text.KJV: Them also king David dedicated unto the Lord, with the silver and the gold that he brought from all these nations; from Edom, and from Moab, and from the children of Ammon, and from the Philistines, and from Amalek.
text.NABRE: These also King David consecrated to the Lord along with all the silver and gold that he had taken from the nations: from Edom, Moab, the Ammonites, the Philistines, and Amalek.
text.DRB: But all the vessels also of gold, and silver, and brass king David consecrated to our Lord, with the silver and gold, which he had taken out of all the nations, as well of Idumea, and Moab, and the children of Ammon, as of the Philisthiims & Amalec.

text.NRSV: Abishai son of Zeruiah killed eighteen thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, Abishai, son of Zeruiah, smote Edom, in the Valley of Salt,—eighteen thousand;
text.KJV: Moreover Abishai the son of Zeruiah slew of the Edomites in the valley of salt eighteen thousand.
text.NABRE: Abishai, the son of Zeruiah, also defeated eighteen thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt.
text.DRB: And Abisai the son of Sarvia struck Edom in the Vale of salt pits, eighteen thousand:

text.NRSV: He put garrisons in Edom; and all the Edomites became subject to David. And the Lord gave victory to David wherever he went.
text.Rotherham: and set, in Edom, garrisons, and all Edom became servants unto David,—and Yahweh gave David the victory, whithersoever he went.
text.KJV: And he put garrisons in Edom; and all the Edomites became David’s servants. Thus the Lord preserved David whithersoever he went.
text.NABRE: He set up garrisons in Edom, and all the Edomites became David’s subjects. Thus the Lord brought David victory in all his undertakings.
text.DRB: and he appointed a garrison in Edom, that Idumea should serve David, and our Lord saved David in all things to which he went.

text.NRSV: So David reigned over all Israel; and he administered justice and equity to all his people.
text.Rotherham: So David reigned over all Israel,—and he was executing justice and righteousness for all his people;
text.KJV: So David reigned over all Israel, and executed judgment and justice among all his people.
text.NABRE: David’s Officials. David was king over all Israel; he dispensed justice and right to all his people.
text.DRB: David therefore reigned over all Israel, & did judgement and justice to all his people.

text.NRSV: Joab son of Zeruiah was over the army; Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was recorder;
text.Rotherham: and, Joab, son of Zeruiah, was over the army,—and, Jehoshaphat, son of Elihud, was recorder;
text.KJV: And Joab the son of Zeruiah was over the host; and Jehoshaphat the son of Ahilud, recorder.
text.NABRE: Joab, son of Zeruiah, was in command of the army; Jehoshaphat, son of Ahilud, was chancellor;
text.DRB: Moreover Joab the son of Sarvia was over the army, and Josaphat the son of Ahilud commenter.

text.NRSV: Zadok son of Ahitub and Ahimelech son of Abiathar were priests; Shavsha was secretary;
text.Rotherham: and, Zadok, son of Ahitub, and Abimelech son of Abiathar, were priests,—and, Shavsha, was scribe;
text.KJV: And Zadok the son of Ahitub, and Abimelech the son of Abiathar, were the priests; and Shavsha was scribe;
text.NABRE: Zadok, son of Ahitub, and Ahimelech, son of Abiathar, were priests; Shavsha was scribe;
text.DRB: And Sadoc the son of Achitob, and Ahimelech the son of Abiathar, Priests: and Susa, Scribe.

text.NRSV: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and the Pelethites; and David’s sons were the chief officials in the service of the king.
text.Rotherham: and, Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, was over the Cherethites, and the Pelethites,—and, the sons of David, were first, in attendance on the king.
text.KJV: And Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and the Pelethites; and the sons of David were chief about the king.
text.NABRE: Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, was in command of the Cherethites and the Pelethites; and David’s sons were the chief assistants to the king.
text.DRB: Banaias also the son of Joiada over the legions Cerethi, and Phelethi: moreover the sons of David the chief at the king's hand.

text.NRSV: Some time afterward, King Nahash of the Ammonites died, and his son succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after this, that Nahash king of the sons of Ammon died,—and Hanun his son reigned in his stead.
text.KJV: Now it came to pass after this, that Nahash the king of the children of Ammon died, and his son reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: Chapter 19 Campaigns Against Ammon. Afterward Nahash, king of the Ammonites, died and his son succeeded him as king.
text.DRB: And it chanced that Naas the king of the children of Ammon died, and his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: David said, “I will deal loyally with Hanun son of Nahash, for his father dealt loyally with me.” So David sent messengers to console him concerning his father. When David’s servants came to Hanun in the land of the Ammonites, to console him,
text.Rotherham: Then said David—I will show lovingkindness unto Hanun, son of Nahash,—for his father shewed unto me lovingkindness. So David sent messengers to console him concerning his father,—and the servants of David came into the land of the sons of Ammon unto Hanun, to console him.
text.KJV: And David said, I will shew kindness unto Hanun the son of Nahash, because his father shewed kindness to me. And David sent messengers to comfort him concerning his father. So the servants of David came into the land of the children of Ammon to Hanun, to comfort him.
text.NABRE: David said, “I will show kindness to Hanun, the son of Nahash, for his father showed kindness to me.” Therefore he sent envoys to console him over his father. But when David’s servants had entered the land of the Ammonites to console Hanun,
text.DRB: And David said: I will do mercy with Hanon the son of Naas: for his father hath done me pleasure. And David sent messengers to comfort him upon the death of his father. Who when they were come into the land of the children of Ammon, to comfort Hanon,

text.NRSV: the officials of the Ammonites said to Hanun, “Do you imagine, because David has sent consolers to you, that he is honoring your father? Have not his servants come to you to search and to overthrow and to spy out the land?”
text.Rotherham: Then said the rulers of the sons of Ammon unto Hanun, Doth David honour thy father in thine eyes, that he hath sent to thee comforters? Is it not, for the sake of exploring and overthrowing, and spying out the land, that his servants have come to thee?
text.KJV: But the princes of the children of Ammon said to Hanun, Thinkest thou that David doth honour thy father, that he hath sent comforters unto thee? are not his servants come unto thee for to search, and to overthrow, and to spy out the land?
text.NABRE: the Ammonite princes said to Hanun, “Do you think David is doing this—sending you these consolers—to honor your father? Have not his servants rather come to you to explore the land, spying it out for its overthrow?”
text.DRB: the Princes of the children of Ammon said to Hanon: Thou thinkest perhaps, that David for honour sake toward thy father hath sent some that should comfort thee; neither markest thou, that his servants are come to thee to espy, and seek out, and search thy land.

text.NRSV: So Hanun seized David’s servants, shaved them, cut off their garments in the middle at their hips, and sent them away;
text.Rotherham: So Hanun took the servants of David, and shaved them, and cut off their upper garments in the middle, as far as their parts of shame,—and let them go.
text.KJV: Wherefore Hanun took David’s servants, and shaved them, and cut off their garments in the midst hard by their buttocks, and sent them away.
text.NABRE: So Hanun seized David’s servants and had them shaved and their garments cut off halfway at the hips. Then he sent them away.
text.DRB: Therefore Hanon made the servants of David bald, and shaved them, and cut away their coats from the buttocks to the feet, and sent them away.

text.NRSV: and they departed. When David was told about the men, he sent messengers to them, for they were greatly ashamed. The king said, “Stay at Jericho until your beards have grown, and then return.”
text.Rotherham: And people went and told David, concerning the men, so he sent to meet them, because the men were greatly ashamed,—and the king said—Tarry at Jericho, until your beards be grown, and then return.
text.KJV: Then there went certain, and told David how the men were served. And he sent to meet them: for the men were greatly ashamed. And the king said, Tarry at Jericho until your beards be grown, and then return.
text.NABRE: David was told about the men, and he sent word for them to be intercepted, for the men had been greatly disgraced. “Remain at Jericho,” the king told them, “until your beards have grown again; then come back here.”
text.DRB: Who when they were gone, and had sent word to David, he sent to meet them (for they had sustained great reproach) and commanded them to tarry in Jericho, till their beard grew, and then they should return.

text.NRSV: When the Ammonites saw that they had made themselves odious to David, Hanun and the Ammonites sent a thousand talents of silver to hire chariots and cavalry from Mesopotamia, from Aram-maacah and from Zobah.
text.Rotherham: And, when the sons of Ammon saw, that they had made themselves odious with David, Hanun and the sons of Ammon sent a thousand talents of silver, to hire for themselves—out of Mesopotamia, and out of Aram-maacah, and out of Zobah,—chariots, and horsemen.
text.KJV: And when the children of Ammon saw that they had made themselves odious to David, Hanun and the children of Ammon sent a thousand talents of silver to hire them chariots and horsemen out of Mesopotamia, and out of Syria–maachah, and out of Zobah.
text.NABRE: When the Ammonites realized that they had put themselves in bad odor with David, Hanun and the Ammonites sent a thousand talents of silver to hire chariots and horsemen from Aram Naharaim, from Aram-maacah, and from Zobah.
text.DRB: And the children of Ammon seeing, that they had done injury to David, as well Hanon as the rest of the people, they sent a thousand talents of silver, to hire them chariots and horsemen out of Mesopotamia, and from Syria Maacha, and from Soba.

text.NRSV: They hired thirty-two thousand chariots and the king of Maacah with his army, who came and encamped before Medeba. And the Ammonites were mustered from their cities and came to battle.
text.Rotherham: So they hired for themselves thirty-two thousand chariots, and the king of Maacah, and his people, who came and pitched before Medeba,—and the sons of Ammon gathered themselves together out of their cities, and entered into the war.
text.KJV: So they hired thirty and two thousand chariots, and the king of Maachah and his people; who came and pitched before Medeba. And the children of Ammon gathered themselves together from their cities, and came to battle.
text.NABRE: They hired thirty-two thousand chariots along with the king of Maacah and his army, who came and encamped before Medeba. The Ammonites also assembled from their cities and came out for war.
text.DRB: And they hired two and thirty thousand chariots, & king Maacha with his people. Who when they were come, camped over against Medaba. The children of Ammon also being gathered together out of their cities, came to the battle.

text.NRSV: When David heard of it, he sent Joab and all the army of the warriors.
text.Rotherham: Now, when David heard, he sent Joab and all the army of heroes.
text.KJV: And when David heard of it, he sent Joab, and all the host of the mighty men.
text.NABRE: When David heard of this, he sent Joab and his whole army of warriors against them.
text.DRB: Which when David had heard, he sent Joab, and all the host of valiant men:

text.NRSV: The Ammonites came out and drew up in battle array at the entrance of the city, and the kings who had come were by themselves in the open country.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Ammon came out, and set in array for the battle, at the opening of the city,—whereas, the kings who had come, were by themselves, in the field.
text.KJV: And the children of Ammon came out, and put the battle in array before the gate of the city: and the kings that were come were by themselves in the field.
text.NABRE: The Ammonites marched out and lined up for battle at the entrance of the city, while the kings who had come to their help remained apart in the open field.
text.DRB: and the children of Ammon issuing forth, put their army in array beside the gate of the city: and the kings, that were come to aid him, stood apart in the field.

text.NRSV: When Joab saw that the line of battle was set against him both in front and in the rear, he chose some of the picked men of Israel and arrayed them against the Arameans;
text.Rotherham: So, when Joab saw that the battle confronted him, before and behind,—he chose out of all the choice in Israel, and set in array to meet the Syrians;
text.KJV: Now when Joab saw that the battle was set against him before and behind, he chose out of all the choice of Israel, and put them in array against the Syrians.
text.NABRE: When Joab saw that there was a battle line both in front of and behind him, he chose some of the best fighters among the Israelites and lined them up against the Arameans;
text.DRB: Joab therefore understanding that battle was made against him before and behind, chose the most valiant men of all Israel, and marched on against the Syrian.

text.NRSV: the rest of his troops he put in the charge of his brother Abishai, and they were arrayed against the Ammonites.
text.Rotherham: and, the rest of the people, delivered he into the hand of Abishai his brother,—and they set in array, to meet the sons of Ammon.
text.KJV: And the rest of the people he delivered unto the hand of Abishai his brother, and they set themselves in array against the children of Ammon.
text.NABRE: the rest of the army, which he placed under the command of his brother Abishai, then lined up to oppose the Ammonites.
text.DRB: And the rest of the people he gave under the hand of Abisai his brother: and they went forth against the children of Ammon.

text.NRSV: He said, “If the Arameans are too strong for me, then you shall help me; but if the Ammonites are too strong for you, then I will help you.
text.Rotherham: And he said—If, too strong for me, be the Syrians, then shalt thou come to my help,—but, if, the sons of Ammon, be too strong for thee, then will I help thee.
text.KJV: And he said, If the Syrians be too strong for me, then thou shalt help me: but if the children of Ammon be too strong for thee, then I will help thee.
text.NABRE: And he said: “If the Arameans prove too strong for me, you must come and save me; and if the Ammonites prove too strong for you, I will save you.
text.DRB: And he said: If the Syrian shall overcome me, thou shalt aid me: and if the children of Ammon shall overcome thee, I will aid thee.

text.NRSV: Be brave, and let us fight bravely for our people and for the cities of our God; and may the Lord do what seems good to him.”
text.Rotherham: Be strong, and let us put forth our strength, for the sake of our people, and for the sake of the cities of our God,—and let, Yahweh, do, what is good in his own eyes.
text.KJV: Be of good courage, and let us behave ourselves valiantly for our people, and for the cities of our God: and let the Lord do that which is good in his sight.
text.NABRE: Hold firm and let us show ourselves courageous for the sake of our people and the cities of our God; and may the Lord do what is good in his sight.”
text.DRB: Take courage, and let us play the men for our people, and for the cities of our God: and our Lord will do that which is good in his sight.

text.NRSV: So Joab and the troops who were with him advanced toward the Arameans for battle; and they fled before him.
text.Rotherham: Then did Joab, and all the people who were with him, draw near before the Syrians unto the battle,—and they fled from before him.
text.KJV: So Joab and the people that were with him drew nigh before the Syrians unto the battle; and they fled before him.
text.NABRE: Joab therefore advanced with his men to engage the Arameans in battle; but they fled before him.
text.DRB: Joab therefore marched on, and the people that were with him, against the Syrian to battle: and he put them to flight.

text.NRSV: When the Ammonites saw that the Arameans fled, they likewise fled before Abishai, Joab’s brother, and entered the city. Then Joab came to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, when, the sons of Ammon, saw that the Syrians had fled, then fled, they also, from before Abishai his brother, and entered the city,—and Joab came to Jerusalem.
text.KJV: And when the children of Ammon saw that the Syrians were fled, they likewise fled before Abishai his brother, and entered into the city. Then Joab came to Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: And when the Ammonites saw that the Arameans had fled, they too fled before his brother Abishai, and entered their city. Joab then came to Jerusalem.
text.DRB: Moreover the children of Ammon seeing that the Syrian was fled, themselves also fled from Abisai his brother, and went into the city: and Joab also returned into Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: But when the Arameans saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they sent messengers and brought out the Arameans who were beyond the Euphrates, with Shophach the commander of the army of Hadadezer at their head.
text.Rotherham: And when the Syrians saw that they had been defeated before Israel, then sent they messengers, and brought out the Syrians who were Beyond the River,—with Shophach captain of the army of Hadarezer, before them.
text.KJV: And when the Syrians saw that they were put to the worse before Israel, they sent messengers, and drew forth the Syrians that were beyond the river: and Shophach the captain of the host of Hadarezer went before them.
text.NABRE: Seeing themselves vanquished by Israel, the Arameans sent messengers to bring out the Arameans from beyond the Euphrates, with Shophach, the commander of Hadadezer’s army, at their head.
text.DRB: But the Syrian seeing that he was fallen before Israel, sent messengers, and brought the Syrian, that was beyond the river: and Sophach the General of Adarezer's war, was their captain.

text.NRSV: When David was informed, he gathered all Israel together, crossed the Jordan, came to them, and drew up his forces against them. When David set the battle in array against the Arameans, they fought with him.
text.Rotherham: And it was told David, and he gathered together all Israel, and crossed the Jordan, and came unto them, and set in array against them,—and, when David had set in array against the Syrians for battle, they fought with him.
text.KJV: And it was told David; and he gathered all Israel, and passed over Jordan, and came upon them, and set the battle in array against them. So when David had put the battle in array against the Syrians, they fought with him.
text.NABRE: When this was reported to David, he gathered all Israel together, crossed the Jordan, and met them. With the army of David drawn up to fight the Arameans, they gave battle.
text.DRB: Which when it was told David, he gathered together all Israel, and passed Jordan, and fell upon them, & directed his army against him, they fighting on the contrary part.

text.NRSV: The Arameans fled before Israel; and David killed of the Arameans seven thousand chariot-drivers and forty thousand foot soldiers, and also killed Shophach the commander of their army.
text.Rotherham: And the Syrians fled from before Israel, and David slew of the Syrians [the men of] seven thousand chariots, and forty thousand footmen,—and, Shophach captain of the army, he put to death.
text.KJV: But the Syrians fled before Israel; and David slew of the Syrians seven thousand men which fought in chariots, and forty thousand footmen, and killed Shophach the captain of the host.
text.NABRE: But the Arameans fled before Israel, and David killed seven thousand of their chariot fighters and forty thousand of their foot soldiers; he also put to death Shophach, the commander of the army.
text.DRB: And the Syrian fled from Israel: and David slew of the Syrians seven thousand chariots, and forty thousand footmen, and Sophach General of the army.

text.NRSV: When the servants of Hadadezer saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they made peace with David, and became subject to him. So the Arameans were not willing to help the Ammonites any more.
text.Rotherham: And, when the servants of Hadarezer saw that they were defeated before Israel, they made peace with David and served him,—neither would the Syrians help the sons of Ammon any more.
text.KJV: And when the servants of Hadarezer saw that they were put to the worse before Israel, they made peace with David, and became his servants: neither would the Syrians help the children of Ammon any more.
text.NABRE: When the vassals of Hadadezer saw themselves vanquished by Israel, they made peace with David and became his subjects. After this, the Arameans refused to come to the aid of the Ammonites.
text.DRB: And the servants of Adarezer seeing themselves to be overcome of Israel, fled to David, and served him: and Syria would no more give aid to the children of Ammon.

text.NRSV: In the spring of the year, the time when kings go out to battle, Joab led out the army, ravaged the country of the Ammonites, and came and besieged Rabbah. But David remained at Jerusalem. Joab struck Rabbah, and overthrew it.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, at the time of the turn of the year, at the time of the going forth of kings, that Joab led forth the force of the army, and laid waste the land of the sons of Ammon, and came and besieged Rabbah, but, David, was tarrying in Jerusalem,—and Joab smote Rabbah, and overthrew it.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, that after the year was expired, at the time that kings go out to battle, Joab led forth the power of the army, and wasted the country of the children of Ammon, and came and besieged Rabbah. But David tarried at Jerusalem. And Joab smote Rabbah, and destroyed it.
text.NABRE: Chapter 20 At the turn of the year, the time when kings go to war, Joab led the army out in force, laid waste the land of the Ammonites, and went on to besiege Rabbah; David himself remained in Jerusalem. When Joab had attacked Rabbah and destroyed it,
