text.KJV: And the rest of the sons of Levi were these: Of the sons of Amram; Shubael: of the sons of Shubael; Jehdeiah.
text.NABRE: Other Levites. Of the remaining Levites, there were Shubael, of the sons of Amram, and Jehdeiah, of the sons of Shubael;
text.DRB: Moreover of the children of Levi, which were remaining, there was Subael of the children of Amram: and of the children of Subael, Jehedeia.

text.NRSV: Of Rehabiah: of the sons of Rehabiah, Isshiah the chief.
text.Rotherham: Of Rehabiah, of the sons of Rehabiah, the chief Isshiah;
text.KJV: Concerning Rehabiah: of the sons of Rehabiah, the first was Isshiah.
text.NABRE: Isshiah, the chief, of the sons of Rehabiah;
text.DRB: Also of the children of Rohobia the Prince of Jesias.

text.NRSV: Of the Izharites, Shelomoth; of the sons of Shelomoth, Jahath.
text.Rotherham: of the Izharites, Shelomoth,—of the sons of Shelomoth, Jahath;
text.KJV: Of the Izharites; Shelomoth: of the sons of Shelomoth; Jahath.
text.NABRE: Shelomith of the Izharites, and Jahath of the sons of Shelomith.
text.DRB: And the son of Isaari Salemoth, & the son of Salemoth Jahath:

text.NRSV: The sons of Hebron: Jeriah the chief, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, Jekameam the fourth.
text.Rotherham: and, the sons [of Hebron], Jeriah,—Amariah, the second, Jehaziel, the third, Jekameam, the fourth;
text.KJV: And the sons of Hebron; Jeriah the first, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, Jekameam the fourth.
text.NABRE: The sons of Hebron were Jeriah, the chief, Amariah, the second, Jahaziel, the third, Jekameam, the fourth.
text.DRB: and his son Jeriau the first, Amarias the second, Jahaziel the third, Jecmaan the fourth.

text.NRSV: The son of Uzziel, Micah; of the sons of Micah, Shamir.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Uzziel, Micah, of the sons of Micah, Shamir;
text.KJV: Of the sons of Uzziel; Michah: of the sons of Michah; Shamir.
text.NABRE: The sons of Uzziel were Micah; Shamir, of the sons of Micah;
text.DRB: The son of Oziel, Micha: the son of Micha, Samir.

text.NRSV: The brother of Micah, Isshiah; of the sons of Isshiah, Zechariah.
text.Rotherham: the brother of Micah, Isshiah, of the sons of Isshiah, Zechariah.
text.KJV: The brother of Michah was Isshiah: of the sons of Isshiah; Zechariah.
text.NABRE: Isshiah, the brother of Micah; and Zechariah, a descendant of Isshiah.
text.DRB: The brother of Micha, Jesia: and the son of Jesia, Zacharias.

text.NRSV: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. The son of Jaaziah: Beno.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Merari, Mahli and Mushi,—the sons of Jaaziah, Beno;
text.KJV: The sons of Merari were Mahli and Mushi: the sons of Jaaziah; Beno.
text.NABRE: The sons of Merari were Mahli, Mushi, and the sons of his son Uzziah.
text.DRB: The sons of Merari: Moholi and Musi. The son of Oziau: Benno.

text.NRSV: The sons of Merari: of Jaaziah, Beno, Shoham, Zaccur, and Ibri.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Merari, of Jaaziah, Beno, and Shoham and Zaccur and Ibri:
text.KJV: The sons of Merari by Jaaziah; Beno, and Shoham, and Zaccur, and Ibri.
text.NABRE: The sons of Merari through his son Uzziah: Shoham, Zaccur, and Ibri.
text.DRB: The son also of Merari: Oziau, and Soam, and Zacchur, and Hebri.

text.NRSV: Of Mahli: Eleazar, who had no sons.
text.Rotherham: of Mahli, Eleazar, who had no sons;
text.KJV: Of Mahli came Eleazar, who had no sons.
text.NABRE: The sons of Mahli were Eleazar, who had no sons,
text.DRB: Moreover the son of Moholi: Eleazar, who had no children.

text.NRSV: Of Kish, the son of Kish: Jerahmeel.
text.Rotherham: of Kish—the sons of Kish, Jerameel;
text.KJV: Concerning Kish: the son of Kish was Jerahmeel.
text.NABRE: and Jerahmeel, of the sons of Kish.
text.DRB: And the son of Cis, Jeramael.

text.NRSV: The sons of Mushi: Mahli, Eder, and Jerimoth. These were the sons of the Levites according to their ancestral houses.
text.Rotherham: and, the sons of Mushi, Mahli and Eder and Jerimoth. These, were the sons of the Levites, belonging to their ancestral house.
text.KJV: The sons also of Mushi; Mahli, and Eder, and Jerimoth. These were the sons of the Levites after the house of their fathers.
text.NABRE: The sons of Mushi were Mahli, Eder, and Jerimoth. These were the sons of the Levites according to their ancestral houses.
text.DRB: The sons of Musi: Moholi, Eder, and Jerimoth. These be the sons of Levi according to the houses of their families.

text.NRSV: These also cast lots just as their kindred the sons of Aaron did in the presence of King David, Zadok, Ahimelech, and the heads of ancestral houses of the priests and of the Levites, the chief as well as the youngest brother, casting lots alike.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, they also, cast lots along with their brethren the sons of Aaron, before David the king and Zadok and Ahimelech, and the ancestral chiefs, pertaining to the priests and to the Levites, the ancestral chief along with his younger brethren.
text.KJV: These likewise cast lots over against their brethren the sons of Aaron in the presence of David the king, and Zadok, and Ahimelech, and the chief of the fathers of the priests and Levites, even the principal fathers over against their younger brethren.
text.NABRE: They too, in the same manner as their kinsmen, the sons of Aaron, cast lots in the presence of King David, Zadok, Ahimelech, and the heads of the priestly and levitical families; the more important family did so in the same way as the less important one.
text.DRB: And they also did cast lots against their brethren the sons of Aaron before David the king, and Sadoc, and Ahimelech, and the Princes of the Priestly and Levitical families, as well the elder as the younger. Lot divided all equally.

text.NRSV: David and the officers of the army also set apart for the service the sons of Asaph, and of Heman, and of Jeduthun, who should prophesy with lyres, harps, and cymbals. The list of those who did the work and of their duties was:
text.Rotherham: And David and the captains of the host set apart for service, unto the sons of Asaph and Heman and Jeduthun, such as should prophesy, with lyres, with harps, and with cymbals,—and, the number of the workers, for their service, was:
text.KJV: Moreover David and the captains of the host separated to the service of the sons of Asaph, and of Heman, and of Jeduthun, who should prophesy with harps, with psalteries, and with cymbals: and the number of the workmen according to their service was:
text.NABRE: Chapter 25 The Singers. David and the leaders of the liturgy set apart for the service the sons of Asaph, Heman, and Jeduthun, who prophesied to the accompaniment of lyres and harps and cymbals. This is the list of those who performed this service:
text.DRB: Therefore David, and the officers of the host separated for the ministry the sons of Asaph, and Heman, and Idithun: which should prophesy on harps, and psalteries, and cymbals, according to their number serving the office dedicated to them.

text.NRSV: Of the sons of Asaph: Zaccur, Joseph, Nethaniah, and Asarelah, sons of Asaph, under the direction of Asaph, who prophesied under the direction of the king.
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Asaph, Zaccur and Joseph and Nethaniah and Asarelah, the sons of Asaph,—under the direction of Asaph, who prophesied under the direction of the king.
text.KJV: Of the sons of Asaph; Zaccur, and Joseph, and Nethaniah, and Asarelah, the sons of Asaph under the hands of Asaph, which prophesied according to the order of the king.
text.NABRE: Of the sons of Asaph: Zaccur, Joseph, Nethaniah, and Asharelah, sons of Asaph, under the direction of Asaph, who prophesied under the guidance of the king.
text.DRB: Of the sons of Asaph: Zacchur, and Joseph, and Nathania, and Asarela, the sons of Asaph: under the hand of Asaph prophesying near the king.

text.NRSV: Of Jeduthun, the sons of Jeduthun: Gedaliah, Zeri, Jeshaiah, Shimei, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah, six, under the direction of their father Jeduthun, who prophesied with the lyre in thanksgiving and praise to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Of Jeduthun, the sons of Jeduthun,—Gedaliah, and Zeri, and Jeshaiah, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah—six, under the direction of their father Jeduthun, with the lyre, as he prophesied, in giving thanks and offering praise unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: Of Jeduthun: the sons of Jeduthun; Gedaliah, and Zeri, and Jeshaiah, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah, six, under the hands of their father Jeduthun, who prophesied with a harp, to give thanks and to praise the Lord.
text.NABRE: Of Jeduthun, these sons of Jeduthun: Gedaliah, Zeri, Jeshaiah, Shimei, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah; six, under the direction of their father Jeduthun, who prophesied to the accompaniment of a lyre, to give thanks and praise to the Lord.
text.DRB: Moreover Idithun: the sons of Idithun, Godolias, Sori, Jeseias, and Hasabias, and Mathathias, six, under the hand of their father Idithun, who prophesied on harp over them that confessed and praised our Lord.

text.NRSV: Of Heman, the sons of Heman: Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel, and Jerimoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, and Romamti-ezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, Mahazioth.
text.Rotherham: Of Heman, the sons of Heman—Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel, and Jeremoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, and Romamti-ezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, Mahazioth.
text.KJV: Of Heman: the sons of Heman; Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel, and Jerimoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, and Romamti–ezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, and Mahazioth:
text.NABRE: Of Heman, these sons of Heman: Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shubael, and Jerimoth; Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, Romamti-ezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, and Mahazioth.
text.DRB: Of Heman also: the sons of Heman Bocciau, Mathaniau, Oziel, Subuel, and Jerimoth, Hananias, Hanani, Eliatha, Geddelthi, and Romemthiezer, & Jesbacassa, Mellothi, Othir, Mahazioth:

text.NRSV: All these were the sons of Heman the king’s seer, according to the promise of God to exalt him; for God had given Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.
text.Rotherham: All these, were sons of Heman, the seer of the king in the things of God, at the lifting of the horn. And God gave to Heman, fourteen sons and three daughters,
text.KJV: All these were the sons of Heman the king’s seer in the words of God, to lift up the horn. And God gave to Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.
text.NABRE: All these were the sons of Heman, the king’s seer for divine matters; to exalt him God gave Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.
text.DRB: all these the sons of Heman the Seer of the king in the words of God, that he might exalt the horn: & God gave to Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.

text.NRSV: They were all under the direction of their father for the music in the house of the Lord with cymbals, harps, and lyres for the service of the house of God. Asaph, Jeduthun, and Heman were under the order of the king.
text.Rotherham: All these, were under the direction of their father, in the singing of the house of Yahweh, with cymbals, harps and lyres, for the service of the house of God,—Asaph and Jeduthun and Heman, under the direction of the king.
text.KJV: All these were under the hands of their father for song in the house of the Lord, with cymbals, psalteries, and harps, for the service of the house of God, according to the king’s order to Asaph, Jeduthun, and Heman.
text.NABRE: All these, whether of Asaph, Jeduthun, or Heman, were under their fathers’ direction in the singing in the house of the Lord to the accompaniment of cymbals, harps and lyres, serving in the house of God, under the guidance of the king.
text.DRB: All under their fathers were distributed to sing in the temple of our Lord, on cymbals, & psalteries, and harps, for the ministries of the house of our Lord near the king: to wit, Asaph, and Idithun, and Heman.

text.NRSV: The number of them along with their kindred, who were trained in singing to the Lord, all of whom were skillful, was two hundred eighty-eight.
text.Rotherham: And, the number of them—with their brethren, who were instructed in singing unto Yahweh,—even all the skilful, was two hundred and eighty-eight.
text.KJV: So the number of them, with their brethren that were instructed in the songs of the Lord, even all that were cunning, was two hundred fourscore and eight.
text.NABRE: Their number, together with that of their kinsmen who were trained in singing to the Lord, all of them skilled men, was two hundred and eighty-eight.
text.DRB: And the number of them with their brethren, that taught the song of our Lord, all the teachers two hundred eighty eight.

text.NRSV: And they cast lots for their duties, small and great, teacher and pupil alike.
text.Rotherham: So they cast lots for their charges, all alike, as the small so the great, the teacher with the learner.
text.KJV: And they cast lots, ward against ward, as well the small as the great, the teacher as the scholar.
text.NABRE: They cast lots for their functions equally, young and old, master and pupil alike.
text.DRB: And they did cast lots by their courses, equally as well the elder as the younger, the learned and the unlearned together.

text.NRSV: The first lot fell for Asaph to Joseph; the second to Gedaliah, to him and his kindred and his sons, twelve;
text.Rotherham: And the first lot came forth for Asaph, to Joseph,—Gedaliah, the second, he and his brethren and his sons, twelve.
text.KJV: Now the first lot came forth for Asaph to Joseph: the second to Gedaliah, who with his brethren and sons were twelve:
text.NABRE: The first lot fell to Asaph, to the family of Joseph; he and his sons and his kinsmen were twelve. Gedaliah was the second; he and his kinsmen and his sons were twelve.
text.DRB: And the first lot came forth to Joseph, which was of Asaph. The second to Godolias, to him and his sons, and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the third to Zaccur, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The third, Zaccur, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The third to Zaccur, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The third was Zaccur, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The third to Zachur, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the fourth to Izri, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The fourth, to Izri, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The fourth to Izri, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The fourth fell to Izri, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The fourth to Isari, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the fifth to Nethaniah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The fifth, Nethaniah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The fifth to Nethaniah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The fifth was Nethaniah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The fifth to Nathanias, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the sixth to Bukkiah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The sixth, Bukkiah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The sixth to Bukkiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The sixth was Bukkiah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The sixth to Bocciau, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the seventh to Jesarelah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The seventh, Jesharelah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The seventh to Jesharelah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The seventh was Jesarelah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The seventh to Isreela, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the eighth to Jeshaiah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The eighth, Jeshaiah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The eighth to Jeshaiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The eighth was Jeshaiah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The eighth to Jesaia, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the ninth to Mattaniah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The ninth, Mattaniah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The ninth to Mattaniah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The ninth was Mattaniah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The ninth to Mathanias, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the tenth to Shimei, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The tenth, Shimei, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The tenth to Shimei, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The tenth was Shimei, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The tenth to Semeias, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the eleventh to Azarel, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The eleventh, Azarel, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The eleventh to Azareel, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The eleventh was Uzziel, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The eleventh to Azareel, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the twelfth to Hashabiah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The twelfth, to Hashabiah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The twelfth to Hashabiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The twelfth fell to Hashabiah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The twelfth to Hasabia, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the thirteenth to Shubael, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the thirteenth, Shubael, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The thirteenth to Shubael, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The thirteenth was Shubael, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The thirteenth to Subael, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the fourteenth to Mattithiah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the fourteenth, Mattithiah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The fourteenth to Mattithiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The fourteenth was Mattithiah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The fourteenth to Mathathias, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the fifteenth to Jeremoth, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the fifteenth, to Jeremoth, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The fifteenth to Jeremoth, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The fifteenth fell to Jeremoth, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The fifteenth to Jerimoth, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the sixteenth to Hananiah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the sixteenth, to Hananiah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The sixteenth to Hananiah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The sixteenth fell to Hananiah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The sixteenth to Hananias, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the seventeenth to Joshbekashah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the seventeenth, to Joshbekashah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The seventeenth to Joshbekashah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The seventeenth fell to Joshbekashah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The seventeenth to Jesbacassa, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the eighteenth to Hanani, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the eighteenth, to Hanani, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The eighteenth to Hanani, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The eighteenth fell to Hanani, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The eighteenth to Hanani, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the nineteenth to Mallothi, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the nineteenth, to Mallothi, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The nineteenth to Mallothi, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The nineteenth fell to Mallothi, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The nineteenth to Mellothi, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the twentieth to Eliathah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the twentieth, to Eliathah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The twentieth to Eliathah, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The twentieth fell to Eliathah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The twentieth to Eliatha, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the twenty-first to Hothir, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the twenty-first, to Hothir, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The one and twentieth to Hothir, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The twenty-first fell to Hothir, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The one and twentieth to Othir, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the twenty-second to Giddalti, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the twenty-second, to Giddalti, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The two and twentieth to Giddalti, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The twenty-second fell to Giddalti, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The two & twentieth to Geddelthi, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the twenty-third to Mahazioth, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the twenty-third, to Mahazioth, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The three and twentieth to Mahazioth, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve:
text.NABRE: The twenty-third fell to Mahazioth, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The three and twentieth to Mahazioth, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: the twenty-fourth to Romamti-ezer, his sons and his kindred, twelve.
text.Rotherham: As the twenty-fourth, to Romamti-ezer, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: The four and twentieth to Romamti–ezer, he, his sons, and his brethren, were twelve.
text.NABRE: The twenty-fourth fell to Romamti-ezer, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.
text.DRB: The four and twentieth to Romemthiezer, to his sons and his brethren twelve.

text.NRSV: As for the divisions of the gatekeepers: of the Korahites, Meshelemiah son of Kore, of the sons of Asaph.
text.Rotherham: To the courses of the door-keepers, pertained,—of the Korahites, Meshelemiah son of Kore, of the sons of Asaph;
text.KJV: Concerning the divisions of the porters: Of the Korhites was Meshelemiah the son of Kore, of the sons of Asaph.
text.NABRE: Chapter 26 Divisions of Gatekeepers. As for the divisions of gatekeepers: Of the Korahites was Meshelemiah, the son of Kore, one of the sons of Abiasaph.
text.DRB: And the divisions of the porters: of the Corites Meselemia, the son of Core, of the sons of Asaph.

text.NRSV: Meshelemiah had sons: Zechariah the firstborn, Jediael the second, Zebadiah the third, Jathniel the fourth,
text.Rotherham: and, Meshelemiah, had sons,—Zechariah the firstborn, Jediael the second, Zebadiah the third, Jathniel the fourth;
text.KJV: And the sons of Meshelemiah were, Zechariah the firstborn, Jediael the second, Zebadiah the third, Jathniel the fourth,
text.NABRE: Meshelemiah’s sons: Zechariah, the firstborn, Jediael, the second son, Zebadiah, the third, Jathniel, the fourth,
text.DRB: The sons of Meselemia: Zacharias the first-begotten, Jadihel the second, Zabadias the third, Jathanael the fourth,

text.NRSV: Elam the fifth, Jehohanan the sixth, Eliehoenai the seventh.
text.Rotherham: Elam the fifth, Jehohanan the sixth, Eliehoenai the seventh.
text.KJV: Elam the fifth, Jehohanan the sixth, Elioenai the seventh.
text.NABRE: Elam, the fifth, Jehohanan, the sixth, Eliehoenai, the seventh.
text.DRB: Ælam the fifth, Johanan the sixth, Elioenai the seventh.

text.NRSV: Obed-edom had sons: Shemaiah the firstborn, Jehozabad the second, Joah the third, Sachar the fourth, Nethanel the fifth,
text.Rotherham: And, Obed-edom, had sons,—Shemaiah the firstborn, Jehozabad the second, Josh the third, and Sacar the fourth, and Nethanel the fifth;
text.KJV: Moreover the sons of Obed–edom were, Shemaiah the firstborn, Jehozabad the second, Joah the third, and Sacar the fourth, and Nethaneel the fifth,
text.NABRE: Obed-edom’s sons: Shemaiah, the firstborn, Jehozabad, a second son, Joah, the third, Sachar, the fourth, Nethanel, the fifth,
text.DRB: And the sons of Obededom Semeias the first-begotten, Jozabad the second, Joaha the third, Sachar the fourth, Nathanael the fifth,

text.NRSV: Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seventh, Peullethai the eighth; for God blessed him.
text.Rotherham: Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seventh, Peullethai, the eighth,—for God had blessed him.
text.KJV: Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seventh, Peulthai the eighth: for God blessed him.
text.NABRE: Ammiel, the sixth, Issachar, the seventh, Peullethai, the eighth, for God blessed him.
text.DRB: Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seventh, Phollathi the eight: because our Lord blessed him.

text.NRSV: Also to his son Shemaiah were sons born who exercised authority in their ancestral houses, for they were men of great ability.
text.Rotherham: And, to Shemaiah his son, were born sons, who bare rule to their ancestral house,—for, heroes of valour, were they.
text.KJV: Also unto Shemaiah his son were sons born, that ruled throughout the house of their father: for they were mighty men of valour.
text.NABRE: To his son Shemaiah were born sons who ruled over their family, for they were warriors.
text.DRB: And to Semei his son were born sons, the chief of their families: for they were most valiant men.

text.NRSV: The sons of Shemaiah: Othni, Rephael, Obed, and Elzabad, whose brothers were able men, Elihu and Semachiah.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Shemaiah, were Othni and Rephael, and Obed, Elzabad, whose brethren were sons of valour,—Elihu, and Semachiah.
text.KJV: The sons of Shemaiah; Othni, and Rephael, and Obed, Elzabad, whose brethren were strong men, Elihu, and Semachiah.
text.NABRE: The sons of Shemaiah were Othni, Rephael, Obed, and Elzabad; also his kinsmen who were men of substance, Elihu and Semachiah.
text.DRB: The sons then of Semeias: Othni, and Raphael, and Obed, Elizabad, and his brethren most valiant men: Eliu also, and Samachias.

text.NRSV: All these were of the sons of Obed-edom with their sons and kindred, able men qualified for the service; sixty-two of Obed-edom.
text.Rotherham: All these, were of the sons of Obed-edom, they and their sons and their brethren, able men in strength for the service,—sixty-two, pertaining to Obed-edom.
text.KJV: All these of the sons of Obed–edom: they and their sons and their brethren, able men for strength for the service, were threescore and two of Obed–edom.
text.NABRE: All these were the sons of Obed-edom, who, together with their sons and their kinsmen, were men of substance, fit for the service. Of Obed-edom, sixty-two.
text.DRB: All these of the sons of Obededom: they, & their sons, & their brethren most able to minister, sixty two of Obededom.

text.NRSV: Meshelemiah had sons and kindred, able men, eighteen.
text.Rotherham: And, Meshelemiah, had sons, and brethren, sons of valour, eighteen.
text.KJV: And Meshelemiah had sons and brethren, strong men, eighteen.
text.NABRE: Of Meshelemiah, eighteen sons and kinsmen, men of substance.
text.DRB: Moreover the sons of Meselemia, and their brethren most strong, eighteen.

text.NRSV: Hosah, of the sons of Merari, had sons: Shimri the chief (for though he was not the firstborn, his father made him chief),
text.Rotherham: And, Hosah of the sons of Merari, had sons,—Shimri the chief, for though he was not the firstborn, yet his father, made him, chief.
text.KJV: Also Hosah, of the children of Merari, had sons; Simri the chief, (for though he was not the firstborn, yet his father made him the chief;)
text.NABRE: Hosah, a descendant of Merari, had these sons: Shimri, the chief (for though he was not the firstborn, his father made him chief),
text.DRB: And of Hosa, that is, of the sons of Merari: Semri the Prince ( for he had not a first-begotten, and therefore his father made him chief)

text.NRSV: Hilkiah the second, Tebaliah the third, Zechariah the fourth; all the sons and kindred of Hosah were thirteen.
text.Rotherham: Hilkiah the second, Tebaliah the third, Zechariah the fourth,—all the sons and brethren of Hosah, were thirteen.
text.KJV: Hilkiah the second, Tebaliah the third, Zechariah the fourth: all the sons and brethren of Hosah were thirteen.
text.NABRE: Hilkiah, the second son, Tebaliah, the third, Zechariah, the fourth. All the sons and kinsmen of Hosah were thirteen.
text.DRB: Helcias the second, Tabelias the third, Zacharias, the fourth: all these the sons, and brethren of Hosa, thirteen.

text.NRSV: These divisions of the gatekeepers, through their chief men, had duties, just as their kindred did, ministering in the house of the Lord;
text.Rotherham: To these, belonged the courses of door-keepers, to the chiefs of the strong men, belonged charges, equally with their brethren,—to be in attendance in the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: Among these were the divisions of the porters, even among the chief men, having wards one against another, to minister in the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: To these divisions of the gatekeepers, by their chief men, were assigned watches for them to minister in the house of the Lord, for each group in the same way.
text.DRB: These were divided into porters, that the Princes also of the watches, even as their brethren, might minister in the house of our Lord.

text.NRSV: and they cast lots by ancestral houses, small and great alike, for their gates.
text.Rotherham: Yea they cast lots, as the small so the great, by their ancestral house, for every several gate.
text.KJV: And they cast lots, as well the small as the great, according to the house of their fathers, for every gate.
text.NABRE: They cast lots for each gate, small and large families alike.
text.DRB: Lots therefore were cast equally, both to little, and great, by their families, for every one of the gates.

text.NRSV: The lot for the east fell to Shelemiah. Then they cast lots for his son Zechariah, a prudent counselor, and his lot came out for the north.
text.Rotherham: And the lot eastward fell to Shelemiah. And, for Zechariah his son, a discreet counsellor, they cast lots, and his lot came out, northward.
text.KJV: And the lot eastward fell to Shelemiah. Then for Zechariah his son, a wise counsellor, they cast lots; and his lot came out northward.
text.NABRE: When the lot was cast for the east side, it fell to Meshelemiah. Then they cast lots for his son Zechariah, a prudent counselor, and the north side fell to his lot.
text.DRB: The lot then of the East fell to Selemia. Moreover to Zacharias his son, a most wise man, and skillful, the North quarter chanced by lot.

text.NRSV: Obed-edom’s came out for the south, and to his sons was allotted the storehouse.
text.Rotherham: To Obed-edom, southward,—and, to his sons, the storehouse.
text.KJV: To Obed–edom southward; and to his sons the house of Asuppim.
text.NABRE: To Obed-edom fell the south side, and to his sons the storehouse.
text.DRB: But to Obededom and his sons, toward the South: in which part of the house was the Council of the ancients.

text.NRSV: For Shuppim and Hosah it came out for the west, at the gate of Shallecheth on the ascending road. Guard corresponded to guard.
text.Rotherham: To Shuppim and to Hosah, westward, near the refuse-gate, in the causeway that goeth up,—one ward as well as another.
text.KJV: To Shuppim and Hosah the lot came forth westward, with the gate Shallecheth, by the causeway of the going up, ward against ward.
text.NABRE: To Hosah fell the west side with the Shallecheth gate at the ascending highway. For each family, watches were established.
text.DRB: Sephim, and Hosa toward the West, by the gate which leadeth to the way of ascent: watch against watch.

text.NRSV: On the east there were six Levites each day, on the north four each day, on the south four each day, as well as two and two at the storehouse;
text.Rotherham: Eastward—daily, were six,—northward—daily, four, southward—daily, four, and, for the stores, two and two.
text.KJV: Eastward were six Levites, northward four a day, southward four a day, and toward Asuppim two and two.
text.NABRE: On the east, six watched each day, on the north, four each day, on the south, four each day, and at the storehouse they were two and two;
text.DRB: And toward the East six Levites: and toward the North four a day: and toward the South likewise in a day four: and where the Council was, two and two.

text.NRSV: and for the colonnade on the west there were four at the road and two at the colonnade.
text.Rotherham: For the precinct—westward, four, at the causeway, two—by the precinct.
text.KJV: At Parbar westward, four at the causeway, and two at Parbar.
text.NABRE: as for the large building on the west, there were four at the highway and two at the large building.
text.DRB: In the cells also of the porters toward the West four in the way: and two at every cell.

text.NRSV: These were the divisions of the gatekeepers among the Korahites and the sons of Merari.
text.Rotherham: These, were the courses of the door-keepers, of the sons of the Korahites, and of the sons of Merari.
text.KJV: These are the divisions of the porters among the sons of Kore, and among the sons of Merari.
text.NABRE: These were the classes of the gatekeepers, sons of Korah and Merari.
text.DRB: These be the divisions of the porters of the sons of Core, and of Merari.

text.NRSV: And of the Levites, Ahijah had charge of the treasuries of the house of God and the treasuries of the dedicated gifts.
text.Rotherham: And, the Levites, their brethren, were over the treasuries of the house of God, even to the treasuries of hallowed things.
text.KJV: And of the Levites, Ahijah was over the treasures of the house of God, and over the treasures of the dedicated things.
text.NABRE: Treasurers. Their brother Levites had oversight of the treasuries of the house of God and the treasuries of votive offerings.
text.DRB: Moreover Achias was over the treasures of the house of God, and the vessels of the holy places.

text.NRSV: The sons of Ladan, the sons of the Gershonites belonging to Ladan, the heads of ancestral houses belonging to Ladan the Gershonite: Jehieli.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Ladan—the sons of the Gershonites belonging to Ladan—the ancestral chiefs belonging to Ladan the Gershonite, were Jehieli;
text.KJV: As concerning the sons of Laadan; the sons of the Gershonite Laadan, chief fathers, even of Laadan the Gershonite, were Jehieli.
text.NABRE: Among the sons of Ladan the Gershonite, the family heads were sons of Jehiel:
text.DRB: The sons also of Ledan, the sons of Gersonni: of Ledan princes of the families, Ledan, and Gersonni, Jehieli.

text.NRSV: The sons of Jehieli, Zetham and his brother Joel, had charge of the treasuries of the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Jehieli, Zetham and Joel his brother who were over the treasuries of the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: The sons of Jehieli; Zetham, and Joel his brother, which were over the treasures of the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: the sons of Jehiel, Zetham and his brother Joel, who oversaw the treasures of the house of the Lord.
text.DRB: The sons of Jehieli: Zathan, and Joel, his brethren over the treasures of the house of our Lord,

text.NRSV: Of the Amramites, the Izharites, the Hebronites, and the Uzzielites:
text.Rotherham: Then came certain of the Amramites, of the Izharites, of the Hebronites, of the Uzzielites.
text.KJV: Of the Amramites, and the Izharites, the Hebronites, and the Uzzielites:
text.NABRE: Of the Amramites, Izharites, Hebronites, and Uzzielites,
text.DRB: to the Amramites, and Isaarites, and Hebronites, and Ozielites.

text.NRSV: Shebuel son of Gershom, son of Moses, was chief officer in charge of the treasuries.
text.Rotherham: And, Shebuel, son of Gershom, son of Moses, was chief ruler over the treasuries.
text.KJV: And Shebuel the son of Gershom, the son of Moses, was ruler of the treasures.
text.NABRE: Shubael, son of Gershom, son of Moses, was principal overseer of the treasures.
text.DRB: And Subael the son of Gersom, the son of Moyses, chief over the treasures.

text.NRSV: His kindred by Eliezer: his son Rehabiah, and his son Jeshaiah, and his son Joram, and his son Zichri, and his son Shelomoth.
text.Rotherham: And, his brethren pertaining to Eliezer, were Rehabiah his son, and Jeshaiah his son, and Joram his son, and Zichri his son, and Shelomoth his son.
text.KJV: And his brethren by Eliezer; Rehabiah his son, and Jeshaiah his son, and Joram his son, and Zichri his son, and Shelomith his son.
text.NABRE: His associate was of the line of Eliezer, whose son was Rehabiah, whose son was Jeshaiah, whose son was Joram, whose son was Zichri, whose son was Shelomith.
text.DRB: His brethren also Eliezer, whose son Rahabia, and his son Isaias, and his son Joram, his son also Zechri, and his son Selemith.

text.NRSV: This Shelomoth and his kindred were in charge of all the treasuries of the dedicated gifts that King David, and the heads of ancestral houses, and the officers of the thousands and the hundreds, and the commanders of the army, had dedicated.
text.Rotherham: The same Shelomoth and his brethren, were over all the treasuries of the hallowed things, which David the king and the ancestral chiefs, with the captains of thousands and hundreds and the captains of the army, had hallowed:
text.KJV: Which Shelomith and his brethren were over all the treasures of the dedicated things, which David the king, and the chief fathers, the captains over thousands and hundreds, and the captains of the host, had dedicated.
text.NABRE: This Shelomith and his kinsmen oversaw all the treasures of the votive offerings dedicated by King David, the heads of the families, the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and the commanders of the army;
text.DRB: Selemith himself, and his brethren over the treasures of the sanctified holy things, which David the king sanctified, and the Princes of families, and the centurions, and the captains of the host

text.NRSV: From booty won in battles they dedicated gifts for the maintenance of the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: out of the battles and out of the spoil, had they hallowed [them],—to strengthen [the provision] for the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: Out of the spoils won in battles did they dedicate to maintain the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: what came from wars and from spoils, they dedicated for the support of the house of the Lord.
text.DRB: of the wars, and of the spoils of the battles, which they had consecrated to the maintenance and furniture of the temple of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Also all that Samuel the seer, and Saul son of Kish, and Abner son of Ner, and Joab son of Zeruiah had dedicated—all dedicated gifts were in the care of Shelomoth and his kindred.
text.Rotherham: And, all that Samuel the seer, and Saul son of Kish, and Abner son of Ner, and Joab son of Zeruiah, had hallowed, whosoever had hallowed anything, it was under the direction of Shelomoth, and his brethren.
text.KJV: And all that Samuel the seer, and Saul the son of Kish, and Abner the son of Ner, and Joab the son of Zeruiah, had dedicated; and whosoever had dedicated any thing, it was under the hand of Shelomith, and of his brethren.
text.NABRE: Also, whatever Samuel the seer, Saul, son of Kish, Abner, son of Ner, Joab, son of Zeruiah, and all others had consecrated, was under the charge of Shelomith and his kinsmen.
text.DRB: And all these things did Samuel the Seer sanctify, and Saul the son of Cis, and Abner the son of Ner, and Joab the son of Sarvia: and all that had sanctified them by the hand of Selemith, and of his brethren.

text.NRSV: Of the Izharites, Chenaniah and his sons were appointed to outside duties for Israel, as officers and judges.
text.Rotherham: Of the Izharites, Chenaniah and his sons, were for the outward business over Israel,—as officers, and as judges.
text.KJV: Of the Izharites, Chenaniah and his sons were for the outward business over Israel, for officers and judges.
text.NABRE: Magistrates. Among the Izharites, Chenaniah and his sons were in charge of Israel’s civil affairs as officials and judges.
text.DRB: But the chief over the Isaarites was Chonenias, and his sons, to the works abroad over Israel, to teach and judge them.

text.NRSV: Of the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his kindred, one thousand seven hundred able men, had the oversight of Israel west of the Jordan for all the work of the Lord and for the service of the king.
text.Rotherham: Of the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his brethren, sons of valour, one thousand seven hundred, had the oversight of Israel, across the Jordan westward,—for all the business of Yahweh, and for the service of the king.
text.KJV: And of the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his brethren, men of valour, a thousand and seven hundred, were officers among them of Israel on this side Jordan westward in all the business of the Lord, and in the service of the king.
text.NABRE: Among the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his kinsmen, one thousand seven hundred men of substance, had the administration of Israel on the western side of the Jordan for all the work of the Lord and the service of the king.
text.DRB: Moreover the Hebronites Hasabias, and his brethren most able men, a thousand seven hundred were chief over Israel beyond Jordan against the West, in all the works of our Lord, & for the ministry of the king.

text.NRSV: Of the Hebronites, Jerijah was chief of the Hebronites of whatever genealogy or ancestral house. In the fortieth year of David’s reign search was made and men of great ability among them were found at Jazer in Gilead.
text.Rotherham: Of the Hebronites, Jerijah, was chief, [he was] of the Hebronites by his generations, by his fathers,—in the fortieth year of the reign of David, were they sought out, and there were found among them heroes of valour, in Jazer of Gilead;
text.KJV: Among the Hebronites was Jerijah the chief, even among the Hebronites, according to the generations of his fathers. In the fortieth year of the reign of David they were sought for, and there were found among them mighty men of valour at Jazer of Gilead.
text.NABRE: Among the Hebronites, Jerijah was their chief according to their family records. In the fortieth year of David’s reign search was made, and there were found among them warriors at Jazer of Gilead.
text.DRB: And the Prince of the Hebronites was Jeria according to their families and kindreds. In the fortieth year of king David they were numbered, and there were found most valiant men in Jazer Galaad,

text.NRSV: King David appointed him and his kindred, two thousand seven hundred able men, heads of ancestral houses, to have the oversight of the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of the Manassites, for everything pertaining to God and for the affairs of the king.
text.Rotherham: and, his brethren that were heroes of valour, were two thousand and seven hundred, ancestral chiefs,—so David the king gave them oversight, over the Reubenites and the Gadites and the half tribe of Manassites, in all the affairs of God, and the affairs of the king.
text.KJV: And his brethren, men of valour, were two thousand and seven hundred chief fathers, whom king David made rulers over the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half tribe of Manasseh, for every matter pertaining to God, and affairs of the king.
text.NABRE: His kinsmen were also men of substance, two thousand seven hundred heads of families. King David appointed them to the administration of the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh for everything pertaining to God and to the king.
text.DRB: and his brethren of stronger age, two thousand seven hundred Princes of families. And David the king made them overseers over the Rubenites and the Gaddites, and the half tribe of Manasses, for all the ministry of God, and the king.

text.NRSV: This is the list of the people of Israel, the heads of ancestral houses, the commanders of the thousands and the hundreds, and their officers who served the king in all matters of the divisions that came on duty and went off duty month by month throughout the year, each division numbering twenty-four thousand:
text.Rotherham: Now, the sons of Israel—as to the number of them, the ancestral chiefs—and rulers of thousands and hundreds, and their officers who waited upon the king as to any matter of the courses, who came in and went out month by month, for all the months of the year, in each course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: Now the children of Israel after their number, to wit, the chief fathers and captains of thousands and hundreds, and their officers that served the king in any matter of the courses, which came in and went out month by month throughout all the months of the year, of every course were twenty and four thousand.
text.NABRE: Chapter 27 Army Commanders. This is the list of the Israelite family heads, commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and other officers who served the king in all that pertained to the divisions, of twenty-four thousand men each, that came and went month by month throughout the year.
text.DRB: And the children of Israel according to their number, the Princes of families, tribunes, and centurions, and overseers, that ministered to the king according to their companies, coming in and going out every month in the year, every one was chief over four and twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: Jashobeam son of Zabdiel was in charge of the first division, for the first month; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: Over the first course, for the first month, was Jashobeam, son of Zabdiel,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: Over the first course for the first month was Jashobeam the son of Zabdiel: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
text.NABRE: Over the first division for the first month was Ishbaal, son of Zabdiel, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men;
text.DRB: Over the first company the first month Jesboam was chief the son of Zabdiel, and under him four & twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: He was a descendant of Perez, and was chief of all the commanders of the army for the first month.
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Perez, was the chief for all the rulers of the hosts, for the first month.
text.KJV: Of the children of Perez was the chief of all the captains of the host for the first month.
text.NABRE: a descendant of Perez, he was chief over all the commanders of the army for the first month.
text.DRB: Of the sons of Phares, the Prince of all the Princes in the host the first month.

text.NRSV: Dodai the Ahohite was in charge of the division of the second month; Mikloth was the chief officer of his division. In his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: And, over the course for the second month, was Dodai an Ahohite, and, of his course, was Mikloth also a chief ruler,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: And over the course of the second month was Dodai an Ahohite, and of his course was Mikloth also the ruler: in his course likewise were twenty and four thousand.
text.NABRE: Over the division of the second month was Eleazar, son of Dodo, from Ahoh, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.
text.DRB: The company of the second month had Dudia an Ahohite, & after him another named Macelloth, which governed part of four and twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: The third commander, for the third month, was Benaiah son of the chief priest Jehoiada; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The ruler of the third host, for the third month, was Benaiah, son of Jehoiada the priest—a chief,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: The third captain of the host for the third month was Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, a chief priest: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
text.NABRE: The third army commander, for the third month, was Benaiah, son of Jehoiada the chief priest, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.
text.DRB: Captain also of the third company in the third month, was Banaias the son of Joiada the Priest: and in his division four and twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: This is the Benaiah who was a mighty warrior among the Thirty and in command of the Thirty; his son Ammizabad was in charge of his division.
text.Rotherham: The same Benaiah, was a hero of thirty, and over the thirty,—and, over his course, was Ammizabad his son.
text.KJV: This is that Benaiah, who was mighty among the thirty, and above the thirty: and in his course was Ammizabad his son.
text.NABRE: This Benaiah was a warrior among the Thirty and over the Thirty. His son Ammizabad was over his division.
text.DRB: The same is Banaias the most valiant among thirty, and above the thirty, and over his company Amizabad his son was chief.

text.NRSV: Asahel brother of Joab was fourth, for the fourth month, and his son Zebadiah after him; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The fourth, for the fourth month, was Asahel, brother of Joab, and Zebadiah his son, after him,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: The fourth captain for the fourth month was Asahel the brother of Joab, and Zebadiah his son after him: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
text.NABRE: Fourth, for the fourth month, was Asahel, brother of Joab, and after him his son Zebadiah, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.
text.DRB: The fourth, in the fourth month, Asahel the brother of Joab, and Zabadias his son after him: and in his company four and twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: The fifth commander, for the fifth month, was Shamhuth the Izrahite; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: Of the fifth, for the fifth month, the ruler, was Shamhuth the Izrahite,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: The fifth captain for the fifth month was Shamhuth the Izrahite: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
text.NABRE: Fifth, for the fifth month, was the commander Shamhuth, a descendant of Zerah, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.
text.DRB: The fifth, in the fifth month, Prince Samaoth, a Jezerite: and his company four and twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: Sixth, for the sixth month, was Ira son of Ikkesh of Tekoa; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The sixth, for the sixth month, was Ira son of Ikkesh, the Tekoite,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: The sixth captain for the sixth month was Ira the son of Ikkesh the Tekoite: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
text.NABRE: Sixth, for the sixth month, was Ira, son of Ikkesh, from Tekoa, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.
text.DRB: The sixth, in the sixth month, Hira the son of Acces a Thecuite: and in his company four and twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: Seventh, for the seventh month, was Helez the Pelonite, of the Ephraimites; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The seventh, for the seventh month, was Helez the Pelonite, of the sons of Ephraim,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: The seventh captain for the seventh month was Helez the Pelonite, of the children of Ephraim: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
text.NABRE: Seventh, for the seventh month, was Hellez, from Beth-pelet, of the Ephraimites, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.
text.DRB: The seventh, in the seventh month, Helles a Phallonite of the children of Ephraim: and in his company four and twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: Eighth, for the eighth month, was Sibbecai the Hushathite, of the Zerahites; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The eighth, for the eighth month, was Sibbecai the Hushathite, of the Zerahites,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: The eighth captain for the eighth month was Sibbecai the Hushathite, of the Zarhites: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
text.NABRE: Eighth, for the eighth month, was Sibbecai the Hushathite, a descendant of Zerah, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.
text.DRB: The eight, in the eighth month, Sobochai an Husathite of the stock of Sarahi: and in his company four and twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: Ninth, for the ninth month, was Abiezer of Anathoth, a Benjaminite; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The ninth, for the ninth month, was Abiezer the Anathothite, of the Benjamites,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: The ninth captain for the ninth month was Abiezer the Anetothite, of the Benjamites: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
text.NABRE: Ninth, for the ninth month, was Abiezer from Anathoth, of Benjamin, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.
text.DRB: The ninth, in the ninth month, Abiezer an Anathothite of the children of Jemini: and in his company four and twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: Tenth, for the tenth month, was Maharai of Netophah, of the Zerahites; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The tenth, for the tenth month, was Maharai the Netophathite, of the Zerahites,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: The tenth captain for the tenth month was Maharai the Netophathite, of the Zarhites: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
text.NABRE: Tenth, for the tenth month, was Maharai from Netophah, a descendant of Zerah, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.
text.DRB: The tenth, in the tenth month, Marai, & he a Netophathite of the stock of Zarahi: & in his company four and twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: Eleventh, for the eleventh month, was Benaiah of Pirathon, of the Ephraimites; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The eleventh, for the eleventh month, was Benaiah the Pirathonite, of the sons of Ephraim,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: The eleventh captain for the eleventh month was Benaiah the Pirathonite, of the children of Ephraim: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
text.NABRE: Eleventh, for the eleventh month, was Benaiah the Pirathonite, of the Ephraimites, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.
text.DRB: The eleventh in the eleventh month, Banaias, a Pharathonite of the children of Ephraim: & in his company four & twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: Twelfth, for the twelfth month, was Heldai the Netophathite, of Othniel; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The twelfth, for the twelfth month, was Heldai the Netophathite, of Othniel,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: The twelfth captain for the twelfth month was Heldai the Netophathite, of Othniel: and in his course were twenty and four thousand.
text.NABRE: Twelfth, for the twelfth month, was Heldai the Netophathite, of the family of Othniel, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.
text.DRB: The twelfth, in the twelfth month, Holdai a Netophathite, of the stock of Gothoniel: and in his company four and twenty thousand.

text.NRSV: Over the tribes of Israel, for the Reubenites, Eliezer son of Zichri was chief officer; for the Simeonites, Shephatiah son of Maacah;
text.Rotherham: Furthermore, over the tribes of Israel, the chief ruler of the Reubenites, was Eliezer, son of Zichri. Of the Simeonites, Shephatiah, son of Maacah.
text.KJV: Furthermore over the tribes of Israel: the ruler of the Reubenites was Eliezer the son of Zichri: of the Simeonites, Shephatiah the son of Maachah:
text.NABRE: Tribal Leaders. Over the tribes of Israel, for the Reubenites the leader was Eliezer, son of Zichri; for the Simeonites, Shephatiah, son of Maacah;
text.DRB: Furthermore there were chief over the tribes of Israel, over the Rubenites, Duke Eliezer the son of Zechri: over the Simeonites, Duke Saphatias the son of Maacha:

text.NRSV: for Levi, Hashabiah son of Kemuel; for Aaron, Zadok;
text.Rotherham: Of Levi, Hashabiah, son of Kemuel. Of Aaron, Zadok.
text.KJV: Of the Levites, Hashabiah the son of Kemuel: of the Aaronites, Zadok:
text.NABRE: for Levi, Hashabiah, son of Kemuel; for Aaron, Zadok;
text.DRB: over the Levites, Hasabias the son of Camuel: over the Aaronites, Sadoc:

text.NRSV: for Judah, Elihu, one of David’s brothers; for Issachar, Omri son of Michael;
text.Rotherham: Of Judah, Elihu, one of the brethren of David. Of Issachar, Omri, son of Michael.
text.KJV: Of Judah, Elihu, one of the brethren of David: of Issachar, Omri the son of Michael:
text.NABRE: for Judah, Eliab, one of David’s brothers; for Issachar, Omri, son of Michael;
text.DRB: over Juda, Eliu the brother of David: over Issachar, Amri the son of Michael:

text.NRSV: for Zebulun, Ishmaiah son of Obadiah; for Naphtali, Jerimoth son of Azriel;
text.Rotherham: Of Zebulun, Ishmaiah, son of Obadiah. Of Naphtali, Jeremoth, son of Azriel.
text.KJV: Of Zebulun, Ishmaiah the son of Obadiah: of Naphtali, Jerimoth the son of Azriel:
text.NABRE: for Zebulun, Ishmaiah, son of Obadiah; for Naphtali, Jeremoth, son of Azriel;
text.DRB: over the Zabulonites, Jesmaias the son of Abdias: over the Nephthalites, Jerimoth the son of Ozriel:

text.NRSV: for the Ephraimites, Hoshea son of Azaziah; for the half-tribe of Manasseh, Joel son of Pedaiah;
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Ephraim, Hoshea son of Azaziah. Of the half tribe of Manasseh, Joel, son of Pedaiah.
text.KJV: Of the children of Ephraim, Hoshea the son of Azaziah: of the half tribe of Manasseh, Joel the son of Pedaiah:
text.NABRE: for the Ephraimites, Hoshea, son of Azaziah; for the half-tribe of Manasseh, Joel, son of Pedaiah;
text.DRB: over the children of Ephraim, Osee the son of Ozaziu: over the half tribe of Manasses, Joel the son of Phadaia:

text.NRSV: for the half-tribe of Manasseh in Gilead, Iddo son of Zechariah; for Benjamin, Jaasiel son of Abner;
text.Rotherham: Of the half of Manasseh, in Gilead, Iddo, son of Zechariah. Of Benjamin, Jaasiel, son of Abner.
text.KJV: Of the half tribe of Manasseh in Gilead, Iddo the son of Zechariah: of Benjamin, Jaasiel the son of Abner:
text.NABRE: for the half-tribe of Manasseh in Gilead, Iddo, son of Zechariah; for Benjamin, Jaasiel, son of Abner;
text.DRB: and over the half tribe of Manasses in Galaad, Jaddo the son of Zacharias: and over Benjamin, Jasiel the son of Abner.

text.NRSV: for Dan, Azarel son of Jeroham. These were the leaders of the tribes of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Of Dan, Azarel, son of Jeroham. These, were the rulers of the tribes of Israel.
text.KJV: Of Dan, Azareel the son of Jeroham. These were the princes of the tribes of Israel.
text.NABRE: for Dan, Azarel, son of Jeroham. These were the commanders of the tribes of Israel.
text.DRB: But over Dan, Ezrihel the son of Jeroham: these be the Princes of the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: David did not count those below twenty years of age, for the Lord had promised to make Israel as numerous as the stars of heaven.
text.Rotherham: But David took not the number of them, from twenty years old, and under,—because Yahweh had said, he would multiply Israel like the stars of the heavens.
text.KJV: But David took not the number of them from twenty years old and under: because the Lord had said he would increase Israel like to the stars of the heavens.
text.NABRE: David did not count those who were twenty years of age or younger, for the Lord had promised to multiply Israel like the stars of the heavens.
text.DRB: And David would not number them from twenty years downward: because our Lord had said that he would multiply Israel as the stars of heaven.

text.NRSV: Joab son of Zeruiah began to count them, but did not finish; yet wrath came upon Israel for this, and the number was not entered into the account of the Annals of King David.
text.Rotherham: Joab son of Zeruiah, began to number, but finished not, when there arose, on this account, indignation against Israel,—neither did the number come up into the account of the chronicles of King David.
text.KJV: Joab the son of Zeruiah began to number, but he finished not, because there fell wrath for it against Israel; neither was the number put in the account of the chronicles of king David.
text.NABRE: Joab, son of Zeruiah, began to take the census, but he did not complete it, for because of it wrath fell upon Israel. Therefore the number was not recorded in the book of chronicles of King David.
text.DRB: Joab the son of Sarvia began to number, and finished not: because upon this there fell wrath upon Israel: and therefore the number of them that were numbered, was not registered in the chronicles of king David.

text.NRSV: Over the king’s treasuries was Azmaveth son of Adiel. Over the treasuries in the country, in the cities, in the villages, and in the towers, was Jonathan son of Uzziah.
text.Rotherham: And, over the treasuries of the king, was Azmaveth, son of Adiel. And, over the treasuries in the fields, in the cities, in the villages and in the castles, was Jonathan, son of Uzziah;
text.KJV: And over the king’s treasures was Azmaveth the son of Adiel: and over the storehouses in the fields, in the cities, and in the villages, and in the castles, was Jehonathan the son of Uzziah:
text.NABRE: Overseers. Over the treasuries of the king was Azmaveth, the son of Adiel. Over the treasuries in the country, the cities, the villages, and the towers was Jonathan, son of Uzziah.
text.DRB: And over the king's treasures was Azmoth the son of Adiel, and over those treasures, which were in the cities, and in the towns, and in the towers, Jonathan the son of Ozias was president.

text.NRSV: Over those who did the work of the field, tilling the soil, was Ezri son of Chelub.
text.Rotherham: And, over the workers of the field, for the tillage of the ground, was Ezri, son of Chelub.
text.KJV: And over them that did the work of the field for tillage of the ground was Ezri the son of Chelub:
text.NABRE: Over the farm workers who tilled the soil was Ezri, son of Chelub.
text.DRB: And over husbandry, and the husbandmen, which tilled the ground, Ezri the son of Chelub was overseer:

text.NRSV: Over the vineyards was Shimei the Ramathite. Over the produce of the vineyards for the wine cellars was Zabdi the Shiphmite.
text.Rotherham: And, over the vineyards, was Shimei, the Ramathite. And, over that which was in the vineyards, for the treasuries of wine, was Zabdi, the Shiphmite.
text.KJV: And over the vineyards was Shimei the Ramathite: over the increase of the vineyards for the wine cellars was Zabdi the Shiphmite:
text.NABRE: Over the vineyards was Shimei from Ramah, and over their produce for the wine cellars was Zabdi the Shiphmite.
text.DRB: and over the dressers of vineyards, Semeias a Romathite: and over the wine cellars, Zabdias an Aphonite.

text.NRSV: Over the olive and sycamore trees in the Shephelah was Baal-hanan the Gederite. Over the stores of oil was Joash.
text.Rotherham: And, over the olive-trees and the sycamore-trees that were in the lowland, was Baal-hanan the Gederite. And, over the treasuries of oil, was Joash.
text.KJV: And over the olive trees and the sycomore trees that were in the low plains was Baal–hanan the Gederite: and over the cellars of oil was Joash:
text.NABRE: Over the olive trees and sycamores of the Shephelah was Baalhanan the Gederite, and over the stores of oil was Joash.
text.DRB: For over the Olivets and the fig-groves, which were in the champion, was Balanam a Gederite: and over the oil cellars, Joas.

text.NRSV: Over the herds that pastured in Sharon was Shitrai the Sharonite. Over the herds in the valleys was Shaphat son of Adlai.
text.Rotherham: And, over the herds that pastured in Sharon, was Shitrai, the Sharonite. And, over the herds in the vales, Shaphat, son of Adlai.
text.KJV: And over the herds that fed in Sharon was Shitrai the Sharonite: and over the herds that were in the valleys was Shaphat the son of Adlai:
text.NABRE: Over the cattle that grazed in Sharon was Shitrai the Sharonite, and over the cattle in the valleys was Shaphat, the son of Adlai;
text.DRB: Furthermore over the herds that fed in Saron, the overseer was Setrai a Saronite: and over the oxen in the valleys, Saphat the son of Adli:

text.NRSV: Over the camels was Obil the Ishmaelite. Over the donkeys was Jehdeiah the Meronothite.
text.Rotherham: And, over the camels, was Obil, the Ishmaelite. And, over the asses, was Jehdeyahu the Meronothite.
text.KJV: Over the camels also was Obil the Ishmaelite: and over the asses was Jehdeiah the Meronothite:
text.NABRE: over the camels was Obil the Ishmaelite; over the donkeys was Jehdeiah the Meronothite;
text.DRB: but over the camels, Ubil an Ismaelite: and over the asses, Jadias a Meronathite:

text.NRSV: Over the flocks was Jaziz the Hagrite. All these were stewards of King David’s property.
text.Rotherham: And, over the flocks, was Jaziz, the Hagrite. All these, were rulers over the possessions that belonged to King David.
text.KJV: And over the flocks was Jaziz the Hagerite. All these were the rulers of the substance which was king David’s.
text.NABRE: and over the flocks was Jaziz the Hagrite. All these were the overseers of King David’s possessions.
text.DRB: over the sheep also Jaziz an Agarene. All these, Princes of the substance of king David.

text.NRSV: Jonathan, David’s uncle, was a counselor, being a man of understanding and a scribe; Jehiel son of Hachmoni attended the king’s sons.
text.Rotherham: And, Jonathan the relative of David, was a counsellor, a man of understanding and a scribe, was he. And, Jehiel son of Hachmoni, was with the sons of the king.
text.KJV: Also Jonathan David’s uncle was a counsellor, a wise man, and a scribe: and Jehiel the son of Hachmoni was with the king’s sons:
text.NABRE: David’s Court. Jonathan, David’s uncle and a man of intelligence, was counselor and scribe; he and Jehiel, the son of Hachmoni, attended the king’s sons.
text.DRB: And Jonathan David's uncle, a counsellor, a man wise and learned: He and Jahiel the son of Hachamoni were with the king's sons.

text.NRSV: Ahithophel was the king’s counselor, and Hushai the Archite was the king’s friend.
text.Rotherham: And, Ahitophel, was counsellor to the king. And, Hushai the Archite, was the companion of the king.
text.KJV: And Ahithophel was the king’s counsellor: and Hushai the Archite was the king’s companion:
text.NABRE: Ahithophel was also the king’s counselor, and Hushai the Archite was the king’s friend.
text.DRB: Achitophel also the king's counsellor, and Chusai an Arachite the king's friend.

text.NRSV: Ahithophel was succeeded by Jehoiada son of Benaiah, and Abiathar. Joab was commander of the king’s army.
text.Rotherham: And, after Ahitophel, was Jehoiada son of Benaiah—and Abiathar. And, the captain of the king’s army, was Joab.
text.KJV: And after Ahithophel was Jehoiada the son of Benaiah, and Abiathar: and the general of the king’s army was Joab.
text.NABRE: After Ahithophel came Jehoiada, the son of Benaiah, and Abiathar. The commander of the king’s army was Joab.
text.DRB: After Achitophel was Joiada the son of Banaias, and Abiathar. And the Prince of the king's army was Joab.

text.NRSV: David assembled at Jerusalem all the officials of Israel, the officials of the tribes, the officers of the divisions that served the king, the commanders of the thousands, the commanders of the hundreds, the stewards of all the property and cattle of the king and his sons, together with the palace officials, the mighty warriors, and all the warriors.
text.Rotherham: And David called together all the rulers of Israel, the rulers of tribes and the rulers of the courses who waited upon the king,—and the rulers of thousands and the rulers of hundreds, and the rulers over all the possessions and substance that belonged to the king and to his sons, with the courtiers and the heroes, even every hero of valour, unto Jerusalem.
text.KJV: And David assembled all the princes of Israel, the princes of the tribes, and the captains of the companies that ministered to the king by course, and the captains over the thousands, and captains over the hundreds, and the stewards over all the substance and possession of the king, and of his sons, with the officers, and with the mighty men, and with all the valiant men, unto Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: Chapter 28 The Assembly at Jerusalem. David assembled at Jerusalem all the commanders of Israel, the tribal commanders, the commanders of the divisions who were in the service of the king, the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, those in command of all the king’s estates and possessions, and his sons, together with the courtiers, the warriors, and every person of substance.
text.DRB: David therefore called together all the Princes of Israel, the Dukes of the tribes, and the heads of the companies, which ministered to the king: the tribunes also and the centurions, and them that were chief over the substance and possessions of the king, and his sons with the eunuchs, and the mighty, and all the most valiant in the host of Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Then King David rose to his feet and said: “Hear me, my brothers and my people. I had it in my heart to build a house of rest for the ark of the covenant of the Lord, for the footstool of our God; and I made preparations for building.
text.Rotherham: Then David the king rose up on his feet, and said, Hear me, my brethren and my people,—As for me, it was near my heart, to build a house of rest for the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, and for the footstool of our God, and I made ready to build.
text.KJV: Then David the king stood up upon his feet, and said, Hear me, my brethren, and my people: As for me, I had in mine heart to build an house of rest for the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and for the footstool of our God, and had made ready for the building:
text.NABRE: King David rose to his feet and said: “Hear me, my kinsmen and my people. It was my purpose to build a house of repose myself for the ark of the covenant of the Lord, the footstool for the feet of our God; and I was preparing to build it.
text.DRB: And when the king had risen up, and stood, he said: Hear me my brethren, and my people: I meant to have built a house, wherein the Ark of our Lord might rest, and the foot stool of the feet of our God: and to build it I prepared all things.

text.NRSV: But God said to me, ‘You shall not build a house for my name, for you are a warrior and have shed blood.’
text.Rotherham: But, God, said to me, Thou shalt not build a house for my Name,—for, a man of war, thou art, and, blood, hast thou shed.
text.KJV: But God said unto me, Thou shalt not build an house for my name, because thou hast been a man of war, and hast shed blood.
text.NABRE: But God said to me, You may not build a house for my name, for you are a man who waged wars and shed blood.
text.DRB: But God said to me: Thou shalt not build a house to my name: because thou art a man of war, and hast shed blood.

text.NRSV: Yet the Lord God of Israel chose me from all my ancestral house to be king over Israel forever; for he chose Judah as leader, and in the house of Judah my father’s house, and among my father’s sons he took delight in making me king over all Israel.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit Yahweh, God of Israel, made choice of me, out of all of the house of my father, to become king over Israel unto times age-abiding, for, of Judah, had he made choice to be chief ruler, and, in the house of Judah, the house of my father,—and, among the sons of my father, with me, was he well-pleased, to make me king over all Israel;
text.KJV: Howbeit the Lord God of Israel chose me before all the house of my father to be king over Israel for ever: for he hath chosen Judah to be the ruler; and of the house of Judah, the house of my father; and among the sons of my father he liked me to make me king over all Israel:
text.NABRE: However, the Lord, the God of Israel, chose me from all my father’s family to be king over Israel forever. For he chose Judah as leader, then one family of Judah, that of my father; and finally, among all the sons of my father, it pleased him to make me king over all Israel.
text.DRB: Howbeit our Lord the God of Israel chose me of all the house of my father, to be king over Israel for ever: for of Juda he chose the Princes: moreover of the house of Juda, my father's house: and of the sons of my father, it pleased him to choose me king over all Israel.

text.NRSV: And of all my sons (for the Lord has given me many sons) he has chosen my son Solomon to sit upon the throne of the kingdom of the Lord over Israel.
text.Rotherham: and, from among all my sons,—for, many sons, hath Yahweh given me,—he hath made choice of Solomon my son, to sit upon the throne of the kingdom of Yahweh, over Israel.
text.KJV: And of all my sons, (for the Lord hath given me many sons,) he hath chosen Solomon my son to sit upon the throne of the kingdom of the Lord over Israel.
text.NABRE: And of all my sons—for the Lord has given me many sons—he has chosen my son Solomon to sit on the throne of the Lord ’s kingship over Israel.
text.DRB: Yea and of my sons (for our Lord hath given me many sons) he hath chosen Salomon my son, to sit in the throne of the Kingdom of our Lord over Israel,

text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘It is your son Solomon who shall build my house and my courts, for I have chosen him to be a son to me, and I will be a father to him.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto am, Solomon thy son—he, shall build my house, and my courts,—for I have made choice of him that he may be my son, and that, I, may be his father.
text.KJV: And he said unto me, Solomon thy son, he shall build my house and my courts: for I have chosen him to be my son, and I will be his father.
text.NABRE: For he said to me: It is your son Solomon who shall build my house and my courts, for I have chosen him for my son, and I will be a father to him.
text.DRB: and he said to me: Salomon thy son shall build my house, and my courts: for him have I chosen to me for a son, and I will be to him for a father.

text.NRSV: I will establish his kingdom forever if he continues resolute in keeping my commandments and my ordinances, as he is today.’
text.Rotherham: So will I establish his kingdom, unto times age-abiding,—if he be strong, to do my commandments and my regulations, as at this day.
text.KJV: Moreover I will establish his kingdom for ever, if he be constant to do my commandments and my judgments, as at this day.
text.NABRE: I will establish his kingdom forever, if he perseveres in carrying out my commandments and ordinances as he does now.
text.DRB: And I will confirm his Kingdom for ever, if he shall persevere to do my precepts, and my judgments, as at this day.

text.NRSV: Now therefore in the sight of all Israel, the assembly of the Lord, and in the hearing of our God, observe and search out all the commandments of the Lord your God; that you may possess this good land, and leave it for an inheritance to your children after you forever.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, in the eyes of all Israel—the convocation of Yahweh, and in the ears of our God, Observe and seek all the commandments of Yahweh your God,—to the end that ye may possess the good land,—and suffer your sons after you to inherit it, unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: Now therefore in the sight of all Israel the congregation of the Lord, and in the audience of our God, keep and seek for all the commandments of the Lord your God: that ye may possess this good land, and leave it for an inheritance for your children after you for ever.
text.NABRE: Therefore, in the sight of all Israel, the assembly of the Lord, and in the hearing of our God: keep and carry out all the commandments of the Lord, your God, that you may continue to possess this good land and afterward leave it as an inheritance to your children forever.
text.DRB: Now then before all the assembly of Israel in the hearing of our God, keep ye, and search all the commandments of our Lord God: that you may possess the good land, & may leave it to your children after you for ever.

text.NRSV: “And you, my son Solomon, know the God of your father, and serve him with a single mind and a willing heart; for the Lord searches every mind, and understands every plan and thought. If you seek him, he will be found by you; but if you forsake him, he will abandon you forever.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, Solomon my son, Know thou the God of thy father, and serve him with a perfect heart and with a willing soul, for, all hearts, doth Yahweh search, and, every devised purpose, doth he understand,—If thou seek him, He will be found of thee, but, if thou forsake him, He will cast thee off for ever.
text.KJV: And thou, Solomon my son, know thou the God of thy father, and serve him with a perfect heart and with a willing mind: for the Lord searcheth all hearts, and understandeth all the imaginations of the thoughts: if thou seek him, he will be found of thee; but if thou forsake him, he will cast thee off for ever.
text.NABRE: “As for you, Solomon, my son, know the God of your father and serve him with a whole heart and a willing soul, for the Lord searches all hearts and understands all the mind’s thoughts. If you search for him, he will be found; but if you abandon him, he will cast you off forever.
text.DRB: And thou Salomon my son, know the God of thy father, and serve him with a perfect heart, and a voluntary mind: for our Lord searcheth all hearts, and understandeth all cogitations of minds. If thou seek him, thou shalt find: but if thou forsake him he will reject thee for ever.

text.NRSV: Take heed now, for the Lord has chosen you to build a house as the sanctuary; be strong, and act.”
text.Rotherham: See! now, that, Yahweh, hath made choice of thee, to build a house for a sanctuary—be strong and do!
text.KJV: Take heed now; for the Lord hath chosen thee to build an house for the sanctuary: be strong, and do it.
text.NABRE: See, then! The Lord has chosen you to build a house as his sanctuary. Be strong and set to work.”
text.DRB: Now therefore because our Lord hath chosen thee to build the house of the Sanctuary, take courage, and perfit it.

text.NRSV: Then David gave his son Solomon the plan of the vestibule of the temple, and of its houses, its treasuries, its upper rooms, and its inner chambers, and of the room for the mercy seat;
text.Rotherham: Then gave David unto Solomon his son, the plan of the porch, and the recesses thereof, and the treasuries thereof, and the upper rooms thereof, and the inner chambers thereof, and the recess for the propitiatory;
text.KJV: Then David gave to Solomon his son the pattern of the porch, and of the houses thereof, and of the treasuries thereof, and of the upper chambers thereof, and of the inner parlours thereof, and of the place of the mercy seat,
text.NABRE: Temple Plans Given to Solomon. Then David gave to his son Solomon the design of the portico and of the house itself, with its storerooms, its upper rooms and inner chambers, and the shrine containing the cover of the ark.
text.DRB: And David gave to Salomon his son a description of the porch, and of the temple and of the cellars, and of the upper loft, and of the chambers in the inner rooms, and of the house of the propitiation,
